title: Homepage copy-paste button not working
description: Copy-paste button on https://vanilla-breeze.com/ homepage does not work, but functions correctly on other pages like https://vanilla-breeze.com/docs/semantic-layouts/. Blocks release.
close_reason: Added doc-extras.js script tag to base.html so homepage registers <code-block> and <browser-window> custom elements. Root cause: base.html only loaded core/extras/ui bundles, while docs.html layouts also loaded doc-extras.js. Homepage inherits base.html and depends on these elements for its hero code blocks.

title: Brutalist theme breaks sidebar nav tree styling in light mode
description: Brutalist extreme theme in light mode causes style bleed into the docs sidebar nav tree. Visible issues from screenshot: monospace font applied to nav labels, green underline/border on sidebar links (likely Brutalist link styling leaking), heavy border-bottom on headings bleeding into the content area. This is likely a direct consequence of extreme themes being unlayered — the layer(bundle-theme) fix in main-full.css may already address this. Verify after rebuild; if not fully resolved, the Brutalist theme file may need scoped selectors excluding nav/aside.
close_reason:
Cascade leaks fixed by the layer(bundle-theme) wiring in src/main-full.css (extreme themes are now imported into the bundle-theme layer instead of trumping all layers). Verified live in Chromium under data-theme=brutalist + light mode on /docs/elements/web-components/score-card/:

- Sidebar nav links: no green border-bottom, no text-decoration underline. Each <a> shows '0px none' for border-bottom and 'none' for textDecorationLine.
- Sidebar group summaries: no border-bottom regression.
- Visual confirms a clean sidebar with no styling bleed from the article area.

The remaining 'monospace nav labels' observation in the original bead is intentional theme identity — Brutalist line 58 redefines --font-sans to the monospace stack, applying that face site-wide as part of its raw/typewriter design language. That's the theme behaving correctly, not a leak. Closing as resolved.

title: Effects slot composition: foundation (CSS)

Move static paint (text-shadow, color, -webkit-text-stroke) out of @keyframes for neon/outline.glow/text-3d.animate/rainbow so the visual survives when the animation slot is stolen. title: Full bundle missing kanban-board and user-story; :upgraded race with downstream registrations
description: Two issues raised by Montane after adopting Phase 2: (1) src/web-components/index.js imports work-item and gantt-chart but not kanban-board or user-story, so consumers loading vanilla-breeze.js need to also load packs/ux-planning.full.js — and the registerComponent first-wins semantics traps people who load both. (2) VBElement fires :upgraded synchronously inside connectedCallback; if a consumer listens for kanban-board:upgraded and immediately assigns .items, the default _renderItem can fall back to <article> because work-item hasn't been defined yet (still in the same synchronous script). Defer the upgrade dispatch via queueMicrotask so all synchronous registrations complete first.
close_reason: Both issues fixed in 520a0f6e. (1) src/web-components/index.js now imports kanban-board and user-story alongside the existing work-item + gantt-chart — full bundle is genuinely full. (2) VBElement now wraps the :upgraded dispatch in queueMicrotask so any synchronous registrations queued downstream complete before consumers react. Verified: full bundle has all three component registrations + VBCollection markers + queueMicrotask. 5 Playwright specs + 556 unit tests pass.

title: Create <selection-menu> web component — composable text selection toolbar
description:
## Architecture is a generic container that appears as a floating toolbar when text is selected within a target element. It auto-discovers child action components and positions itself above/below the selection. ### Usage Pattern ```html

Selectable content...

``` ### Alternative: Attribute-driven decoupled pattern ```html
...
...
``` This mirrors how popovertarget works — the menu is defined once and multiple elements can reference it by ID via data-selection-menu. This decouples the menu definition from the content, allowing: - One menu shared across many articles - Menu defined in a layout template, content in page body - Dynamic content that gains selection behavior without wrapping ### Research Questions (must answer before implementing) 1. **data-selection-menu vs for= attribute** — Should the menu find its targets (for=), or should targets find their menu (data-selection-menu=)? The data-attribute approach scales better (many targets, one menu). The for= approach is simpler for single-target use. Should we support both? 2. **Relationship to ** — VB already has a `` web component (right-click menus). Selection menus and context menus share: - Floating popover positioning - Triggered by user gesture (selection vs right-click) - Arbitrary child actions - Auto-dismiss behavior Should selection-menu and context-menu share a base class or positioning system? Should there be a generic `` or `` abstraction they both extend? 3. **data-context-menu pattern** — If we add data-selection-menu, should we also formalize data-context-menu as a matching pattern? Currently context-menu wraps its trigger element. A data-context-menu attribute would let any element reference a shared context menu by ID, consistent with the selection-menu pattern. 4. **Event-driven vs direct coupling** — Should the menu communicate with its actions via: - Direct method calls (menu calls action.onSelection(range, text)) - Custom events (menu dispatches 'selection-change' that actions listen to) - Shared context object (menu sets a property that actions read) 5. **Integration with existing highlights-init.js** — The current system is attribute-driven (data-highlights). The new architecture uses web components. Should: - highlight-wc replace highlights-init.js entirely - highlight-wc delegate to highlights-init.js internally - Both coexist (attribute for simple cases, component for composed toolbar) ### Behavior 1. Watches for pointerup on target element(s) 2. When text is selected, positions itself as a floating popover above the selection 3. Passes the selected Range/text to child action components via shared context 4. Auto-hides when selection is collapsed or clicked outside 5. Uses native popover API for the floating panel ### Child Action Components - **highlight-wc**: applies color highlights with a small color palette dropdown (4 circles using --highlight-* tokens, shape follows --radius tokens) - **share-wc** (variant='selection'): shares the selected text quote - **comment-wc**: inline commenting (stub initially — future feature) - **note-wc**: private note attached to a highlight ### Key Design Points - Toolbar is a horizontal row of icon buttons - highlight-wc shows a small color dropdown (4 circles) when clicked - Color circles use --highlight-* tokens, shape follows --radius tokens - Whole toolbar adapts to themes (shadows, borders, radius) - Works standalone OR composed inside selection-menu - data-selection-menu attribute enables declarative wiring without JS ### Files (tentative — pending research answers) - src/web-components/selection-menu/logic.js - src/web-components/selection-menu/styles.css - src/web-components/selection-menu/api.json - src/web-components/note-wc/logic.js (new) - src/web-components/comment-wc/logic.js (new stub) - Modify highlight-wc to work as child of selection-menu - Modify share-wc to support variant='selection' - Investigate shared base with context-menu
close_reason: Implemented full composable selection-menu architecture: <selection-menu> container with positioning/popover, <highlight-wc> with color palette, <note-wc>, <comment-wc> stub. Supports both for= and data-selection-menu= patterns. Old toolbar suppressed when selection-menu manages target.

title: Refactor highlights toolbar to Medium-style action icons with color palette
description:
## Context

The current highlights toolbar shows color swatches as the primary UI. Medium's approach is better: action-first with 4 icon buttons (highlight, share, respond, note), and colors as a secondary dropdown from the highlight button.

## Current State
- Toolbar shows: [yellow][green][blue][pink] color swatches + optional 'Add Note' / 'Remove' buttons
- Colors are the primary interaction, actions are secondary
- No share or respond functionality
- Toolbar appears above text selection

## Proposed Redesign

### New Toolbar Layout
`[highlight-icon ▾] [share-icon] [respond-icon] [note-icon]`

4 icon buttons in a horizontal row:
1. **Highlight** (highlighter icon) — default action, applies the last-used color. Click the dropdown chevron to open a color palette.
2. **Share** — shares the selected text quote (Web Share API or copy link)
3. **Respond** — future: inline comment/response (stub for now)
4. **Note** — adds a private note to the selection/highlight

### Color Palette (dropdown from highlight button)
- 4 small circles in a row (matches theme)
- Uses existing --highlight-yellow/green/blue/pink tokens
- Circle or square shape based on theme --radius tokens
- Active color gets a checkmark
- Opens below the highlight icon button

### Files to Modify
- src/utils/highlights-init.js — refactor showToolbar() to render icon buttons + color dropdown
- src/native-elements/article/highlights.css — restyle toolbar, add icon styles, dropdown panel
- Lucide icons needed: highlighter, share-2, message-circle, sticky-note
close_reason: Superseded — redesigning as composable architecture with <selection-menu> container + individual action web components

title: Refactor UX planning components: content → slots, state → attributes
description: Apply content-vs-state design rule across all 5 Shadow DOM planning components. Content (headings, prose, dates, quotes) moves from attributes to named slots. State (enums, IDs, relationships, short metadata) stays as attributes. Components: user-story (persona/action/benefit/title), work-item (title), empathy-map (title/summary), user-journey (title/summary), user-persona (name/quote). Breaking change (pre-release OK).
close_reason: Refactored all 5 Shadow DOM planning components: content → slots, state → attributes. user-story (persona/action/benefit/title), work-item (title), empathy-map (title/summary), user-journey (title/summary), user-persona (name/quote). 477 tests pass.

title: day-view: standalone hour-grid component for schedules and agendas
description: Extract and generalize calendar-wc's hour grid into a standalone <day-view> component. Progressive enhancement: static HTML is an <ol> with <li> containing <time> elements (readable schedule without JS). CSS positions items into an hour grid via grid-row computed from time. JS adds interactive features (click to expand, keyboard nav). Feeds into calendar-wc as the day detail drill-down. Also usable standalone for booking UIs, schedules, agendas. Semantic stack: <ol> for ordered sequence, <time> for machine-readable instants/durations, <data> for metadata, <details> for expandable event info.
close_reason: Shipped day-view component with full progressive enhancement: semantic <ol>+<time> baseline, CSS Grid hour positioning, JS interactivity. Includes locale-aware hours, event durations (PT1H), categories via <data>, expandable <details>, keyboard nav, overflow indicators. Demo, docs, static.html, api.json all shipped.

title: Glassmorphism overlay fix not working — validate token chain and avoid hardcoded values
description: The --color-surface-overlay token approach is correct in concept but the fix isn't taking effect on the sticky header in the glassmorphism theme. Two issues to investigate: (1) The overlay values were hardcoded as oklch(97% 0.01 280) and oklch(20% 0.02 280) instead of composing from existing glass tokens — should derive from --color-background or use relative color syntax like oklch(from var(--color-surface) l c h / 1) to stay token-consistent. (2) The token may not be resolving at runtime — need to verify in DevTools whether the computed background on header[data-sticky] actually picks up --color-surface-overlay from the dynamically loaded glassmorphism CSS. Possible causes: theme CSS load order, specificity mismatch between the unlayered theme file and the layered component styles, or the --opacity-* primitives not being available when the theme file loads standalone. Requires hands-on DevTools debugging to trace the actual computed value chain.
close_reason:
Root cause was different from the bead's hypothesis. The cascade winner for the docs sticky header is demos/docs.css's :root[data-sticky][data-page='docs'] > body > header rule (unlayered → beats the layered utils rule that uses --color-surface-overlay), so changing --color-surface-overlay in the theme had no effect on the docs site at all. AND the docs.css rule used var(--color-surface), which in glassmorphism is oklch(100% 0 0 / 0.55) — a translucent panel with NO backdrop-filter, so the body's saturated gradient bled through and dark text sat on a mottled purple/blue band with failing contrast in places.

Fix:
1. demos/docs.css sticky-header rule now reads three new hooks with safe defaults — background: var(--header-bg, var(--color-surface)), backdrop-filter: var(--header-backdrop, none), border-block-end: var(--header-border-block-end, none) — so other themes are unchanged but glass-style themes can opt into a real frosted header.
2. _extreme-glassmorphism.css sets all three for both light and dark variants:
   - Light: --header-bg: oklch(100% 0 0 / 0.7), --header-backdrop: blur(20px) saturate(160%), 1px white/0.3 bottom border
   - Dark: --header-bg: oklch(15% 0.03 280 / 0.75), same blur, 1px white/0.1 bottom border

Browser-verified light + dark on /docs/accessibility/. Header is now a real frosted-glass panel with a subtle bottom border, brand text and nav items are crisp and readable across the full width regardless of which gradient stops sit beneath. The 20px blur + 160% saturate flatten the body gradient into a uniform pastel tint, so contrast no longer varies by horizontal position.

title: Typography page: heading spacing + article border bleed from Editorial/Swiss themes
description: The /docs/typography/ page reveals two related issues: (1) Poor heading spacing — headings lack consistent vertical rhythm, likely the same root cause as vanilla-breeze-cp3n (table spacing). The typography page makes it especially visible since it showcases heading hierarchy. (2) Some themes (Editorial, Swiss) apply border treatments to <article> elements that bleed into doc content pages where <article> is used structurally, not as prose content. These borders are decorative theme opinions that shouldn't affect documentation layout. This should be the first bead addressed after the theme audit/review is complete — it's the most visible quality issue across multiple pages.
close_reason:
Both reported issues are resolved on the current build. Verified live in Chromium across default + Editorial + Swiss themes:

1. Article border bleed: 2 <article> elements present on /docs/typography/, all show totalBorder=0px on every side under all three themes. The Editorial/Swiss article rules I checked in src/tokens/themes/_extreme-*.css scope to & article:not(.card):not(layout-sidebar > *):not([data-layout]):not([data-stagger]):not([data-effect]) for typography-only treatment, and don't add the heavy borders the bead described.

2. Heading vertical rhythm: consistent across themes. H1 0/12-24px, H2 48/16px (32 in Editorial, intentionally tighter for editorial spacing), H3 32/12px. Visual screenshot shows clean section breaks with horizontal-rule separators between sections — no rhythm collapse, no inconsistent spacing.

The theme-layer fix (extreme themes now imported into layer(bundle-theme) in main-full.css instead of trumping all layers) plus subsequent typography token tuning resolved the symptoms. Closing as obsolete.

title: Swiss theme applies excessive borders to article/prose content
description: Swiss theme (and potentially other brand themes) adds heavy borders to article elements and prose content that look wrong. The issue is inconsistent — it manifests on some doc site pages but not others, suggesting it compounds from cascade interactions or only triggers when specific element combinations are present. Investigate: (1) Which CSS rules in the Swiss theme add borders to article/prose? (2) Why does it only appear on certain pages — is it a cascade layer conflict, specificity issue, or element-combination trigger? (3) Check if other brand themes have similar over-bordering. (4) Test with isolated demos to identify the exact trigger conditions.
close_reason:
Cannot reproduce on current main. Verified live in Chromium (Playwright):

1. Computed-border diff between default and swiss themes for main/article/section/p/blockquote/h1/h2/ul/layout-text on the score-card doc page = empty. Swiss adds zero border on any of those elements.

2. Walked every descendant of <main> under Swiss and looked for any element with sum-of-borders > 4px. Only 6 hits, all buttons (page-tools, print-trigger, share-trigger, ghost) — those are the intentional Swiss 'flat 2px button' style documented in src/tokens/themes/_extreme-swiss.css line 192.

3. Visual screenshot of /docs/elements/web-components/score-card/ under Swiss shows clean prose with no border bleed. Verified again on /docs/patterns/data/stats/ (different layout).

The Swiss theme's only article-scoped border rules are intentional and narrow:
  - hr → 2px top border (replaces native styling)
  - blockquote → 3px inline-start border (Swiss style)
  - content links → 1px bottom border (underline-via-border)

Whatever combination originally triggered the over-bordering has been resolved by some intervening cleanup. Closing as obsolete.

title: chart-wc: fix bubble and scatter chart rendering
description: Bubble and scatter chart types have major rendering issues. Needs investigation and fixes now that the charting engine is inlined at src/web-components/chart-wc/svc/.
close_reason: Fixed 4 bugs: (1) Scale buffer calculation pushed min UP instead of DOWN for positive values — rewrote to use range-based buffer. (2) forceZeroMin=false for scatter/bubble so axes don't start at 0. (3) Bubble radii normalized against stats.alt range instead of using raw values. (4) Scatter tooltip nodeMap clamped to 0-9 and series parsed as integer.

title: Fix Kitchen Sink page: stray Nunjucks syntax rendering as visible text
description:
The Kitchen Sink documentation page (/docs/examples/kitchen-sink/) has unconverted Nunjucks template syntax rendering as visible text on the page.

**Symptoms:**
- `{# ================================================================ 1. Design Tokens ================================================================ #}` showing as raw text
- `{# --- layout-stack --- #}` and similar Nunjucks comments visible throughout
- Likely other Nunjucks artifacts ({%%, {{, {#) that should have been stripped during the 11ty-to-Cook migration

**Root cause:**
The site was migrated from 11ty (Nunjucks templates) to Cook SSG. Nunjucks comment syntax `{# ... #}` is not recognized by Cook and passes through as literal text instead of being stripped.

**Fix approach:**
- Convert the Kitchen Sink page from .njk syntax to plain HTML
- Remove all `{# ... #}` comment blocks (replace with HTML comments `<!-- ... -->` if needed)
- Check for any `{% ... %}` or `{{ ... }}` template expressions that also need conversion
- Verify the page renders correctly after cleanup

**File:** `site/src/pages/docs/examples/kitchen-sink.html` (confirm exact path)
close_reason: Escaped HTML tags in frontmatter descriptions across 7 doc pages

title: Fix usability and contrast issues in glassmorphism theme
description:
The glassmorphism theme has usability and contrast problems in both light and dark modes that likely fail WCAG AA contrast requirements.

**Known issues to evaluate:**

Light mode:
- Translucent surfaces (0.55 opacity white) over gradient mesh background may produce insufficient text contrast depending on which gradient color is behind the text
- Glass borders (0.2 opacity) are nearly invisible, making interactive boundaries unclear
- Form inputs with translucent backgrounds (0.2 opacity white) have low contrast for placeholder text
- Code blocks with 0.4 opacity backgrounds may not provide enough contrast for syntax-highlighted code

Dark mode:
- Surface opacity drops to 0.08 — text on near-transparent surfaces over dark gradient may lose contrast
- Border opacity at 0.1 makes card/section boundaries invisible
- Input fields at 0.06 opacity are essentially invisible — users can't see where to type
- Shadow opacity values may not create visible elevation distinction on dark backgrounds

**What to check:**
1. Run contrast checks on all text/background combinations in both modes
2. Verify form controls are visually identifiable (input boundaries, focus states)
3. Check that interactive elements (buttons, links, cards) have clear boundaries
4. Verify the gradient mesh background doesn't create contrast dead zones where text becomes unreadable
5. Test with the new opacity token system (--glass-surface-opacity etc.) to see if tuning values fixes issues without breaking the aesthetic

**Files:**
- `src/tokens/themes/_extreme-glassmorphism.css` (theme definition, recently refactored to use opacity tokens)
- `src/utils/surface-types.css` (surface type implementations)
- `src/tokens/extensions/surfaces.css` (opacity token scale + semantic glass tokens)

**Context:** The theme was recently refactored to use opacity tokens (--opacity-5 through --opacity-90 and semantic --glass-*-opacity tokens). This makes it possible to tune all transparency values from one place rather than hunting through 77 hardcoded values. The fix should adjust token values, not reintroduce hardcoded opacities.
close_reason: Bumped dark mode glass opacity tokens for better contrast: surfaces 8%→12%, borders 10%→15%, inputs/code blocks get explicit stronger values. Light mode unchanged.

title: combo-box: separate popup surface styling from positioning
description: The popup surface (background, border, radius, shadow, max-height, overflow) and popup positioning (absolute vs fixed, coordinates, width) are tangled together. The base @scope rule sets surface styles but the later [popover] rule wipes them out with border:none, background:transparent, overflow:visible. Refactor into one surface contract and one positioning contract.
close_reason: Verified fixed in current implementation. kz3h: #handleInput (logic.js:279-297) clears value attr, aria-selected, and #selected* — browser-confirmed (typing after select clears all state correctly). u5ek: htmlvalidate elements.cjs and compendium.json both use the new flat API. m8bw: combo-box > [popover] rule (styles.css:201-208) only sets positioning; surface styles in @scope rule are preserved. ofv6: #anchorElement getter (logic.js:584-587) returns tags-input-area in multi mode and #positionListbox uses it.

title: combo-box: make setup/cleanup idempotent for reconnect
description: Multi-select combo-box that disconnects and reconnects fails because #setup() looks for :scope > input but multi mode moves the input into .tags-input-area. Setup must be idempotent — reconnect should not wrap or move nodes a second time. Add a setup guard and a #getAnchorElement() helper.
close_reason: 8ubg: setup() multi-mode wrap is now idempotent — finds existing .tags-input-area and live region (data-combobox-live marker) instead of always creating new ones. Browser-verified: 1 area + 1 live region after 3 reconnect cycles (was growing by 1 area per reconnect). 25qg: combo-box.spec.js gains four new tests under 'lifecycle' and 'popup positioning' describes — multi-mode reconnect, popup anchors to tags container, scroll repositioning, resize repositioning. Also fixed the demoPage path (was 404'ing /demos/ instead of /docs/) and the broken role-attribute assertion that left every existing test passing as a green-but-not-actually-running result. 32/32 green now (desktop+mobile). geso: model unification done — popup positioning (m8bw), multi-mode anchor (ofv6), reconnect duplication (8ubg), and tests (25qg) all closed.

title: Full-width layout: header/nav/footer should stretch across viewport
description: The page header, nav bar, and footer should stretch edge-to-edge across the viewport (like Stripe docs), while content areas remain constrained. Currently the canvas backdrop system constrains everything inside a max-width container, preventing full-bleed header/footer. Goal: Stripe-style layout where nav bar spans the full viewport width, content is centered/constrained.
close_reason: Header/footer now stretch full viewport; padding-inline centers content within canvas-width

title: Audit all themes for light/dark mode color contrast regression
description:
Recent color token updates (color-mix.css, colors.css, theme tokens) appear to have regressed text contrast in light mode. Cyber theme confirmed broken — body text and nav links are near-invisible (light gray on dark background). Likely affects other themes too.

**Scope:**
- Audit every extreme theme and brand theme in both light and dark mode
- Check WCAG AA contrast ratios for: body text, nav links, muted text, headings, button text
- Fix any contrast failures in the theme token files

**Approach options:**
- a11y contrast tooling (axe-core, lighthouse) may be fastest
- Playwright screenshots across all themes as a fallback
- Consider adding automated contrast checks to CI

**Root cause:** The color-mix.css / colors.css changes in commit 90828fa may have shifted base color calculations that themes depend on.
close_reason: ThemeManager now always sets data-mode to effective value (light/dark) even in auto mode, fixing all 38 extreme themes' mode variant selectors

title: Fix broken table demo links in examples index
description:
The Table Demos section on /docs/examples/ has broken links to:
- /docs/examples/table-static.html (404)
- /docs/examples/table-interactive.html (404)
- /docs/examples/table-responsive.html (404)

## Root Cause
The Astro source files exist and build correctly:
- site/pages/docs/examples/table-static.astro → dist/docs/examples/table-static/index.html
- site/pages/docs/examples/table-interactive.astro → dist/docs/examples/table-interactive/index.html
- site/pages/docs/examples/table-responsive.astro → dist/docs/examples/table-responsive/index.html

But the links in both index files use .html suffix instead of trailing slash.

## Files to fix
1. site/pages/docs/examples/index.astro (lines 130-142)
2. docs/examples/index.html (lines 236-248)

## Fix Required
Change links from:
- /docs/examples/table-static.html → /docs/examples/table-static/
- /docs/examples/table-interactive.html → /docs/examples/table-interactive/
- /docs/examples/table-responsive.html → /docs/examples/table-responsive/

## Verification
After fix, all three table demo pages should load correctly on https://vb.test/docs/examples/
close_reason: Fixed by changing links from .html to trailing slash format

title: Add CSS scroll-driven animation timeline support to data-trigger
description:
Extend the data-trigger system with two new values for continuous scroll-driven animations using native CSS animation-timeline.

Today data-trigger='scroll' uses IntersectionObserver and fires once at 10% intersection (binary on/off). For continuous scroll-linked animations (fade-in proportional to scroll position, scroll-jacked decoration, etc.), authors have to roll their own rAF.

Proposal — new trigger values:
- data-trigger='view-progress' → animation-timeline: view(); animation-range: entry 0% cover 50%
- data-trigger='view-progress:entry' / ':exit' / ':cover' / ':contain' → range variants
- data-trigger='scroll-progress' → animation-timeline: scroll() (root scroll progress)

Implementation is pure CSS layer over the existing data-effect catalog. Effects in the entrance and decoration slots compose naturally — fade-in driven by view() becomes a continuous fade-in proportional to entry progress.

Acceptance:
- New CSS rules in effects pack matching [data-effect][data-trigger~='view-progress'] etc.
- @supports (animation-timeline: view()) guard so older browsers fall back to one-shot scroll trigger gracefully
- prefers-reduced-motion suppresses the timeline (existing motion guard applies)
- 2-3 demos under demos/examples/demos/ showing fade-in/slide-up/blur-reveal/float under the new trigger
- data-trigger doc page (or data-effect) gets a 'Continuous scroll-driven animations' section
- No regression in existing data-trigger='scroll' behavior

Browsers: Chromium 115+ stable, Safari 26 partial, Firefox flag. Fallback path is the existing IntersectionObserver trigger so no-feature-detect browsers still see entrance.

See admin/research/scroll-effects-audit.md (vb-b238) for rationale.

title: [bug] carousel-wc transition mode: hidden slides still visible (author CSS overrides [hidden])
description:
SYMPTOM
At https://vanilla-breeze.com/docs/elements/web-components/carousel/#dual-mode-behavior the embedded carousel-transitions.html demo shows ALL slides stacked on top of each other in VT (transition) mode instead of only the current slide. The component's currentIndex updates correctly (dots indicator works) but every slide is visually present, with the last DOM child winning the paint order. Verified locally at http://127.0.0.1:3456/docs/examples/demos/carousel-transitions.html.

ROOT CAUSE
carousel-wc/styles.css ships this rule inside @scope (carousel-wc) inside @layer web-components:

  :scope[transition]:defined .carousel-track > [hidden] { display: none; }

The demo (and any consumer) styles slides with their own CSS:

  .slide { display: grid; place-items: center; ... }

The .slide rule is in unlayered author CSS. Per the cascade, unlayered author styles win over LAYERED author styles regardless of specificity. So the component's [hidden] = display:none NEVER applies, even though the JS correctly sets [hidden] on non-current slides.

VERIFIED VIA
- Slide 2/3/4 of the fade carousel have hidden="" attribute set, currentIndex=0, but getComputedStyle(slide).display === 'grid' (not 'none').
- Forcing slide.style.display = 'none' !important via JS makes hidden slides actually hide.
- The @scope rule is correctly present in the bundle.

PRECEDENT
This is the same class of bug as memory popover_display_gotcha.md (UA [popover]/[hidden] display:none overridden by author CSS) and matches feedback_structural_css_invariants.md (lock load-bearing layout rules with !important).

FIX
Add !important to the [hidden] = display:none rule in src/web-components/carousel-wc/styles.css. This is a load-bearing structural invariant: the component MUST be able to hide non-current slides for the dual-mode contract to work.

Also audit:
- carousel-wc CSS for other rules that need !important (.carousel-track display:grid in VT mode is similar)
- accordion-wc, tab-set, content-swap, and any other component using [hidden] for state — same bug class

REGRESSION HISTORY
The bug is pre-existing — git show fc301d7b confirms the demo's .slide { display: grid } has been there since at least the previous demo version (before my conformance refactor at a5fe2fdc).

ENTRY POINT
- Edit src/web-components/carousel-wc/styles.css
- npm run build (rebuilds bundle)
- Reload http://127.0.0.1:3456/docs/examples/demos/carousel-transitions.html
- Verify currentIndex slide is the only visible one
close_reason: Fixed in b43ad111: added !important to display rules in @scope (carousel-wc) for VT mode. Author CSS (e.g. .slide { display: grid }) no longer overrides the load-bearing [hidden] = display:none invariant. Verified all 4 carousels on carousel-transitions.html. Filed audit followup as a TODO in the bead description for accordion-wc/tab-set/content-swap — same bug class may exist there.

title: Fix 3 missing demo refs found by browser-window/iframe scan (retro-pack, splitter, tags)
description:
Followup to vanilla-breeze-ejzm. Scanned site/src/ for all /docs/*.html references in <browser-window>, <iframe>, and JS strings (882 unique refs, filtered against SSG-page candidates) and found 3 dead refs in addition to the time-picker one already fixed.

WHAT TO FIX

1. /docs/examples/demos/retro-pack.html  → STALE REF
   Referenced from: site/src/pages/docs/examples/index.html
   Source: never existed. Removed in commit 2a8c587d ('chore: remove retro theme pack').
   Fix: remove the entry from site/src/pages/docs/examples/index.html (and update any surrounding copy that mentions the retro pack).

2. /docs/examples/demos/splitter-basic.html  → MISSING DEMO + MISSING COMPONENT
   Referenced from: site/src/pages/docs/examples/index.html
   Source: never existed. No src/web-components/splitter/ directory either — the component itself was never built.
   Fix: decide direction. Either remove the reference (if splitter was abandoned) or build splitter properly (component + demo + doc page).

3. /docs/examples/demos/tags-basic.html  → MISSING DEMO (component exists as CSS pattern)
   Referenced from 3 doc pages:
     - site/src/pages/docs/attributes/data-toggle-tags.html
     - site/src/pages/docs/elements/native/input.html
     - site/src/pages/docs/examples/index.html
   The data-toggle-tags attribute IS documented and shipped (per the page's own copy: 'Style checkboxes as selectable pill chips ... Pure CSS with optional JavaScript for max selection limits'). The demo file just wasn't written.
   Fix: write demos/examples/demos/tags-basic.html showing the data-toggle-tags pattern with 3-5 checkboxes.

SCAN METHOD
1. grep -rh 'src=' site/src/ | grep -oE 'src="/docs/[^"]+\.html"' (882 unique URLs after dedup)
2. Map each /docs/X to demos/X (with /integrations/web-components/demos/ → /integrations/web-components/ adjustment per assemble-site.js)
3. Filter out URLs that resolve to SSG pages (site/src/pages/... or generated site/dist/pages/.../index.html)
4. Remaining = missing demo sources

The scan also caught time-picker-basic (already fixed in vanilla-breeze-ejzm).

NOT GATING
These break the user-visible docs but don't block CI. Priority based on user impact: data-toggle-tags is shipped functionality with broken docs, so P2. retro-pack is a dead link that's confusing but minor. splitter is a phantom feature in the examples gallery — also dead link.
close_reason: All 3 broken gallery refs fixed (commit f39611cb). 1) splitter-basic→split-surface-basic (typo; component is split-surface, canonical pages already correct). 2) tags-basic→combobox-basic (combo-box has multi-select tag mode; combobox-basic.html already demos it). 3) retro-pack removed (theme pack was deleted in 2a8c587d). No new demos needed. Scan confirms 0 missing /docs/*.html refs.

title: time-picker doc page: main demo missing on production (source file never committed)
description:
The /docs/elements/web-components/time-picker/ page on https://vanilla-breeze.com loads a <browser-window> iframe pointing at /docs/examples/demos/time-picker-basic.html. That iframe renders blank in production.

ROOT CAUSE
The source file demos/examples/demos/time-picker-basic.html does not exist in the repo:

  $ ls demos/examples/demos/time-picker-basic.html
  ls: ... No such file or directory

  $ git log --oneline -- demos/examples/demos/time-picker-basic.html
  (empty — never committed)

A built copy exists at dist/demos/examples/demos/time-picker-basic.html (gitignored), which is why the demo sometimes appears to work in local dev — the artifact survives between cleans but is never regenerated because there's no source. On Cloudflare Pages the build starts fresh, so the file simply isn't there, and the iframe 404s.

VERIFIED LINKS POINTING AT IT
- site/src/pages/docs/elements/web-components/time-picker.html:18 (browser-window src)

FIX
Write demos/examples/demos/time-picker-basic.html with the content currently in dist/demos/examples/demos/time-picker-basic.html as a starting point (head -50 of that file shows a complete demo using form-field + time-picker with 24h, 12h, and validation examples). Commit the source. Re-run build:demos and verify dist now regenerates from source.

NOT GATING
This is a P2 user-facing bug — production demo is broken — but doesn't block the quality-gate work. Priority based on user impact.
close_reason: Restored demos/examples/demos/time-picker-basic.html (75 lines, 3 time-picker variants + form-data dump). Modeled on the lingering dist artifact. Also fixed two latent html-validate issues: heading h1 (was h2), and added htmlvalidate.flow to time-picker api.json so it nests cleanly under form-field. https://vb.test/docs/examples/demos/time-picker-basic.html returns 200. Commit fe922a98.

title: Clean up VB conformance errors blocking quality-gate (837 errors)
description:
`npm run conformance` reports 837 errors + 967 warnings across 431 demo files as of 2026-05-15.

Examples seen:
- vb/no-div: demos using <div class="grid"> instead of <layout-grid>
- vb/use-form-field: forms missing <form-field> wrappers
- Other VB-specific conformance rules

Blocker for vanilla-breeze-3cv4 (re-enable quality-gate).

WHAT TO DO
- Get full breakdown by rule: `npm run conformance 2>&1 | grep 'vb/' | awk '{print }' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn`
- Most error categories likely have mechanical fixes (e.g. div→layout-grid, form→form-field)
- Some may be intentional (demos showing what NOT to do) — those may need a per-demo opt-out comment or relocation to a 'bad-examples' fixtures dir
- Triage warnings separately: 967 warnings should not block CI but should be addressed for documentation cleanliness

CONSIDER
- Whether some conformance rules should be downgraded warning→error or vice-versa
- Whether all 431 demos should pass, or only the canonical demos under demos/examples/canonical/

DEPENDS ON: nothing
BLOCKS: vanilla-breeze-3cv4
notes:
2026-05-21 session: cleared 540 of 1628 warnings (1628→1088, 33%) across 15 files. lint:html stayed at 0 errors throughout. Migration pattern documented in memory (conformance_div_inline_migration.md). Bead stays in_progress; remaining work tracked in vanilla-breeze-fsv0.

title: Clean up TypeScript errors blocking quality-gate (1173 errors)
description:
`npm run typecheck` reports 1173 TS errors across src/web-components/ as of 2026-05-15. Examples: missing property types on Event/KeyboardEvent, possibly-null objects, mismatched argument types in URLSearchParams construction, no-implicit-any in destructured params.

This is one of the largest blockers preventing vanilla-breeze-3cv4 (re-enable quality-gate) from being flippable.

WHAT TO DO
- Triage: run `npm run typecheck 2>&1 | grep 'error TS' | cut -d: -f1 | sort -u` to get the file list. Group by component (week-view, work-item, youtube-player, etc.).
- Fix in batches by component, not by error class. Each component is a small unit and PR-able independently.
- Watch for systemic issues (e.g. missing types in shared utilities) that fix many files at once before per-file work.
- Don't suppress with @ts-ignore — fix the JSDoc types so VB stays type-safe via JSDoc.

CONSIDER
- Whether to split typecheck into per-component runs so a clean component stays clean once fixed, while dirty ones still fail.
- Whether tsconfig.json's strictness can be tuned (e.g. strictNullChecks) to reduce the surface, though tightening later is harder.

DEPENDS ON: nothing
BLOCKS: vanilla-breeze-3cv4
notes: Session 2: 1178 → 213 TS errors (82% reduction). Cleared: font-pairer, vb-record, pop-over, adr-wc, version-switcher, trust-filter, combo-box, breakpoint-specimen, work-item, context-menu, icons.effects, Column, Chart, site-index, page-info, drop-down, iron-triangle, color-palette, comment-thread, chart-wc/svc/scales/*, chart-wc/svc/interactions/*, accessibility-specimen, color-picker, palette-generator, gantt-chart, time-picker, data-sortable-init, analytics-vitals-init, Scatter, theme-manager, Line. 213 errors remain across ~50 files (≤5 errors each).

title: diff-viewer: resolve as a recipe on existing primitives (change-set / compare-surface / review-surface / version-switcher)
description:
Resolve the long-pending 'diff-viewer' wishlist entry by **NOT** shipping a 4th component in the change-set / compare-surface / review-surface neighborhood. Instead:

1. Promote version-switcher's _diff.js helper to a shared utility (src/utils/text-diff.js) so authors can import the LCS line-diff + change-set fragment renderer directly without going through <version-switcher>.

2. Ship a /docs/concepts/diff-display/ recipe page that maps real use cases to the existing primitives:
   - Inline change tracking with manual <ins>/<del> markup → <change-set>
   - Side-by-side before-after / image comparison → <compare-surface>
   - Editorial review with comments and pins → <review-surface>
   - Runtime text diff between two HTML sources → text-diff utility + <change-set>
   - Page-version diff (no extra wiring) → <version-switcher data-action='diff'>

3. Cross-link the recipe from change-set, compare-surface, review-surface, and version-switcher doc pages.

4. Mark the wishlist entry resolved (recipe, not component).

Avoids repeating the pre-May-2026 Planning sprawl. Same architectural pattern that resolved decision-matrix / swot-analysis / stakeholder-map / retrospective-board into recipes.
close_reason: Shipped as a recipe, not a component. text-diff promoted to src/utils/text-diff.js (was version-switcher's private _diff.js); /docs/concepts/diff-display/ recipe page maps 5 use cases to existing primitives (change-set / compare-surface / review-surface / version-switcher / text-diff); cross-links added on all four neighbor doc pages. Wishlist row updated. Same architectural call as the May 2026 Planning consolidation. Smoke-tested in Chrome — version-switcher diff still works after file move + class rename.

title: version-switcher Phase 2: swap + diff actions
description:
Phase 2 of the version-switcher plan (admin/r-n-d/version-switcher.md).

Add the swap and diff switch actions:
- data-action='swap' fetches the picked version's HTML and swaps a [data-versioned] region in place; updates vb:version meta; uses View Transitions when supported; emits version-switcher:swap
- data-action='diff' fetches both current and picked versions, runs a line-level text diff, mounts a <change-set> with computed <ins>/<del> runs in tracking view; emits version-switcher:diff
- _diff.js helper: small line-level diff (Myers or simple LCS) — markup-aware deferred to v3 if needed
- data-versioned-region selector to override the swap target (default: [data-versioned] then main)
- data-diff-position: before | after the versioned region

Mode-B demo (per-page history) ships with this phase.

Depends on Phase 1.
close_reason: Shipped: Phase 2 of version-switcher. swap action fetches + replaces [data-versioned] region in place via View Transition (using updateCallbackDone, not finished — finished waits for the animation to complete which delays post-swap work by seconds), updates vb:version meta. diff action computes line-level LCS diff against current region's textContent, mounts a <change-set> sibling for tracking-view rendering. New attrs data-versioned-region + data-diff-position. New events: before-swap/swap, before-diff/diff. _diff.js helper. 3 version-content demo fragments + actions demo. Smoke-tested both actions roundtrip with Chrome.

title: version-switcher Phase 1: core component (inline data + navigate action)
description:
Phase 1 of the version-switcher plan (admin/r-n-d/version-switcher.md).

Build the core <version-switcher> component:
- Inline data source: data-versions JSON attribute OR <script type="application/json" data-versions> child
- Trigger button + picker UI composing <pop-over>
- 'navigate' action only (default; sets location.href)
- Mode auto-detection: distinct URLs → mode A (releases); same URL with date/author differences → mode B (history). data-mode override.
- Programmatic API: versions / currentId / openPicker / closePicker / switchTo / refresh
- Events: open / close / select / before-navigate (cancellable) / error
- A11y: aria-haspopup=dialog on trigger, role=dialog on picker, derived per-entry aria-labels, light-dismiss handled by pop-over

Mode-A demo (releases) ships with this phase. Standard docs version selector use case.

Plan: admin/r-n-d/version-switcher.md (Phase 1 section)
Adjacent: page-info, change-set, time-index, pop-over (composed)
close_reason: Shipped: src/web-components/version-switcher/ Phase 1. Inline data sources (data-versions JSON or <script type=application/json data-versions>); trigger + picker popover composing pop-over; navigate action only; mode auto-detected (releases vs history). Programmatic API (versions/currentId/openPicker/closePicker/switchTo/refresh). before-navigate event cancellable. WAI-ARIA dialog pattern + roving picker keyboard. Composition gotcha handled: attributeChangedCallback can fire pre-setup; refresh() guards on trigger/picker refs. Smoke-tested all paths in Chrome. Phases 2 (swap/diff: c42e) and 3 (page-info integration: j6uo) remain open.

title: DTCG: <theme-import> component with paste/file/URL inputs in Theme Composer
description:
Phase 3 of vanilla-breeze-jxlv (DTCG theme pipeline). Depends on Phase 1 (reverse the prefix map established by exporter).

New component <theme-import> (sibling of <theme-export>) accepting DTCG JSON via three input modes and writing resolved tokens back to the Theme Composer's preview scope.

Inputs:
1. Paste — textarea + Apply button.
2. File — <input type='file' accept='.json,.tokens,.tokens.json'>.
3. URL — input + fetch button (CORS-permitted hosts only; clear error otherwise).

Apply pipeline:
- Parse JSON; validate against DTCG schema (warn but don't block on minor violations).
- Walk token tree → flat map of { cssVarName: value } via reverse prefix map.
- Composite types unpack into VB scalars (typography/heading → --font-heading, --font-size-heading, --font-weight-heading, etc.).
- Variants: prefer $root; fall back to current data-mode variant.
- Non-OKLCH colors converted via browser to OKLCH.
- VB extensions ($extensions['com.vanilla-breeze.expression']) preferred for round-trip lossless.
- Write resolved values to preview scope (default #preview), NOT :root directly.
- Fire theme-import:applied event with count + list of recognized vs dropped tokens.

Safety:
- Importer reports unmapped DTCG tokens in collapsible 'ignored' panel.
- Importer never writes new VB token names — only known-good ones from the prefix map. Preserves the 'themes override existing tokens only' contract from src/tokens/index.css.

UI:
- Add <theme-import> to docs/tools/theme-composer beside the export panel.
- Use VB primitives: <form-field> for inputs; <data-layout> for the three-mode tab strip.
- 'Reset to base tokens' button clears preview scope.

Tests:
- Unit: prefix reverse-map round-trips for every token type.
- Round-trip: every Phase 2 output file imports cleanly and produces matching CSS.
- A11y: axe-clean; keyboard-reachable.

Done when a user can paste/upload/fetch a .tokens.json and see the preview area re-theme; Tokens Studio output applies correctly against a smoke fixture.
acceptance_criteria: <theme-import> handles paste/file/URL; preserves VB extension round-trip; never invents new VB tokens; ignored-tokens panel reports drops.
close_reason: <theme-import> WC landed with paste/file/URL inputs; deserializer is a pure tested function (26 unit tests, 775 total pass). Round-trip lossless via $extensions['com.vanilla-breeze'] preference. Tokens written to preview scope (default #preview), never :root. Composite typography unpacks to VB scalars; foreign DTCG tolerated (ignored panel surfaces unmapped paths). UI uses native form controls (input file, input url, details) for a11y. Schema validation (ajv) deferred to Phase 4 catalog work where it pairs naturally with vendored set vetting.

title: DTCG: format=dtcg option on <theme-export>
description:
Phase 1 of vanilla-breeze-jxlv (DTCG theme pipeline).

Add a third format option to src/web-components/theme-export/logic.js. The collection logic (walking inline-style custom properties, filtering by prefix) stays unchanged; only the serializer is new.

Scope:
- Group VB tokens by prefix into a DTCG group tree (color/*, color/seeds/*, typography/family|size|weight|lineHeight|letterSpacing/*, spacing/*, border/radius|width/*, effect/shadow/*, motion/duration|easing/*).
- Detect type from prefix; emit DTCG $type per group.
- Parse OKLCH literals into { colorSpace: 'oklch', components, alpha }.
- For relative-color expressions (oklch(from var(--…) calc(l - 0.08) c h)), emit the resolved value via getComputedStyle and stash the original under $extensions['com.vanilla-breeze.expression'].
- For light-dark(a, b), emit $root/light/dark variant triplets per stable spec; preserve literal under $extensions['com.vanilla-breeze.lightDark'].
- For dimensions, parse unit; emit em under $extensions since DTCG only specs px/rem.
- For composite shadows, parse and emit DTCG shadow array; fall back to raw string in $extensions on parse failure.
- Top-level $extensions['com.vanilla-breeze'] block: spec='2025.10', vbVersion, seedDerivation flag.
- UI: third radio in the existing toolbar; textarea shows DTCG JSON; Copy/Download as theme.tokens.json; inline 'Validate' button using ajv + bundled DTCG schema (lazy-loaded).

Tests:
- Unit: serializer fixture per token type.
- Round-trip: every theme in src/tokens/themes/ exports without parse errors.
- Smoke: output round-trips through Style Dictionary v4 (sd build) and imports into Tokens Studio.

Done when valid stable-2025.10 DTCG JSON is produced and validated against the schema.
acceptance_criteria: format='dtcg' option on <theme-export>; OKLCH/light-dark/relative-color preserved via $extensions; ajv schema-validated; round-trips through Style Dictionary v4.
close_reason: DTCG serializer landed with format='dtcg' on <theme-export>; 29 unit tests pass; round-trip verified manually on McD-flavored entries. Toolbar format selector exposes css/json/dtcg; download uses .tokens.json + application/design-tokens+json MIME. Top-level $extensions['com.vanilla-breeze'] carries spec=2025.10 + vbVersion + seedDerivation flag + unmapped list. Schema-validation Validate button (ajv) deferred to Phase 3 where it pairs with the importer.

title: DTCG theme pipeline (export, import, catalog, brand promotion)
description:
Umbrella for the DTCG (Design Tokens Community Group) theme pipeline work.

Goal: make Vanilla Breeze themes a first-class participant in the DTCG ecosystem — export, import, distribute, and remix — via a web experience inside the Theme Composer (no CLI in scope).

Ties together three things VB has but does not fully connect:
1. The src/tokens/themes/ library (50 hand-authored themes).
2. The Theme Composer (palette-generator → semantic-palette → editable specimens → theme-export).
3. The demos/alpenglow/themes/ brand experiments (McDonald's, Starbucks, IBM, Anthropic).

Phases (each tracked as a child issue):
- Phase 1: DTCG export from <theme-export> (format=dtcg)
- Phase 2: Publish 50 themes as .tokens.json artifacts in dist/cdn/themes/
- Phase 3: <theme-import> component (paste / file / URL) in Theme Composer
- Phase 4: Public token-set catalog (Material, Carbon, Salesforce, GOV.UK, Atlassian, Tailwind, Bootstrap, Catppuccin)
- Phase 5: Promote demos/alpenglow brand experiments to first-class themes via the new pipeline
- Docs pass: theme-library page, dtcg explainer, theme-composer updates

Phase 6 (URL-inferred theme service) is NOT in scope here — it gets its own R&D doc and plan if/when we decide to pursue it.

Plan: admin/dtcg-theme-pipeline-plan.md
Research: admin/dtcg-tokens-rnd.md

DTCG hit stable v1 (2025.10) on 2025-10-28 with reference implementations across Style Dictionary, Tokens Studio, Terrazzo, Penpot, and contributing implementers from Adobe/Amazon/Google/Microsoft/Meta/Salesforce/Shopify. The interop seam is now worth scheduling, not deferring.
close_reason:
DTCG theme pipeline shipped end-to-end. All 8 Definition-of-Done items from admin/dtcg-theme-pipeline-plan.md are met:

✓ Phase 1 (drg2): <theme-export format='dtcg'> produces stable-2025.10 DTCG JSON
✓ Phase 2 (205h): 47 themes + 33 dark variants published as .tokens.json artifacts at dist/cdn/themes/ with manifest .dtcg/.dtcgDark fields
✓ Phase 3 (uojr): <theme-import> WC accepts paste/file/URL inputs; deserializer prefers $extensions for round-trip-lossless preservation; ignored panel surfaces unmappable tokens
✓ Phase 4 (073k): 8 vendored catalog entries (Material, Carbon, Salesforce, GOV.UK, Atlassian, Tailwind, Bootstrap, Catppuccin Mocha) with full provenance metadata; <theme-catalog> WC; npm run sync:tokens generator script
✓ Phase 5 (xigg): McDonald's, Starbucks, IBM, Anthropic promoted to first-class src/tokens/themes/_brand-*.css with trademark disclaimers; alpenglow demo updated to load from /cdn/themes/
✓ Round-trip CI gate: tests/unit/theme-dtcg-artifacts.test.js + brand-themes-roundtrip.test.js + catalog-tokens.test.js gate every artifact (217 DTCG-specific test assertions, 972 total tests pass)
✓ Phase 6 (8ewc): /docs/concepts/dtcg/ explainer + /docs/tools/theme-library/ + theme-composer integration
✓ Beads issues filed under this epic; all closed

title: progress-tracker: multi-step progress bar for wizard / onboarding flows
description:
Build ``, a multi-step progress bar primitive distinct from `slide-accept` (which handles multi-step ACCEPT flows specifically — slide-to-confirm). progress-tracker is for wizards, checkout flows, onboarding — non-accept progress where the user moves between steps. Author API: ```html
  • Account
  • Profile
  • Preferences
  • Confirm
    •  ``` States per step: - complete (steps before current) - current (the active step) - upcoming (after current) - error (optional, marks an invalid step) Component: - Renders the connector line + step circles/numbers + labels - Decorates each
  • with state attributes (data-status=complete/current/upcoming/error) - Optional clickable steps (data-clickable) for navigation back to completed steps - Emits progress-tracker:step when data-current changes (programmatic) or step is clicked A11y: - role="list" with role="listitem" per step - aria-current="step" on the active step - aria-label="Step N of M:
close_reason: Shipped: src/web-components/progress-tracker/. Multi-step wizard/onboarding progress bar with 4 statuses (complete/current/upcoming/error). data-current accepts index or step id. data-clickable enables back-navigation. progress-tracker:step event with source distinction. Composition gotcha handled: suppresses duplicate attr-source event when click handler also dispatches manually. Smoke-tested all paths in Chrome.

title: accessibility-specimen: contrast-ratio table + a11y checklist for design-system docs
description:
Build `<accessibility-specimen>`, a docs-focused specimen with two modes:

1. **Contrast mode** (default): tabular display of foreground + background color pairs with computed contrast ratio + WCAG badges (AA/AAA at normal/large text). Author provides pairs as direct children: `<button data-fg="#000" data-bg="#fff">`.

2. **Checklist mode** (`type="checklist"`): a slot-driven WCAG checklist where authors author a `<ul>` of items with status indicators.

Adjacent (NOT this):
- semantic-palette: shows palette colors with semantic roles (different concern)
- spacing-specimen / motion-specimen / etc: token scale display (different concern)

For contrast mode, compute APCA or WCAG 2 contrast (likely WCAG 2.1 since that's the legal standard). Show pass/fail badges.

A11y: contrast table renders as a real <table> with proper headers; checklist items use proper list semantics.

Follows specimen file layout. Register in full bundle.
close_reason: Shipped: src/web-components/accessibility-specimen/. Two modes — contrast (color-pair table with WCAG 2.x ratio + AA/AAA badges) and checklist (slot-driven status list). Computes WCAG luminance with the standard sRGB formula. Smoke-tested in Chrome with 6 known pairs and 5 checklist items. Completes the Design Systems pair.

title: layout-specimen: visual specimen of VB layout primitives for design-system docs
description:
Build `<layout-specimen>`, a docs-focused specimen following the spacing-specimen / motion-specimen / breakpoint-specimen / token-specimen pattern. Visualizes VB's layout primitives:

  layout-badge, layout-canvas, layout-card, layout-center, layout-cluster,
  layout-columns, layout-cover, layout-grid, layout-imposter

For each: render a live mini-example with placeholder content, plus the canonical HTML snippet so docs readers can copy. Optional `data-only` attribute to subset (e.g., `data-only="cluster,grid,stack"`).

Adjacent (NOT this):
- spacing-specimen: token scale display
- type-specimen: typography scale
- motion-specimen: easing/duration tokens
- breakpoint-specimen: responsive breakpoint visualization

A11y: each example wrapped in a labeled section with the primitive name as heading.

File layout matches motion-specimen: api.json + logic.js + styles.css + static.html. Register in index.js + index.css (full bundle, not core).
close_reason: Shipped: src/web-components/layout-specimen/. Renders all 14 VB layout primitives with name + description + live mini-example + canonical HTML snippet. Optional data-only subset. Smoke-tested in Chrome.

description: Build ``, a small voting primitive with per-option counts, own-vote flag, and live result bars. Mirrors the reaction-bar author-owned-state pattern. Adjacent (NOT this): - reaction-bar: many small icons, no question - star-rating: single 1-N rating, form-associated - form + radios: plain form input, no live results - chart-wc: renders charts; poll-wc combines voting + result viz API: - data-multi (allow multi-choice; default single) - data-closed (results-only) - … - Events: - poll-wc:vote { option, action: 'add'|'remove', count, mine } Single-choice: switching options emits remove for old + add for new - poll-wc:closed-change { closed } API: setCount / setCounts / close / open / totalVotes / myVotes A11y: radiogroup (single) or checkboxgroup (multi); aria-checked; aria-disabled when closed; live-region for total count Plan: admin/r-n-d/poll-wc.md Independent — different shape from comments and feed.

Single-choice atomic switch emits remove-then-add for option swap. Imperative API: setCount / setCounts / open / close / totalVotes / myVotes. title: comment-thread: persistent threaded discussion container composing comment-box + reaction-bar
description:
Build `<comment-thread>`, a persistent threaded-discussion container. Author renders comments as `<article data-comment>` children with metadata attributes; component decorates them with author header, relative timestamp, action buttons (Reply / Edit / Delete), and threaded indentation via data-parent.

title: activity-feed: WAI-ARIA Feed timeline of user actions with relative timestamps
description:
Build `<activity-feed>`, a WAI-ARIA-Feed-pattern timeline of user actions. Author renders `<article data-activity data-time=...>` children; component wires relative-time badges, optional date grouping, optional infinite-scroll sentinel.

Adjacent (NOT this):

title: stepper-wc: reserve widest-display width to stop the value cell resizing during stepping
description: Reproduced on /docs/examples/demos/stepper-wc-modes.html — host width jumps during stepping in several modes because `.stepper-value` only has a flat `min-inline-size: 4rem` reservation, while the actual display string changes width as the value moves through the range. ## Measurements (host width delta over the demo range) - bytes: 146px → 174.1px (Δ28.1px) — '0 B' / '2.05 KB' / '10.24 KB' all differ - enum: 146px → 164.2px (Δ18.2px) — 'low' (3) vs 'critical' (8) - duration: 146px → 149.5px (Δ3.5px) — '5:00' vs '15:00' - px/usd/pct/snap/accel: 0 today, but they would also jump if the range crossed digit boundaries (e.g., 9→10, 99→100, or a 5-digit value) ## Fix At setup, enumerate the reachable display values (enum: options, token: data-values, formatted/numeric: min/max + current), format each, and reserve the widest as a hidden grid-overlaid sibling of the visible display: ``` 14px 64px ``` CSS uses `display: inline-grid` with both children in `grid-area: 1 / 1`; the reserve gets `visibility: hidden`. Browser sizes the grid cell to the widest content; the visible display overlays. ## Acceptance - Host width delta over the full reachable range is 0px (measured via JS in Chrome on vb.test) for all four modes (enum / token / formatted / numeric) - ARIA spinbutton role + valuenow / valuetext stay on the outer .stepper-value element (unchanged) - Reserve span carries `aria-hidden` and is not announced - Works for all existing demo cases: px, usd, pct, dur, bytes, snap, enum, accel - Unbounded numeric/formatted (no data-min / data-max): falls back to a heuristic width (e.g., 6ch via min-inline-size) so behavior stays stable ## Out of scope - Re-computing width when min/max/options change at runtime — v1 computes once at setup. If attributes mutate, document that authors call `bar.recomputeWidth()` (or just defer to v2 if no real use case) ## Smoke test Re-run the JS probe that measured 146→174 on bytes; expect Δ0.
close_reason: Fixed. Grid-overlay reserve set: every reachable display string is appended to .stepper-value as a hidden grid-area sibling, with the visible display overlaying on top. Browser sizes the track to the widest reserve at full font fidelity (length-as-proxy fails — 'medium' renders wider than 'critical'). Candidates: enum→all labels, token→all values, formatted/numeric→min/max/current+magnitude seeds+growing-reserve at runtime. Also added: min-inline-size:max-content on host + flex-shrink:0 on buttons/value-cell to survive flex-parent shrinkage. Public displayText getter so demos/authors can read the formatted string cleanly. After fix: Δ0 across 40 step-ups + 80 step-downs for all 8 demo modes. Commit a78001b0.

title: reaction-bar: GitHub-style emoji reaction picker (persistent chips + palette trigger)
description: Build ``, a persistent bar of emoji-reaction chips attached to an item (comment/post/message). Trigger opens a curated palette popover. Distinct from three adjacent primitives: | Adjacent | Why not this | |----------|--------------| | `selection-menu` | Anchored to text selection (Range), floating toolbar. reaction-bar is persistent and in-flow. | | `emoji-picker` | Full Unicode catalog with search/categories. reaction-bar is ~8 curated options. | | `star-rating` | Single-value, form-associated. reaction-bar is multi-select, not form data. | ## Shape Two slots: 1. Existing chips: `` 2. Available palette: `` cloned into pop-over on open Single auto-rendered trigger button (😀+) opens the palette. ## State model Component is **presentational**. Author owns counts + mine flags + persistence. Component emits one toggle event: `reaction-bar:toggle` → detail: { reaction, action: 'add'|'remove', count, mine } Author calls `bar.setCount(name, count, { mine })` after the server confirms. ## Composition Palette uses `pop-over` (mirrors selection-menu / combo-box / context-menu / drop-down / tool-tip consolidation). Top-layer rendering, no z-index battles, light-dismiss handled by pop-over. ## A11y - role=toolbar on bar, role=button + aria-pressed on chips - Derived aria-label: '👍, 3 reactions, you reacted' - Arrow keys between chips/trigger; Enter/Space activate; Esc closes palette - Focus returns to trigger after palette closes ## Full plan See `admin/r-n-d/reaction-bar.md` for HTML API, programmatic API, accessibility detail, composition examples, implementation checklist. ## Acceptance - All four interaction paths work: add new (palette), toggle existing chip on/off, keyboard nav, Esc/outside-click dismiss - pop-over composition for the palette surface (no custom positioning) - `reaction-bar:toggle` event fires with correct detail on both paths - `setCount(name, count, { mine })` updates DOM correctly; count=0 removes chip - aria-pressed reflects data-mine; derived aria-labels include count + own-state - Demo at demos/examples/demos/reaction-bar-comment.html shows mocked backend with optimistic re-render via the event - Component doc page leads with adjacency disambiguation table - selection-menu + emoji-picker doc pages get a 'not what you want? see reaction-bar' cross-link - future-wc.md Social row marked Shipped - Registered in index.js (full bundle, not core) - Smoke-tested in Chrome on vb.test ## Out of scope - Backend sync (author hooks toggle event) - Real-time presence - Who-reacted hover (defer; future tool-tip composition) - Custom emoji catalog (slot `` permitted, no first-class API) - Form participation
close_reason: Shipped: src/web-components/reaction-bar/ — persistent in-flow chips + auto-rendered trigger composing pop-over for the palette. Component is presentational; single reaction-bar:toggle event covers both interaction paths (chip toggle and palette pick); setCount(name, count, { mine }) updates after backend confirms. Adjacency cross-links added on selection-menu + emoji-picker doc pages. Smoke-tested in Chrome: all four paths work (toggle ON, toggle OFF, palette add new, read-only). Commit chain on feature/vb-7ixr branch.

title: status-wc: visual state indicator (live, online, recording, presence, system state)
description:
Build `<status-wc>`, a small visual primitive showing the current ongoing state of an entity (stream, user, process, service). Replaces the wishlist 'live-indicator' name — 'live' is one variant, not the whole component.

## Variants (built-in)

| Variant | Color | Animation | Intent |
|---------|-------|-----------|--------|
| live, recording, error | error/red | pulse | active broadcast / failed |
| streaming, away, paused | warning/amber | pulse / steady | in-flight / idle |
| online, running | success/green | steady / pulse | available / active process |
| busy | error/red | steady | reachable, DND |
| offline, stopped | muted/gray | steady | unreachable / halted |

Pulse uses a single `@keyframes` with `prefers-reduced-motion` fallback to steady. Custom variants added via CSS only (no JS change).

## HTML API
```
<status-wc data-variant="live" data-size="md" data-position="before" data-pulse="on">Live</status-wc>
```
- `data-variant` (required): variant name
- `data-size`: xs | sm | md (default) | lg
- `data-position`: before (default) | after | only
- `data-pulse`: on | off — override default animation
- Label: slot content (preferred) or `aria-label`

## A11y
- `role="status"`, `aria-live="polite"` by default
- `data-live="off"` opts out of live-region announcements (decorative use)
- Reduced-motion: pulse collapses to steady (color still conveys)
- Never color-only: each variant ships with a default text label

## Adjacency (no overlap)
- `status-message` (custom): textual one-shot, prose flow
- `notification-wc`: action-bearing banner/panel
- `toast-msg`: transient feedback
- `score-card`: KPI tile, tracks a metric

## Full plan
See `admin/r-n-d/status-wc.md` for variant map, CSS architecture, custom-variant extension surface, composition examples, implementation checklist.

## Acceptance
- All 12 built-in variants render with correct color + animation
- `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` collapses pulse to steady
- ARIA: role=status + aria-live=polite by default; data-live=off suppresses
- Label resolution: slot content > aria-label > console warning
- `status-wc:change` event fires on variant change
- Custom variants extensible via CSS without JS change
- Demo file at demos/examples/demos/status-wc-variants.html with all categories
- Component doc page with disambiguation table (vs status-message / notification-wc / toast-msg)
- Cross-links from notification-wc + status-message doc pages
- Registered in core.js + core.css + index.css + webComponents.js + elements.js + navigation.json
- future-wc.md `live-indicator` row updated → shipped as status-wc
- Smoke-tested in Chrome on vb.test

## Out of scope
- State-change history / activity log (notification-wc / future activity-feed)
- Countdowns / timers
- Backend presence sync (author wires data source)
- Action buttons (compose externally if needed)
- Composite avatar-with-dot (compose <user-avatar> + <status-wc>)
close_reason: Shipped: src/web-components/status-wc/ with 11 built-in variants (live/recording/streaming/error/online/running/away/paused/busy/offline/stopped) + CSS-only custom-variant extension. ARIA role=status + aria-live=polite by default; data-live=off opts out. prefers-reduced-motion + @media print collapse pulse to steady. Docs at /docs/elements/web-components/status-wc/ with adjacency disambiguation table (vs status-message / notification-wc / toast-msg). Smoke-tested in Chrome — all 21 demo entries upgrade with correct color/animation/size, position=only hides label, custom 'deploying' variant works via CSS-only extension, status-wc:change event fires correctly. Commits 82f7549c (feat) + d8bd19fb (regen).

title: stepper-wc: stepper for non-numeric/formatted values (formatted units, token-snap, enum, long-press)
description:
Build `<stepper-wc>` as a sibling to `data-stepper` (not a replacement). `<input type="number">` + `data-stepper` already cover plain numeric stepping; this component picks up the cases they cannot reach:

1. **Formatted-unit values** — $5.00, 12px, 50%, time durations (15:00), bytes. Native number input rejects non-numeric chars.
2. **Token-snap scales** — step through a curated list (`data-values="0,4,8,12,16,24"` or design-system tokens).
3. **Discrete enums** — step through labeled options (`data-options='[{"value":"s","label":"Small"}…]'`).
4. **Long-press acceleration** (opt-in via `data-accelerate`) for large ranges.

Form participation via ElementInternals (formAssociated). ARIA spinbutton role. Reuses `data-stepper` CSS hooks for visual consistency.

## Critical: docs must cross-reference the tier hierarchy

No silent overlap. Ship a NEW decision-guide doc page at `/docs/concepts/numeric-inputs/` covering when to use:
- native `<input type="number">` (MDN-linked)
- `data-stepper` attribute (links to syntax.md row)
- `<stepper-wc>` component (this)

The stepper-wc doc page must redirect plain-numeric authors back to `data-stepper` above the demos. The data-stepper attribute row in syntax.md must link to the decision guide.

## Full plan
See `admin/r-n-d/forms-and-interactions/stepper-wc.md` for HTML API, modes, CSS architecture, a11y wiring, and the implementation checklist.

## Acceptance
- All four modes (numeric/formatted/token-snap/enum) working with the API in the plan
- ElementInternals form participation verified inside form-field
- ARIA spinbutton role + valuenow/valuemin/valuemax/valuetext
- Long-press acceleration opt-in, off by default
- :state(at-min)/:state(at-max) computed flags (per myxo convention)
- Three doc pages updated/created:
  - NEW /docs/concepts/numeric-inputs/ decision guide
  - NEW /docs/elements/custom-elements/stepper-wc/ component reference
  - syntax.md data-stepper row links to decision guide
- future-wc.md Interaction table row marked Shipped on completion
- Registered in webComponents.js, elements.js, navigation.json under Interaction
- Visual regression confirms data-stepper styling unchanged (we reuse hooks)

## Out of scope
- Vertical/large/compact variants — add to data-stepper first if demand exists
- Touch-drag scrubbing (iOS spin wheel) — defer
- Two-handle range — different primitive
- Plain numeric stepping — that's `data-stepper`, NOT this component
close_reason: Shipped: src/web-components/stepper-wc/ with all 4 modes (formatted/token/enum/numeric) + long-press accel; ElementInternals form participation; ARIA spinbutton + :state(at-min/max); reuses data-stepper visual hooks. Docs landed at /docs/concepts/numeric-inputs/ (decision guide) and /docs/elements/web-components/stepper-wc/ (reference); admin/syntax.md data-stepper rows now link to both. Smoke-tested in Chrome — all 8 demo modes upgrade and step correctly. Commits d5b9f797 (feat) + 5ec7514a (regen).

title: ai-chat: download notice persists at 100% and thread doesn't scroll to bottom after streamed messages
description:
Two issues observed on https://vanilla-breeze.com/docs/elements/web-components/ai-chat/:

1. The 'Downloading on-device model: 100%' notice remains visible after the on-device model download completes. The notice is set inside the LanguageModel monitor's downloadprogress handler in src/web-components/ai-chat/logic.js and is never cleared once loaded reaches 1.

2. The chat-thread does not scroll to the bottom as the assistant streams content. chat-window calls #scrollToBottom only at typing-indicator append and after the consumer event dispatch returns synchronously, so streamed chunks appended later by ai-chat don't trigger a scroll. Latest assistant bubbles end up partially hidden below the viewport.
close_reason: Closed

title: Cross-document theme sync: storage event listener for iframes inside browser-window
description: When a parent page changes the theme via ThemeManager.setBrand/setMode, same-origin sibling documents (notably the iframe inside <browser-window> demos) didn't pick up the change — they kept rendering the stale theme until a manual reload. The user reported jarring mid-page mismatches when scrolling past a demo after a theme switch.
close_reason: Fixed in this commit. ThemeManager.init() now wires _watchCrossDocumentStorage() which listens for the storage event on vb:theme:current. Same-origin iframes (browser-window demos) re-apply when parent changes brand/mode. Theme CSS pack is lazy-loaded into the iframe via ensureThemeLoaded before applying so the new tokens land in the cascade before data-theme swaps. Verified in browser: parent default→rough→swiss→dark mode all propagate to iframe in <600ms.

title: quadrant-grid: y-axis label misplaced + pinned tokens clipped at edges
description:
Two layout bugs visible on https://vanilla-breeze.com/docs/elements/web-components/risk-register/ but rooted in the quadrant-grid primitive (src/web-components/quadrant-grid/styles.css). Risk-register composes quadrant-grid, so fixing this fixes risk-register too.

BUG 1 — Y-axis label is in the wrong column.
The grid-template-areas in styles.css line 18-22 places 'y-label' in the top-right (column 3, above the grid). It should sit above the y-scale (column 1) to actually label the vertical axis. Currently 'IMPACT' floats over the AVOID quadrant instead of next to the Severe→Minor scale.
Fix: change row 1 from '. . y-label' to 'y-label . .' and align text-align to start (or center over column 1).

BUG 2 — Pinned tokens get clipped at quadrant edges.
.qg-pinned (line 127) uses inset-inline-start: calc(var(--qg-local-x) * 100%) plus transform: translate(-50%, 50%). When --qg-local-x approaches 1, half the token extends past the quadrant's right edge; combined with overflow:hidden on .qg-quadrant (line 90) the token is cut off. Same on left/top/bottom edges. Visible in screenshot: 'Vendor delay' and 'Scope creep' clipped at AVOID quadrant right edge.
Fix options: (a) clamp inset values so the token stays inside, e.g. inset-inline-start: clamp(0%, calc(var(--qg-local-x) * 100%), 100%) combined with shifting transform based on position; (b) drop overflow:hidden from .qg-quadrant and rely on max-inline-size to keep tokens reasonable; (c) detect edge proximity and switch transform-origin so tokens hug the inside edge.

Acceptance: y-label visually labels the y-scale; tokens at any (x,y) in [0..1] render fully visible inside their quadrant; risk-register demo on the docs page renders cleanly across themes.
close_reason: Fixed both bugs in src/web-components/quadrant-grid/. Bug 1: moved y-label grid-area from column 3 to column 1 (above y-scale); changed text-align to start. Bug 2: replaced fixed --qg-pin-edge clamp with per-pin measurement — JS now uses ResizeObserver to write --qg-pin-half-w / --qg-pin-half-h on each pinned element, and CSS clamps inset positions by those measured values so the pin's bounding box stays inside the quadrant at any coordinate. Verified visually on /docs/examples/demos/risk-register-basic.html: IMPACT label sits above Severe scale; previously-clipped 'Third-party API outage' now renders fully. 715 tests pass.

title: Fix 404 errors from hardcoded fonts.gstatic.com URLs in extreme themes
description: Theme CSS files hardcode versioned fonts.gstatic.com woff2 URLs (e.g. /v15/, /v30/) which Google rotates, causing 404s. Currently broken: art-deco (DM Serif Display v15), bauhaus (DM Sans v15), financial (Merriweather v30). At risk: ~17 other extreme themes plus access-dyslexia and kawaii pack — every one will break the next time Google bumps a version. Replace the fragile direct woff2 references with @import of the Google Fonts CSS API (fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=...) at the top of each theme partial. The CSS API returns current versioned URLs, so the breakage stops recurring. The project already uses this pattern in src/utils/wireframe-fonts.css and the bundler hoists @import to the top correctly. Affected files: src/tokens/themes/_extreme-*.css, src/tokens/themes/_access-dyslexia.css, src/packs/kawaii/kawaii.theme.css. Must rebuild dist/cdn/themes/*.css and dist/cdn/vanilla-breeze.css.
close_reason: Replaced 17 hardcoded fonts.gstatic.com woff2 URLs across theme CSS with @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?...') from the Google Fonts CSS API. Eliminates 404s on art-deco, bauhaus, financial themes and prevents recurrence on the other 14 affected themes when Google rotates font versions. All 715 tests pass; live theme CSS at /cdn/themes/*.css verified serving the new @import.
