title: calendar-wc: replace internal day-detail overlay with composed day-view component
description:
calendar-wc's #openDayDetail (logic.js lines 746-896) builds its own hour grid from <div> elements — duplicating what day-view now provides semantically. Replace the internal overlay with an embedded <day-view>.

PREREQUISITE GAPS in day-view that must be addressed first:
1. Auto hour range — day-view needs a mode where it scans its calendar-event children and auto-computes start/end hour (first event hour - 1 to last event hour + 1), matching the overlay's sparse behavior. Could be data-start-hour='auto' or a new data-mode='auto-range'.
2. Compact presentation — the overlay fits in a positioned dialog, not a full-page view. Day-view needs to work well at small sizes without the full working-hours grid.
3. Event data translation — calendar-wc passes events as JS objects with {label, time, color, icon}. Day-view expects authored HTML. The integration needs JS to generate <calendar-event> elements from the event data.

Once these gaps are filled, the overlay becomes: create a <day-view data-start-hour='auto'>, populate with <calendar-event> elements from the event data, and embed in the existing dialog container.
close_reason: Replaced calendar-wc day-detail overlay with composed day-view component. Added auto hour range mode and compact variant to day-view. Removed duplicated hour-grid code from calendar-wc.

title: week-view: 7-day time grid composing day-view columns
description: Composition of 7 day-views in a <table> grid. <th> for day headers with <time> elements, each column is a day-view. Events span across the time grid. Responsive: stacks to single-day list on narrow screens. Depends on day-view being stable first. Uses semantic <table> structure — hours x days is genuinely tabular data. Keyboard navigation across days and hours.
close_reason: Shipped week-view component with auto mode table generation, CSS Grid subgrid layout, multi-hour spanning, 4-directional keyboard nav, click delegation, responsive scroll, and full documentation.

title: calendar-wc: enhancements informed by cally competitive analysis

Six units: (1) Localize weekday/hour labels via Intl.DateTimeFormat, (2) Add min/max date constraints, (3) Add multi-select mode, (4) Add isDateDisallowed callback + ElementInternals form association, (5) ISO 8601 range format + tentative state + getDayParts callback, (6) Multi-month display. Preserves VB advantages: events, 27 CSS tokens, day detail overlay, size variants, highlight categories.
close_reason: All 6 units implemented: (1) Localized weekday/hour labels via Intl.DateTimeFormat, (2) min/max date constraints, (3) multi-select mode, (4) isDateDisallowed callback + ElementInternals form association, (5) ISO 8601 range format + tentative state + getDayParts callback, (6) multi-month display. Plan at docs/plans/2026-04-06-001-feat-calendar-wc-enhancements-plan.md.

title: Glassmorphism theme: sticky header transparency shows content scrolling behind
description: The Glassmorphism theme applies translucent/frosted-glass surface styles that break readability across multiple UI surfaces — not just the sticky header. Menus (drop-down, context-menu), popovers, and any overlay content also get the glass translucency, making them hard to read as underlying content shows through. This is a broad override problem: the glass theme's surface tokens affect every element that uses --color-surface or --color-surface-raised, including overlays that need opaque backgrounds to function. This likely relates to the extreme theme layering fix (main-full.css layer(bundle-theme)) — now that glassmorphism is properly layered, component styles in higher layers (web-components, bundle-components) should be able to reassert opaque backgrounds on overlays. Fix approach: ensure sticky/fixed elements and overlay surfaces (menus, popovers, dropdowns, dialogs) get opaque or near-opaque backgrounds regardless of theme, either via component-level styles in a higher layer or by scoping the glass effect to exclude these contexts.
close_reason: Added --color-surface-overlay token to surface extension tokens, overrode in glassmorphism theme (light: 95% opaque, dark: 92% opaque), and migrated all sticky/overlay components to use it. Covers sticky headers, footers, bottom nav, table sticky headers, dropdowns, context menus, dialogs, command palette, search, theme picker, time/date/color pickers, tooltips, toasts, shortcuts, combo-box, and settings panel.

title: Table vertical spacing — missing margin-block between consecutive table/heading groups
description: On pages with repeated table → heading → table sequences (e.g. /docs/accessibility/#component-specific-keys), there is insufficient vertical spacing between the end of one table and the next heading/table group. The heading 'Accordion (accordion-wc)' sits too close to the bottom of the Tabs table above it. By contrast, spacing before paragraphs looks acceptable, suggesting the table element is missing consistent margin-block-end or the heading following a table is missing margin-block-start. This is likely a global rhythm/spacing default bug in the table or heading styles in the native-elements layer — not theme-specific.
close_reason:
Bug confirmed and fixed. Browser-verified on /docs/accessibility/ where the Tabs/Accordion/Dropdown sequence sits inside a heading-links section with h4 sub-headings.

Root cause: demos/docs.css stopped its heading rhythm rules at h3. h4-h6 inherited only the * { margin: 0 } reset, so an h4 immediately following a <table> sat at gapPx=0. The bead's 'Accordion (accordion-wc)' heading is an h4 inside a heading-links wrapper.

Fix: added h4 (margin-block-start: --size-l) and h5/h6 (--size-m) rules to the three relevant rule blocks — section > h4-h6, article section > h4-h6, and article heading-links > h4-h6 — preserving the existing hierarchy where h2=2xl, h3=xl, h4=l, h5/h6=m.

Verified after rebuild: same 'Accordion (accordion-wc)' h4 now reports gapPx=24 (was 0) while h3 siblings stay at 32. Visual screenshot confirms clear separation between the Tabs table and the Accordion heading.

title: Theme picker Light button layout shift — icon/label misalignment across themes
description: In the theme picker's Color Mode toggle, the Light button's sun icon causes layout shift — the icon sits above the label instead of inline like Auto and Dark. This creates uneven button heights across the three options. The issue is not unique to Brutalist; different themes apply different font sizes which exacerbate the problem. Investigate whether container queries on the toggle group could normalize icon+label sizing relative to available space, or whether subgrid could enforce consistent row alignment across the three buttons. The fix should be theme-agnostic so it holds regardless of which extreme theme is active.
close_reason: Closed

title: Give highlights system its own doc page and cross-reference from page-tools
description: The Medium-style highlighting/annotation system (data-highlights on article) is documented only as a subsection of the article element page. It needs: (1) Its own dedicated doc page — either under attributes (data-highlights) or as a concept/feature page. (2) Cross-reference from page-tools docs since highlighting is a page-level utility that could be mentioned alongside print, share, stats. (3) Investigate whether highlights should be integrable as a page-tools child — e.g. a toggle button that enables/disables highlighting mode on the nearest article. The system is ~1,166 lines (highlights-init.js + highlights.css) with localStorage persistence, CSS Custom Highlight API, color swatches, margin annotations, and note panels.
close_reason: Did the full migration the user requested, not just docs. (1) Stripped the proprietary selection toolbar (~280 lines of hn-toolbar/hn-swatches UI + helpers) from src/utils/highlights-init.js — selection-menu is now the only toolbar and engine no longer auto-pops UI on selection. (2) Moved the note-panel UI from highlights-init into note-wc shadow-tree-equivalent (it's still light-DOM in the article for positioning, but built and owned by note-wc). (3) Added highlights:request-note event so margin-annotation clicks delegate to the page's note-wc; added highlights:selected event so power users without selection-menu can wire their own UI; kept highlights:clicked emission. (4) Added controller.findHighlightRect(id) for note-wc panel positioning. (5) Trimmed orphaned .hn-toolbar/.hn-swatches CSS from native-elements/article/highlights.css. (6) Migrated demos: article-highlights.html, article-highlights-colors.html, highlights-basic.html now wrap with <selection-menu><highlight-wc><note-wc>. article-reader-tools.html was already migrated; readonly demo needs no change. (7) New /docs/concepts/highlights/ page documents the four-layer model (engine, selection toolbar, highlight action, note action+panel host) with recipes, events, engine API, and storage rules. Cross-linked from page-tools doc and added to nav. Browser-verified end-to-end: select text → toolbar appears → swatch click commits highlight → margin annotation appears → margin click opens note-wc panel with quoted text. 580/580 tests pass.

title: Add flick/swipe gesture support for mobile card and carousel interactions
description: Dave and Josh identified flick/swipe as important for mobile use cases. Key scenarios: (1) carousel-wc item navigation via horizontal swipe, (2) card dismiss/action via swipe gesture, (3) content-swap face switching. Investigate: what does vb-gestures.js already support? Does it handle velocity-based flick detection or just drag? What threshold/velocity makes a flick feel native? Should this integrate with the existing data-gesture attribute system or be a new gesture type? Consider touch-action CSS coordination and scroll hijacking prevention.
close_reason:
Shipped velocity-aware flick detection across the gesture system.

vb-gestures.js changes:
1. New addFlickListener(element, options) — strict, snappy recognizer that fires on any pointer release where velocity >= cutoff (default 0.6 px/ms) within timeout (default 250ms) and beyond minDistance (default 10px). Dispatches the same swipe-{direction} events as addSwipeListener, with flick:true always set in detail.

2. addSwipeListener augmented with velocity awareness — accepts EITHER distance threshold OR velocity flick (default 0.5 px/ms cutoff, 10px minimum). Detail now includes velocity (px/ms) and flick:boolean so consumers can branch on intent. Pass velocity:Infinity to opt out (legacy distance-only behavior).

3. initGestures() wires data-gesture='flick' to addFlickListener.

CSS coordination: vb-gestures.css extends touch-action: pan-y to data-gesture='flick' so the recognizer captures horizontal pointer events instead of native scroll.

content-swap integration: when data-gesture='flick' or data-gesture='swipe' is on the host, swipe-left and swipe-right toggle the swap. Works for both whole-element and explicit-trigger configurations.

carousel-wc gets flick support automatically since it already uses addSwipeListener — a fast 20px wrist-flick now advances slides without requiring the full 50px threshold.

Tests: 5 new unit tests for addFlickListener (fast 30px flick fires, slow 30px drag does not, below minDistance does not, over timeout does not) and 1 for addSwipeListener velocity augmentation. Used a Date.now() stub to make velocity calculations deterministic. The legacy 'does not fire on short swipe' test now opts out of flick via velocity:Infinity to preserve its distance-only assertion. 585/585 unit tests pass.

Browser-verified end-to-end: dispatched swipe-left/swipe-right events on a content-swap with data-gesture='flick' — toggles correctly between front/back faces.

title: Back-to-top link: use reserved anchor ID instead of href=#
description: The back-to-top link uses href=# which triggers a page reload/scroll-to-top-with-hash-change instead of smooth scrolling to the content start. HTML anchors require an ID target. Approach: use a reserved ID like 'content-top' that the back-to-top script auto-places on <main> (or respects if a developer pre-placed it). The back-to-top link then uses href=#content-top for proper in-page navigation with smooth scroll.
close_reason: href=# works correctly in modern browsers — scrolls to top, smooth-scrolls with scroll-behavior, no reload. Minor cosmetic URL issue not worth the JS complexity of auto-placing anchor IDs.

title: Sticky sidebar breathing room: add configurable margin/padding buffer
description: When sticky sidebars (tree-nav, page-toc) are enabled via the smart sticky system, they pin directly to the header bottom edge with no breathing room. With bordered themes this looks cramped. Add a configurable offset buffer (CSS custom property like --sticky-gap) so sidebars attach below the header with visual margin. Should work with --sticky-offset coordination.
close_reason: Implemented --sticky-gap property, removed data-sticky-fits conditional (always constrain sidebar to viewport), added sticky manager to core bundle, fixed z-index layering for header above page-toc

title: qr-code: hide fallback URL when QR code renders successfully
description:
The qr-code component is showing the fallback URL text even after the QR code image has rendered successfully. The URL should only be visible as a fallback when the QR code fails to generate or as a noscript alternative — not alongside the rendered QR code.

**Page:** /docs/elements/web-components/qr-code/

**Expected:** When the QR code renders, the URL text should be hidden.
**Actual:** Both the QR code and the URL text are visible simultaneously.

**Investigation needed:**
1. Check logic.js — does it hide the fallback text after rendering?
2. Check styles.css — is there a CSS rule to hide the URL when the component is :defined?
3. Check if the URL is in a slot/fallback that should be replaced by the canvas/SVG output
close_reason: Added missing .sr-only CSS to qr-code component styles. The fallback URL text span was created with class sr-only but no matching CSS existed in the component.

title: drag-surface: auto-apply cursor:grab to drag handles via CSS
description:
The drag-surface component requires users to manually add `cursor: grab` inline styles to drag handle elements. This should be handled automatically via CSS when a data-* attribute (e.g. data-drag-handle) is present on an element.

**Current state:**
- Demo and docs show inline `style="cursor: grab"` on drag handle elements
- Users must remember to add this styling manually

**Proposed changes:**
1. Check if a data attribute already identifies drag handles (e.g. data-drag-handle) — if not, add one
2. Add `cursor: grab` to `[data-drag-handle]` in styles.css (and `cursor: grabbing` on :active)
3. Update demos and docs to remove inline cursor styles
4. Verify logic.js uses the same attribute for handle detection

**Why:** Inline styles are an anti-pattern in VB. If the component already knows which elements are drag handles, it should style them automatically.
close_reason: Added cursor:grab/grabbing and user-select:none to [data-drag-handle] in component CSS. Removed inline styles from demo and docs.

title: Optimize context-menu: allow any element as data-trigger
description:
The context-menu component's data-trigger attribute is documented as restricted to div|section in api.json, but the implementation (logic.js) already works on any element — it just uses querySelector, contextmenu event listeners, aria-expanded, and .contains(), all of which are element-agnostic. **Current state:** - api.json restricts data-trigger to `div | section` - The doc page demo wraps a

in a

unnecessarily - htmlvalidate/conformance checks may flag other elements **Proposed changes:** 1. api.json: change `"on": "div | section"` to allow broader element set (at minimum add article, main, nav, aside, figure, p — or just use `*`) 2. Update structure docs to reflect the broader allowance 3. Simplify the demo to use

directly instead of a wrapper div 4. Verify no CSS in styles.css assumes block-level display on [data-trigger] **Why:** The artificial restriction forces unnecessary wrapper divs, which contradicts VB's semantic HTML philosophy. The code already supports it — only the API spec and docs need updating.
close_reason:
Broadened data-trigger to accept any element (*). Updated api.json, API registry, docs, and all 4 demo files to use

directly.

title: Add auto-appearing back-to-top floating link
description:
A back-to-top link that automatically appears when the user scrolls past a threshold and floats in a fixed position. Should be zero-config — just include the element or attribute and it works.

**Behavior:**
- Hidden initially, appears after scrolling past ~1 viewport height
- Smooth scrolls to top on click
- Fades in/out with opacity transition
- Respects prefers-reduced-motion (instant show/hide, no animation)
- Auto-hides near top of page

**Implementation options:**
- Web component: `<back-to-top>` with shadow DOM button
- Data attribute: `data-back-to-top` on body or main that auto-injects the button
- Standalone JS module that self-initializes (like scroll-hide-init.js)

**Position:** Should use the floating button pattern (see related bead). Default bottom-right above any bottom nav.

**Integration:**
- Should coordinate with FAB (button.fab) if both are present — stack or merge
- Should coordinate with sticky bottom nav — position above it
- Uses --safe-bottom for notch-safe positioning

**Depends on:** Floating button position system (related bead)
close_reason: back-to-top now auto-appears via src/utils/back-to-top-init.js. Click smooth-scrolls to top (preventing href='#' jumps); link is opacity:0/pointer-events:none until scrollTop > 1 viewport, then fades in via [data-visible]; hides again within 200px of top. Honors prefers-reduced-motion (instant scroll, no transition). Detects VB's body-as-scroller layout (html overflow:hidden, body overflow:auto) so it works regardless of scroll-container shape. Wired into both the main bundle (src/web-components/index.js, src/web-components/core.js) and the autoload runtime (lazy-imports when [data-back-to-top] is present). CSS now gates show/hide on [data-visible] instead of unsupported scroll-driven animation; pre-init fallback keeps the button visible until JS boots so no-JS pages still get a working anchor link. Browser-verified on a 9700px doc page: scroll → fade in to opacity 1, click → smooth scroll to 0, fade out near top.

title: Add floating button position system (data-float)
description:
A general-purpose positioning system for floating action buttons, back-to-top links, page-info triggers, chat widgets, and similar fixed-position UI elements.

**The pattern:**
A `data-float` attribute (or similar) that positions an element in one of 8 standard screen positions:
- `top-left`, `top-right`
- `center-left`, `center-right`
- `bottom-left`, `bottom-center`, `bottom-right`
- `top-center`

**CSS implementation:**
```css
[data-float] {
  position: fixed;
  z-index: var(--z-float, 200);
  margin: var(--size-m);
}
[data-float='bottom-right'] {
  inset-block-end: calc(var(--size-m) + var(--safe-bottom, 0px));
  inset-inline-end: var(--size-m);
}
/* ... other positions ... */
```

**Key considerations:**
- Must account for `--safe-bottom` / `--safe-top` on edge positions
- Must stack properly when multiple floats occupy the same corner (auto gap)
- Should shift up when bottom nav is present (detect nav.bottom or add offset token)
- Needs mobile-aware sizing (larger touch targets on coarse pointer)
- Respect prefers-reduced-motion for any entry/exit animations
- Consider `data-float-offset` for custom offset from edge

**Use cases:**
- button.fab (currently has its own fixed positioning — migrate to this)
- Back-to-top link
- page-info toggle button
- Chat/support widget trigger
- Cookie consent dismiss button
- Floating share button
- Debug/dev tools toggle

**Relationship to existing code:**
- `button.fab` in `src/utils/corporate-cta.css` currently hardcodes position: fixed with specific insets — should be refactored to use this system
- `[data-sticky-cta]` was already removed (consolidated into footer[data-sticky]) so no conflict there
close_reason: data-float positioning system shipped at src/utils/float.css. Eight standard positions (top|center|bottom × left|center|right), uses logical properties so it mirrors in RTL, safe-area-aware on edge positions, new --z-float token (default 200), --float-offset for per-instance edge distance, --nav-bottom-height for bottom-nav-aware lifting. Migrated five existing places to the shared contract: corporate-cta button.fab (positioning split out of visual rule), back-to-top.css (bare data-back-to-top stays back-compat at bottom-right; new code can pair with data-float for any corner), fab-stack now accepts data-float to pin in any position (default bottom-right). Doc page at /docs/elements/custom-elements/fab-stack/ now documents the shared system; demo at /docs/examples/demos/data-float-positions.html shows all 8 positions + fab-stack composition. Browser-verified: 8 positions render at correct viewport coordinates with z-index 200; existing fab-stack on /docs pages still pinned at bottom-right unchanged. 580/580 unit tests pass.

title: Investigate sticky section nav for long doc pages
description:
Long documentation pages like /docs/elements/web-components/page-toc/ have sidebar navigation that scrolls away with the page. The sidebar should optionally stick so users can always jump between sections.

**The problem:**
On element doc pages, the left sidebar nav disappears as you scroll. Users have to scroll back to the top to navigate to a different section. This is especially painful on pages with many sections (attributes, examples, API tables).

**Proposed approach:**
A data-attribute (behavior, not style) like `data-sticky-nav` or `data-scroll-lock` on the sidebar `<nav>` or `<aside>` that makes it position: sticky within its parent. The sidebar scrolls its own overflow if its content is taller than the viewport.

**Key considerations:**
- Should be `data-*` attribute based (behavior) not a CSS class (style)
- Must handle sidebar content taller than viewport (internal overflow-y: auto)
- Should respect the site header height (sticky top offset = header height)
- Needs to deactivate on mobile where sidebars stack vertically
- Consider interaction with data-page-layout sidebar/split layouts
- Check if data-layout-sticky on the existing layout system already handles this or if it needs extension

**Reference:**
- Current sidebar in doc layouts: `site/src/layouts/docs.html` and `site/src/layouts/element.html`
- Existing sticky header: `header[data-sticky]` and `header[data-scroll-hide]`
- Layout attributes: `src/custom-elements/layout-attributes.css` (data-layout-sticky exists but may not handle sidebar case)
close_reason:
Implemented three features from this bead and vanilla-breeze-pktb:
1. Sticky sidebar nav via data-layout-sticky on layout-sidebar children (CSS) + overflow-x:clip on body/main in doc layouts to unblock position:sticky
2. Horizontal page-toc variant via orientation=horizontal attribute (CSS only)
3. Back-to-top CSS utility with scroll-driven animation (data-back-to-top)
Applied data-layout-sticky to all 4 doc layouts (element, attribute, pattern, guide).

title: Investigate page-toc extensions: horizontal variant, back-to-top, sticky forms
description:
Explore extending the page-toc web component with three new capabilities:

**1. Horizontal variant**
Current page-toc is vertical sidebar-style. Investigate a horizontal variant (e.g. sticky top bar or inline breadcrumb-style) for mobile and single-page layouts where vertical sidebar space isn't available.

**2. Back-to-top links**
Auto-inject a 'Back to top' link at the end of each major section, or provide a floating back-to-top button that appears after scrolling past the first section. Should integrate with page-toc's existing IntersectionObserver scroll-spy.

**3. Sticky form navigation (horizontal + vertical)**
For long multi-section forms (checkout, onboarding, settings), a sticky section indicator that shows current position and allows jumping between form sections. Should work in both horizontal (top bar) and vertical (sidebar) orientations. Could complement or integrate with the wizard system.

**Questions to answer:**
- Should these be page-toc variants (attributes) or separate components?
- How does horizontal page-toc interact with auto-hiding headers?
- Should back-to-top be part of page-toc or a standalone utility?
- Can sticky form nav reuse page-toc's scroll-spy internals?
close_reason:
Investigation complete. Recommendations:

**1. Horizontal variant → page-toc attribute**
Add `orientation="horizontal"` attribute. The scroll-spy internals (IntersectionObserver, heading tracking, active link management) are fully decoupled from layout — only CSS needs to change (flex-direction: row, horizontal padding instead of indentation, border-block-end instead of border-inline-start). Keep in same component since 90% of logic is shared. On mobile, horizontal variant would replace the collapsible details with a scrollable horizontal bar.

**2. Back-to-top → standalone CSS utility**
Back-to-top is NOT a page-toc concern — it's a page-level affordance. Implement as a `data-back-to-top` attribute on an `<a href="#">` element with CSS scroll-driven animation to show/hide. Pure CSS: `animation-timeline: scroll()` with opacity keyframes. No JS needed. If we want auto-injection, that's a separate tiny component (`<back-to-top>`).

**3. Sticky form nav → page-toc with form sections**
page-toc already works with any heading levels — forms with `<fieldset><legend>` sections can be scoped. Add a `sections` attribute as alternative to `levels` that accepts a CSS selector (e.g. `sections="fieldset"`) to scan for non-heading landmarks. The scroll-spy and active-state logic is identical. Horizontal orientation (from #1) makes this natural for form wizards.

**Architecture decision:** All three are variants/extensions of page-toc, not separate components. The scroll-spy internals are already cleanly separated from the view layer.

title: Add horizontal scroll hints to layout-reel
description:
layout-reel horizontal scroll zones aren't visually obvious — users don't realize there's more content to scroll. Add a subtle scroll affordance hint.

**Options to explore:**
- Faded gradient edge mask on the overflow side (CSS-only, no JS)
- Hoverable arrow overlay with low opacity that appears when content overflows
- CSS scroll-driven animation that fades the hint as user scrolls

**Constraints:**
- Must be CSS-only or very minimal JS (IntersectionObserver on sentinel elements)
- Respect prefers-reduced-motion
- Should work on touch (no hover) — consider initial fade-in that disappears after first scroll
- Don't break existing reel scroll-snap behavior

**Context:** Observed in the mobile biosite demo — stats strip and pipeline cards are horizontally scrollable but nothing signals this. The original R&D biosite had the same issue.
close_reason: Added CSS-only data-scroll-hint attribute with gradient edge masks. Supports both-edges and start-only variants. Customizable via --scroll-hint-size.

title: Table border conflicts across extreme themes
description:
Table outer borders render incorrectly across multiple extreme themes. The base table styles and theme overrides conflict, producing visible artifacts:

**Observed issues:**
- **Swiss**: Heavy double-border effect on the outer table edge
- **Kawaii**: Square border box visible around rounded corners (border-radius + border-collapse conflict)
- **Brutalist**: Grey border color (likely base table border leaking through instead of theme color)
- **Cyber**: White/light border instead of the expected cyan/purple theme border color

**Root cause (suspected):**
The native `<table>` element styles likely set borders using base tokens (--color-border or hardcoded values) that extreme themes don't fully override. Additionally, `border-collapse: collapse` prevents `border-radius` from working on tables, causing the square-on-round artifact in Kawaii.

**Scope:**
- Audit `src/native-elements/table/styles.css` for border declarations
- Check which border tokens tables use vs what themes override
- Fix the base table border strategy so themes can cleanly control it
- Verify fix across Swiss, Kawaii, Brutalist, Cyber, and all other extreme themes
- Consider whether tables should use `border-collapse: collapse` or `border-spacing: 0` (the latter allows border-radius)
close_reason: Removed table from border-styles auto-apply selector — tables use their own cell border system

title: Add <ol> list markup to breadcrumb navs for a11y
description:
## Context All 44 breadcrumb `
` elements across the site use bare ``/`` without list markup. VB's CSS already supports `nav.breadcrumb > ol` (provides `display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; align-items: center; gap` plus separator styling via `li:not(:last-child)::after`). Adding `
    ` is correct for breadcrumbs since they represent an ordered path. ## Scope 44 instances across two categories: ### Category 1: Site doc pages (~37 instances) Breadcrumbs in Astro pages following this pattern: ```html
    Snippets / Tabs
    ``` Should become: ```html
    1. Snippets
    2. Tabs
    ``` The `/` separator text should be removed — VB's `nav.breadcrumb li:not(:last-child)::after` generates separators via CSS (`content: "/"` by default, configurable via `data-separator`). Files (site/pages/docs/): - snippets/**/*.astro (~12 files) - elements/**/*.astro (~5 files) - examples/extensions/*.astro (~6 files) - integrations/*.astro (2 files) - lab/experiments/*.astro (~4 files) - lab/theme-picker/*.astro (2 files) ### Category 2: Demo HTML pages (~7 instances) Breadcrumbs in example demo pages, mostly single back links like: ```html
    ← Back to Examples
    ``` These single-link breadcrumbs could either: - Get `
      /
    1. ` wrapping for consistency - Or stay as-is since they're just back links (not real breadcrumb trails) Files (docs/examples/demos/): - dashboard-analytics.html, grid-identity.html, layout-attributes.html, layout-attributes-refactored.html, layout-comparison.html, media-basic.html, regions-basic.html ## CSS Already Supports This From `src/native-elements/nav/styles.css`: - `nav ul, nav ol { list-style: none; margin: 0; padding: 0; }` — list reset - `nav.breadcrumb > ol { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; align-items: center; gap }` — flex layout - `nav.breadcrumb li:not(:last-child)::after { content: "/" }` — auto separators - Separator variants: `data-separator="chevron|arrow|dot|pipe"` No CSS changes needed. ## Approach 1. Write a script to find all breadcrumb navs and transform them 2. For multi-segment breadcrumbs: wrap in `
        /
      1. `, remove literal `/` separators 3. For single back-link breadcrumbs: add `

title: Add opt-in grid identity system for page layouts
description:
Extract grid identity concept from zen-garden as opt-in modules. Semantic HTML elements auto-register grid areas based on document structure.

## Reference
See r-n-d/grid-identity-option.md for full implementation details including complete CSS code.

## Concept
Instead of manually assigning grid areas, semantic elements get them automatically:
- body > header → grid-area: body-header
- body > nav → grid-area: body-nav
- main > article → grid-area: main-article

## Deliverables
1. src/custom-elements/layout-grid-identity.css (identity rules)
2. src/custom-elements/layout-page.css (page templates)
3. Demo page showing page-stack, page-sidebar, page-holy-grail layouts

## Key Features
- Opt-in (not in main bundle by default)
- Works with pure semantic HTML
- Includes :has() adaptations (adjust when nav absent)
- Responsive breakpoints built in
- Complementary to existing layout-attributes system

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Grid identity rules work as documented
- [ ] page-sidebar layout demo functional
- [ ] page-holy-grail layout demo functional
- [ ] :has() adaptations work (missing nav)
- [ ] No conflicts with layout-attributes system
- [ ] Works at all responsive breakpoints
close_reason: Implemented container queries expansion, grid identity system, and subgrid demos with evaluation

title: Expand container queries to sectioning elements
description:
Expand container-type: inline-size beyond cards to semantic sectioning elements (main, article, section, aside) enabling component-level responsive design.

## Reference
See r-n-d/container-queries-expansion.md for full implementation details.

## Summary
- Phase 1: Add container-type to sectioning elements
- Phase 2: Add [data-container] utility attributes
- Phase 3: Add container-aware variants to stack/cluster layouts

## Key Files to Modify
- Create src/utils/containers.css (recommended)
- Or add to src/base/reset.css
- Update src/utils/index.css to import

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sectioning elements establish container context
- [ ] Components adapt to container width not viewport
- [ ] No layout shifts or performance regressions
- [ ] Existing demos continue working
close_reason: Implemented container queries expansion, grid identity system, and subgrid demos with evaluation

title: Heading anchor link icons are too large on nav.html
description:
On /docs/elements/native/nav.html, the heading anchor link icons (chain/link icon next to headings like 'Usage') are visually too large.

**Observed in DevTools:**
- Element structure: `<heading-links data-levels="h2,h3">` contains `<a class="heading-anchor">` with `<icon-wc name="link" size="sm">`
- The icon-wc[size="sm"] has styles: `inline-size: 1.25em; block-size: 1.25em`
- This makes the link icon disproportionately large compared to the heading text

**Expected:** The link icon should be smaller/more subtle relative to the heading text size.

**Location:** Affects heading-links component used within page-toc related pages.
close_reason: Fixed by adding font-size: 1rem to .heading-anchor so icon em units calculate from a fixed base instead of the large heading font-size

title: Scroll-recipes doc bundle (sticky hero, pinned cards, scroll-down, horizontal scroll, share bar)
description:
Add a /docs/patterns/scroll/ folder (or extend /docs/patterns/) with five recipe pages for common scroll-composition patterns CodyHouse ships as templates but VB has no canonical doc for:

1. Scroll-down indicator (animated chevron with bounce decoration; CSS-only)
2. Sticky hero with scale-out on exit (ideal candidate for the new view-progress trigger once vb-XXXX lands)
3. Pinned card stack (Apple-style sticky cards stacking on scroll)
4. Horizontal scroll section (sticky vertical container + horizontal-translate driven by scroll progress)
5. Sticky share bar (position: sticky composition)

Each recipe gets a /docs/examples/demos/ demo file referenced via <browser-window>.

Each is achievable with existing VB primitives (data-sticky, data-effect, data-stagger, layout-cover); these docs surface composition patterns rather than introduce new primitives.

See admin/research/scroll-effects-audit.md (vb-b238) for the source list.

title: Audit: CodyHouse scroll effects vs VB data-effect catalog
description: Codyhouse Effects > Scroll has 13 components. Map each against VB data-effect catalog + scroll-related primitives. Output research doc with coverage matrix + decision on whether to extend data-effect or build new scroll attributes.
close_reason: Wrote admin/research/scroll-effects-audit.md. Finding: VB has broad coverage for CodyHouse's scroll-effect territory via data-effect + data-trigger + data-parallax + data-scroll-hide + data-sticky + reading-progress. One substantive capability gap (CSS scroll-driven animation timelines for continuous scroll-linked animations) filed as vb-8zc6 (P2). Doc-recipes gap (sticky hero, pinned cards, scroll-down, horizontal scroll, share bar) filed as vb-i4uw (P3). Effect gallery filed as vb-acz9 (P4). Methodology caveat: 13 Pro components in Effects > Scroll couldn't be individually enumerated; coverage matrix based on sitemap-exposed scroll URLs + public Pro names.

description: codyhouse-gap-analysis.md claimed minor gaps for loader/spinner and empty-state. Audit existing VB coverage and either close as covered or specify what's worth building.
close_reason: Audit found VB has substantial coverage for both, not gaps. Loading: <loading-spinner> custom element (5 sizes, 4 variants, overlay, reduced-motion); [data-loading] container with skeleton/minimal/hide variants; button[data-loading] with form integration + aria-busy + auto-revert; icon-wc[data-animate=spin]. Empty: unified data-state=empty pattern with output[data-empty] + data-feedback=message presentation, three demos, two pattern docs. Corrected codyhouse-gap-analysis.md to remove both false gap claims and added a detailed follow-up audit note. Nothing worth building.

title: Extend data-copy-target with data-copy-attr
description:
Currently data-copy-target only reads textContent of the target. Add data-copy-attr='<attr-name>' to copy the named attribute instead. Unlocks the markup pattern for state-bearing elements: <output value='#3366cc'>, <input value='...'>, custom elements exposing a value attribute.

Acceptance:
- <button data-copy-target='#x' data-copy-attr='value'>...</button> copies #x's value attribute
- attribute name is read once at click time, not cached
- if attr missing, copy nothing (no throw)
- doc page updated with an example
- still falls back to textContent when data-copy-attr absent

See admin/research/clipboard-audit.md (Gap 1). Lower priority than internal consolidation since helper from copyText() covers internal needs.
close_reason: Added data-copy-attr to copy-init.js getText(). When paired with data-copy-target, reads target.getAttribute(attrName) at click time instead of textContent. Missing attribute returns empty string → click handler no-ops (existing !text guard). Unchanged when absent: falls back to textContent. Doc page gained: row in attributes table, 'Copy from an Attribute' section with <output value> demo, caveat about form-control .value not reflecting to attribute (links to JS API section for live form values). JSDoc + @example in copy-init.js header updated. Tests 1047/1047, build clean.

title: [audit] Other VT components likely have the same [hidden] cascade bug as carousel-wc (vb-kd27)
description:
vb-kd27 fixed carousel-wc's transition mode where author CSS like
`.slide { display: grid }` (unlayered) overrode the component's
`@scope (carousel-wc) { :scope[transition]:defined .carousel-track > [hidden] { display: none } }` rule (layered under @layer web-components), leaving non-current slides visually stacked. Fix was !important on display rules in VT mode.

Audit these components for the same pattern:
- accordion-wc — uses [hidden]? or toggles display? Check.
- tab-set — switches tab panels in VT mode. Same risk.
- content-swap — toggles front/back faces. Check whether [hidden] or [data-face] hides.
- watch-wc — likely has stacked-mode hide logic
- carousel-wc OTHER hide rules — search for `[hidden]` and `display:` in styles to find sister rules that should also be !important

For each:
1. Read styles.css, find any `display: none` rules that toggle visual state.
2. Check whether they're inside @layer web-components or @scope.
3. If they ARE in a layer, write a tiny test demo that adds a competing `.x { display: block }` or `.x { display: grid }` author rule and verify the component breaks.
4. Apply !important to the structural rule.

Pattern in memory: popover_display_gotcha.md, feedback_structural_css_invariants.md.

ENTRY POINT
grep -rl '\[hidden\]\|display:' src/web-components/*/styles.css | xargs grep -nE 'display:\s*none' | grep -v '!important'

title: Conformance cleanup: pack demos + long tail (1088 warnings remaining)
description:
Continuation of vanilla-breeze-k7sj. Session 2026-05-21 cleared 540 of 1628 warnings (33%) across 15 files using the migration pattern documented at ~/.claude/projects/-Users-tpowell-src-vanilla-breeze/memory/conformance_div_inline_migration.md. lint:html stayed at 0 errors throughout.

REMAINING WORK
- Pack demos (~317 warnings) — these need a SEPARATE strategy because inline-style and decorative <div> ARE the demo content (e.g. style="background: var(--m-yellow)" is showcasing the token, not styling). Files:
  - memphis-pack.html (111)
  - pack-explorer.html (58)
  - color-mix.html (47)
  - kawaii-pack.html (38)
  - effects-kitchen-sink.html (35)
  - design-system-pack.html (28)
  Options to consider:
    a) Allowlist with rationale in scripts/vb-conformance-allowlist.json
    b) Restructure: replace 18+ palette divs with data-token attribute selectors and one CSS rule per token (verbose but mechanical)
    c) Move pack demos to a documented "showcase" subdir excluded from the rule

- Allowlisted files (~119 warnings) — currently in scripts/vb-conformance-allowlist.json:
  - layout-comparison.html (72) — vb/no-div allowlisted
  - progressive-enhancement.html (47) — vb/no-div + vb/use-form-field allowlisted
  Decision needed: remove from allowlist and migrate, or keep allowlisted?

- Non-pack long tail (~650 warnings across ~150 files, mostly <20 each):
  - carousel-basic.html (19)
  - effects-lab.html (17)
  - backdrop-themes.html (21) — borderline pack-like
  - accordion-transitions.html (13)
  - dashboard-analytics.html
  - effects-lab.html
  - markdown-editable-basic.html
  - markdown-editor-basic.html
  - + many more
  Apply the standard pattern.

MIGRATION PATTERN
See memory file conformance_div_inline_migration.md for the full hierarchy. Key points:
- Layout grid containers → <layout-grid data-layout-min="X" data-layout-gap="Y">
- Body-shell mockup wrappers → <section data-layout="body-X"> (NOT <article> — article can't contain main per HTML spec)
- Content groupings → <section> or <article>
- Decorative inline blocks → <span> with CSS-driven display
- Component face wrappers ([data-face]) → collapse to single semantic element
- <div class="token-display"><pre> → <figure class="token-display"><pre>
- Callouts → <aside>
- Inline styles → data-attribute hooks or named CSS classes

GOTCHAS
- <main> cannot descend from <article>. Body-shell mockup wrappers must be <section>.
- Don't sed-replace </article> → </section> across files; many have both outer wrappers needing change AND inner <article> region-cards/feed-cards that must stay. Edit each closer individually or use a state-tracking Python pass.
- When converting <div> with controls to <nav>, add unique aria-label per landmark to avoid breaking unique-landmark lint rule.

ENTRY POINTS
- npm run conformance > /tmp/conf.out 2>&1 ; tail -3 /tmp/conf.out
- npm run lint:html > /tmp/lint.out 2>&1 ; grep -cE '^\s+[0-9]+:[0-9]+\s+error' /tmp/lint.out (must stay at 0)
- Per-file warning count: awk '/^demos\/.*\.html$/{f=\$0;next} /warning/{print f}' /tmp/conf.out | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn

BLOCKS: nothing
RELATED: vanilla-breeze-k7sj (parent), vanilla-breeze-3cv4 (quality-gate flip — conformance already exits 0, this is quality-pass cleanup not blocking)

title: Local-dev preview reliability fix was reverted on main — re-evaluate
description:
On 2026-05-18, commit 79c49db6 on branch fix/local-preview-reliability landed two related fixes for local vb.test reliability:

1. site/scripts/setup-preview.js — made symlink/copy setup idempotent. The prior `if (existsSync(linkPath)) return;` guard meant once assemble-site.js had copied real directories in, link() never overwrote them on re-run, leaving the preview tree stale. Patch: rmSync the target before link/copy, so re-runs converge.

2. package.json — `npm run preview` was `cd site && npm run preview` which started its own http-server on :3456 (wrong port for Caddy-on-vb.test) and skipped build:cdn + build:demos, so /cdn/* and stitched demo paths 404'd. Patch: replace with build:cdn → build:demos → cd site && build + pagefind → setup-preview chain. ~18s cold. http-server variant preserved as `npm run preview:http`.

CURRENT STATE: main has BOTH fixes reverted. `scripts/setup-preview.js` doesn't exist on main; `npm run preview` is back to `cd site && npm run preview`. The fix/local-preview-reliability branch was deleted on 2026-05-21 as part of consolidation cleanup.

WHAT TO INVESTIGATE: Was the revert intentional? If local vb.test preview is broken, restore. Recovery diff lives in commit 79c49db6 (available via reflog if branch is fully deleted) or in the bd 'Before Request' tags in fleet-local-history snapshots.

title: stamp effect invisible on dark backgrounds (mix-blend-mode: multiply)

title: Author internal VT contract doc for component contributors
description:
From admin/r-n-d/linking-and-navigation/view-transitions.md gap #4.

THE PROBLEM
There is no central internal guide for component authors implementing View Transitions. Patterns are scattered across ~10 components, each rediscovered independently. New contributors have to read existing components and infer conventions.

WHAT TO DOCUMENT
- Naming strategy for view-transition-name (uniqueness within scope, collision avoidance, when to use a stable id vs index)
- When to use view-transition-class (presets vs per-element)
- Direction-state conventions (data-vt-direction on :root vs per-element)
- Fallback requirements (host-not-connected, no startViewTransition, prefers-reduced-motion — all three branches must still mutate the DOM synchronously)
- The shared keyframe vocabulary (vt-fade-in, vt-slide-in-left, etc.) and where they live
- The viewTransitionSwap helper from src/lib/vb-view-transition.js — when and how to use it
- Test matrix expectations: at minimum, one unit test exercising the three fallback branches per component

WHERE TO PUT IT
Likely admin/contributor-guides/view-transitions.md or similar. Consider whether it should be a skill in .claude/skills/ so future Claude sessions pick it up automatically when working on VT-related components.

P3 — meaningful contributor friction, low blast radius to fix.

title: Audit + sync the two View Transitions doc pages (data-transition + view-transitions)
description:
From admin/r-n-d/linking-and-navigation/view-transitions.md gap #2.

THE TWO PAGES
- /docs/attributes/view-transitions/ — historically framed as 'CSS-only page transition effects with named groups, shared element morphing, animation presets, and a declarative init script'
- /docs/attributes/data-transition/ — historically framed as 'Declarative View Transitions API integration. Marks elements that morph, slide, or fade during state changes'

CLAIMED INCONSISTENCIES (from the R&D doc)
1. /docs/attributes/view-transitions/ still frames same-document usage mostly as manual SPA-API usage. Reality: tab-set / accordion-wc / carousel-wc and ~7 more components now provide built-in same-document VT via data-transition. The docs should reflect that.
2. Browser-support messaging is inconsistent across pages (some pages mention Firefox support levels; one table still says pending). Standardize on a single block authors can trust.
3. The content-swap doc doesn't explicitly call out that state changes are already wrapped in same-document VT when supported.

WHAT TO DO
- Read both pages end-to-end
- Update to reflect current reality: ~10 components ship VT built-in via data-transition
- Cross-link the two pages so authors find the right one (data-transition for component-level, view-transitions for page-level)
- Single canonical browser-support block (one of the pages owns it; the other links to it)
- Mention the keyframe dependency from vanilla-breeze-{keyframes-bead-id} once that's resolved

P3 — docs accuracy on a shipped feature.

title: Resolve implicit keyframe dependency: VT animations break without utils/view-transitions.css
description:
From admin/r-n-d/linking-and-navigation/view-transitions.md gap #1.

THE PROBLEM
Several component stylesheets reference VT keyframes defined only in src/utils/view-transitions.css:
- src/web-components/tab-set/styles.css uses vt-fade-in / vt-slide-in-left / vt-slide-in-right
- src/web-components/accordion-wc/styles.css uses vt-fade-in / vt-slide-in-left
- src/web-components/carousel-wc/styles.css likely similar

utils/view-transitions.css is NOT imported by src/main.css or src/main-core.css — it's opt-in via the docs-site config (site.viewTransitionsCss). A consumer who:
  1. Loads vanilla-breeze core
  2. Uses <tab-set data-transition='fade'>

gets a silent animation failure: the animation-name resolves to an undefined keyframe and nothing animates. No console error, no visual hint.

THREE OPTIONS (decide before fixing)
1. Move the keyframes into per-component stylesheets (each component ships with what it needs). Pro: zero implicit deps. Con: keyframes duplicate across files.
2. Auto-import utils/view-transitions.css from src/main.css when any consumer references vt-* keyframes. Pro: components stay slim. Con: pulls in cross-document VT @view-transition rule even on sites that don't want it.
3. Split the keyframes (cheap, no @view-transition) into a dedicated src/utils/vt-keyframes.css that main.css imports unconditionally; leave cross-document config opt-in. Pro: lazy approach, narrow surface. Recommended.

EXISTING SCOPE
Component-level data-transition implementations exist in: content-swap, tab-set, accordion-wc, carousel-wc — plus the Wave 2 view-transition-audit (vanilla-breeze-7ixr) added VT to ~10 more (diagram-wc, gantt-chart, flow-diagram, site-map-wc, empathy-map, etc.) via the shared viewTransitionSwap helper. ALL of them depend on the same shared keyframes if their per-component CSS references them — needs a sweep to verify.

P3 because component-level VT IS a documented feature and the silent-break is a real authoring gotcha.

title: docs: write attribute pages for data-layout, data-layout-density, data-page-layout, data-sticky
description:
Audit (vanilla-breeze-jxlv-followups) found four data-* attributes shipped in src/ but without doc pages — the navigation.json had broken hrefs pointing at non-existent /docs/attributes/{slug}/ pages. The broken nav entries were removed (commit on this same audit pass) so users don't hit dead links. Now write the actual doc pages.

Attributes:
- data-layout — used in src/native-elements/lists/styles.css + src/utils/sticky.css + src/utils/print.css. Likely a generic layout-mode hook.
- data-layout-density — used in src/custom-elements/layout-attributes.css. Density variant for layout primitives (compact / normal / comfortable).
- data-page-layout — used in src/custom-elements/layout-attributes.css + src/tokens/backdrop.css. Page-shape attribute on root or body.
- data-sticky — used in src/main.js + src/main-core.js + src/native-elements/footer/styles.css. Sticky-positioning utility (composed with the StickyManager from src/lib/sticky-manager.js).

For each, write a /docs/attributes/{slug}.html using the standard 'attribute' layout and the data-paged.html / data-toggle.html structure (Overview, Attributes table, Behavior, Accessibility, See also). Wire each into the sidebar under the appropriate group in site/data/navigation.json after creating the doc.

Done when the four pages exist, link is restored in nav, and the audit (the comm -23 between /tmp/attr-docs.txt and /tmp/attr-nav.txt) returns clean for these four entries.
acceptance_criteria: data-layout, data-layout-density, data-page-layout, data-sticky each have a doc page using the attribute layout; nav sidebar links restored to those pages; pages cover what each attribute does, where it's used, and any composition notes.
close_reason: Four attribute doc pages written and nav entries restored. Pages: data-layout (all 25+ values + modifiers + element-vs-attribute symmetry vs <layout-*>), data-layout-density (compact/spacious + token table + composition with themes), data-page-layout (six templates + semantic-element grid-area mapping + container queries + landmark-semantics rationale), data-sticky (both roles + --sticky-offset variable contract + re-measure triggers). Final audit returns zero gaps in either direction (no attribute docs missing from nav; no nav links pointing to non-existent attribute pages). Each page follows the standard 'attribute' layout pattern from data-paged.html / data-toggle.html (Overview → Usage → Values → Modifiers/Composition → When to use).

title: data-toggle: generic attribute-toggling upscale
description:
Generic 'click me, flip an attribute on the target' upscale. The catch-all for the recurring 'make this thing change state' pattern that today fans out into ad-hoc JS or per-component imperative APIs.

OVERLAP CHECK FIRST (do this before coding):
- popovertarget (native) — opens [popover]; no toggling of arbitrary attrs
- commandfor (native) — invokes a target's interestaction-style commands
- data-spoiler — reveals masked content (specific use case)
- data-show-when / data-hide-when — conditional based on form state
- details + data-accordion — open/close <details>
If the proposed shape overlaps too much, scope down or skip.

Proposed API (start narrow):
<button data-toggle="hidden" data-toggle-target="#sidebar">Toggle sidebar</button>
<aside id="sidebar">...</aside>

Or with data-toggle-on/off for explicit values:
<button data-toggle="data-state" data-toggle-target="#x"
        data-toggle-on="open" data-toggle-off="closed">...</button>

Variants:
- Boolean attrs (hidden, disabled, open) — flip presence
- String attrs (data-state, aria-expanded) — cycle on/off values
- Self-target (no data-toggle-target → toggle on the button itself, e.g. aria-pressed)

Behavior:
- aria-expanded auto-set on the button if target has [popover] or [data-state]
- Emits toggle:change { attribute, value, target } on the button
- Idempotent
- Honors keyboard (button is native — Space/Enter just work)

A11y:
- Set aria-controls on the button to the target's id
- Set aria-pressed on the button when self-targeting boolean attrs

Tests:
- Unit (Vitest): attribute resolution, value-flip logic
- Playwright: click toggles, keyboard space/enter, aria-pressed updates, multi-target

Done when:
- the overlap check is documented in the doc page
- works for hidden, disabled, open, [data-state] toggling at minimum
- aria contracts handled for the common cases
- doc page covers when to reach for native popovertarget vs this attribute
acceptance_criteria: data-toggle covers the 'flip an attr on target' shape without overlap with native popovertarget/commandfor; aria-controls + aria-pressed wired; doc page documents when to use vs native alternatives; unit + Playwright coverage.
close_reason: data-toggle shipped as the catch-all 'click button → flip attribute' upscale. Three auto-detected modes: boolean attr presence (hidden/disabled/open/etc.), ARIA two-state (aria-pressed/expanded/selected/checked, true/false strings), custom on/off cycle (data-toggle-on / data-toggle-off, default true/false). Plus class:name shorthand for class toggles. Overlap-checked against native popovertarget, commandfor (via data-command), data-show-when, data-spoiler, data-switch, data-accordion — each covers a narrower shape; data-toggle is the catch-all. A11y: aria-controls auto-set when external; aria-pressed reflected on self-toggling boolean attrs; aria-expanded reflected when target has [popover] or data-toggle-on='open'. Pure logic (parseToggleSpec, nextValue, isBooleanAttr, isAriaTwoState) with 15 unit tests; DOM init ~150 lines; 7 Playwright tests cover boolean/ARIA/custom/class flips + events + keyboard + aria-controls + aria-expanded. Doc page at /docs/attributes/data-toggle/ documents the decision matrix vs other primitives. Demo at /docs/snippets/demos/data-toggle-basic.html.

title: data-sortable: client-side list/table sorting upscale on any container
description:
Sibling to data-paged (vanilla-breeze-07td). Drop data-sortable on any list-shaped container — native
    /
      / or VB collection (, , ) — and direct children get reorderable by named keys. Same upscale-attribute model as data-paged / data-spellcheck / data-accordion: pure attribute, no custom element, auto-attaches via the shared init registry. Composes with data-paged: sort fires a child-list mutation, data-paged re-paginates from page 1. This resolves the open content-feed recipe note ('A generic data-sortable upscale for arbitrary list containers may land later if a third use case appears'). Two API shapes for the two common cases: 1. TABLE — clicking
      cycles asc / desc / off:
      Name Date
      Alice...
      Row sort-value comes from the corresponding       		text content, or data-value on the , or the when type=date. 2. LIST — external control points at the container:
      • ...
      Children declare data-sort-{key}="value". Leading - prefix on the value flips direction. Attributes: - data-sortable opt-in on container - data-sortable-default initial sort key (e.g. "-date" for newest) - data-sort-by (reflected) current sort key - data-sort-direction (reflected) asc | desc | none - data-sort="key" on       		for table mode - data-sort-type="text|number|date" type hint for comparator - data-sort-target="sel" on external controls (button / select) - data-sort-{key}="val" on list children Behavior: - Stable sort (Array.prototype.sort is stable in modern engines) - Reorders DOM nodes in place - Honors a single sort key at a time (multi-key is a future extension) - Emits sort:change { key, direction, total } - prefers-reduced-motion honored (no transitions) - Idempotent: removing data-sortable doesn't restore original order (document) Pieces: - src/lib/data-sortable.js — pure compareByKey + sortItems (testable, no DOM) - src/utils/data-sortable-init.js — DOM upscale (~200 lines) - src/utils/data-sortable.css — minimal styling for column header indicators - main.js + main-autoload.js — lazy-load registration - /docs/attributes/data-sortable/ — full reference page - /docs/snippets/demos/data-sortable-basic.html — table + list demos Tests: - Unit (Vitest): compare functions for text/number/date types, key parsing (- prefix), null/empty value handling - Playwright: click cycles asc→desc→off; ,
        ,
          , , with no per-element wiring - text / number / date type hints all sort correctly - composes with data-paged (sort fires mutation; pagination resets to page 1) - doc page documents both shapes with overlap note vs data-table's built-in sort
acceptance_criteria: data-sortable works on tables (clickable <th>) and external-control lists; text/number/date type hints; composes with data-paged; emits sort:change; pure logic unit-tested; Playwright covers DOM behavior; doc page + demo.
close_reason: data-sortable shipped as upscale attribute with two API shapes (clickable <th> for tables, external <select>/<button> for lists). Pure logic in src/lib/data-sortable.js with 18 unit tests; DOM init in src/utils/data-sortable-init.js (~290 lines); 8 Playwright tests covering both shapes + asc/desc/none cycle + numeric vs string + <time datetime> for date types + keyboard activation + sort:change event + composition with data-paged (sort dispatches paged:goto { page: 1 } so user lands on page 1 of newly sorted list). text/number/date comparators with empty-as-end handling. aria-sort reflected on headers; native a11y for external controls. Doc page at /docs/attributes/data-sortable/ documents the data-table built-in-sort overlap and when to reach for which. Demo at /docs/snippets/demos/data-sortable-basic.html.

Regression since 2026-05-17 — partly new demos landing without lint coverage, partly rule tightening since dd83f792 (checking out dd83f792's demos against current rules also yields 148 errors, so html-validate rules / shared config moved).

WORK STYLE: Interactive — ask the user before each batch. Demos in this repo follow several conventions that aren't obvious from the rule names (no <div> in demos, <browser-window>/<code-block> for doc pages, etc.). title: docs: /docs/patterns/content-feed/ recipe page
description:
Follow-up to vanilla-breeze-07td. The data-paged doc page references this recipe page; landing it closes the content-feed resolution properly (the entry in admin/future-wc.md is already marked 'Resolved as recipe + data-paged').

Page: site/src/pages/docs/patterns/content-feed/index.html (layout: docs)

Recipe sections:
1. The pattern in one snippet — site-search + card-list + data-paged
2. Variants:
   - Blog index (ul + data-paged + data-paged-url='page')
   - Product grid (layout-grid + data-paged + data-paged-style='load-more')
   - Search results (site-search target + card-list + data-paged-style='infinite')
   - Settings table (table + data-paged + data-paged-style='prev-next')
3. URL state strategy — when to opt into data-paged-url and when not to
4. Filtering composition — site-search vs vanilla <select> + JS, with examples
5. Sorting composition — note that VB doesn't ship a generic sort attribute yet (data-table has one for table cells); sort either via consumer's data layer or via a future data-sortable upscale
6. A11y notes — landmark roles, sr-only status announcements
7. When NOT to use this pattern — handoff to <activity-feed> (timelines), <chat-thread> (conversations), <data-table> (cells with sort/filter built-in)

Cross-links:
- /docs/attributes/data-paged/
- /docs/elements/web-components/site-search/
- /docs/elements/web-components/card-list/
- /docs/elements/web-components/activity-feed/ (the 'when not')

Add navigation entry under Patterns sidebar group (check site/data/navigation.json).
acceptance_criteria: Recipe page live at /docs/patterns/content-feed/; covers blog, product, search, table variants; cross-links wired; navigation entry added; page renders without HTML validation errors.
close_reason: Recipe page shipped at /docs/patterns/data/content-feed/ (site/src/pages/docs/patterns/data/content-feed.html). Covers: the one-snippet composition (site-search + card-list + data-paged), four variants (blog index, product grid, search results, settings table), URL state strategy, filter and sort composition notes, accessibility, and a 'when NOT to use this pattern' section with handoffs to activity-feed / chat-thread / comment-thread / data-table. Decoupled-controls section cross-links to the new <pager-wc> element. Listed in the Patterns sidebar (navigation.json Data Display group).

title: data-paged: Playwright visual + behavior baseline
description:
Follow-up to vanilla-breeze-07td (data-paged shipped). Add Playwright coverage for the DOM behavior the unit tests can't cover.

Coverage:
- Visual snapshot of the demo page (/docs/snippets/demos/data-paged-basic.html) — desktop + mobile viewports
- Behavior:
  - Click page 2 → second slice visible, first hidden
  - Click prev/next → navigates correctly
  - Disabled state on prev at page 1 / next at last page
  - URL sync: clicking page 2 updates ?page=2; reload preserves page
  - back/forward: navigating updates both URL and rendered slice
  - load-more style: 'Load more (N)' text reflects remaining count
  - infinite style: scrolling the sentinel into view appends next page
  - MutationObserver: appending children re-paginates without losing current page
- A11y:
  - axe-core clean on each demo section
  - aria-current='page' on active number
  - keyboard navigation through the nav buttons

Lives at tests/components/data-paged.spec.js or tests/visual/data-paged.spec.js (whichever convention fits — check what theme-picker.spec.js does).

Skip if Caddy isn't up; otherwise should run as part of npm run test:components.
acceptance_criteria: Playwright spec covers click-to-navigate, prev/next disable, URL sync round-trip, load-more counter, infinite-scroll sentinel, MutationObserver re-pagination, axe-core clean.
close_reason: 14 Playwright tests at tests/components/data-paged.spec.js cover the full data-paged + pager-wc DOM behavior (all 14 pass in ~4.4s). Coverage: numbered click-to-navigate, prev/next disabled state, paged:change event detail, prev-next status text, load-more counter + exhaustion, table tbody pagination with visible thead, URL sync round-trip + back navigation, MutationObserver re-pagination on appended children, pager-wc multi-instance sync, prev-next status mirroring, pager-wc:navigate event, implicit-target binding to nearest preceding [data-paged] sibling. Bonus fix: discovered the demo pages use the autoload bundle which had no data-paged conditional import — added that to src/main-autoload.js so demos (and any autoload consumer) get pagination. axe-core not in project deps; relied on Playwright's built-in ARIA assertions (aria-current, button accessible names) for a11y coverage instead.

title: pager-wc: standalone pagination controls element targeting any [data-paged] container
description:
Companion to the data-paged upscale attribute (vanilla-breeze-07td, shipped). Provides the element form of the same primitive — authors can choose the surface that fits the situation, mirroring the layout-* element / data-layout-* attribute symmetry across the framework.

Use cases this enables:
- Pagination controls in a page header / sticky toolbar with items below
- Pagination controls in a footer with the list above
- Multiple control instances pointing at the same paginated container (e.g. controls both above and below for long lists, kept in sync)
- A 'jump to page' input as a sibling of the standard prev/next nav
- Controls in a different layout cell (e.g. sidebar nav for a paginated main column)

Element shape:
<pager-wc target='#feed' style='numbered'></pager-wc>
<pager-wc for='feed'>... custom slotted content ...</pager-wc>

Scope:
- src/web-components/pager-wc/ (logic.js, api.json, static.html)
- ~30-50 lines of glue: looks up target element, listens for paged:change, re-renders own controls
- Reuses the same control builders as data-paged-init.js (extract shared helpers into src/lib/data-paged-controls.js)
- Attributes:
  - target / for: CSS selector OR ID of the [data-paged] container
  - style: numbered | prev-next | load-more (omit infinite — sentinel doesn't make sense decoupled)
  - controls-only / minimal: optional layout hints (skip ellipses, etc.)
- Events:
  - pager-wc:navigate { page }: bubbles before delegating to the target
  - Target's existing paged:change still fires from the data-paged engine
- Multi-instance sync: all <pager-wc target='#x'> instances re-render on target's paged:change

Tests:
- Unit: target resolution (selector vs id-fallback), syncs across multiple instances
- E2E (Playwright): controls in header drive items in main; back/forward updates both

Done when:
- a header <pager-wc> can drive a footer-positioned <ul data-paged>
- two <pager-wc> instances pointing at the same target stay in sync
- removing the target gracefully disables the controls (aria-disabled, no errors)
- doc page at /docs/elements/web-components/pager-wc/ covers the symmetry with data-paged

Architectural note: this preserves VB's element/attribute symmetry pattern. Same shape as <layout-grid> + data-layout-min on the layout side. Authors pick the surface that fits the markup; both feed the same engine.
acceptance_criteria: <pager-wc target='#x'> drives any [data-paged] container; multiple instances stay in sync via paged:change; control-builder helpers extracted to src/lib/data-paged-controls.js for reuse; doc page covers element/attribute symmetry.
close_reason: <pager-wc> shipped as the element form of data-paged. Same engine via shared src/lib/data-paged-controls.js (extracted buildNav + wireNav from data-paged-init); decoupled controls communicate with the engine via paged:goto / paged:loadmore custom events on the target. Multi-instance sync via paged:change subscription. Implicit target via nearest preceding [data-paged] sibling. Doc page covers the element/attribute symmetry with data-paged. Demo at /docs/snippets/demos/pager-wc-basic.html (top+bottom sync, sticky header, sidebar-driving-main, implicit target). 991 tests still pass.

title: data-paged: upscale attribute for pagination on any list-shaped container
description:
Upscale attribute that adds pagination to any list-shaped container — native (<ul>, <ol>, <table>) or VB collection (<card-list>, <data-table>, <layout-grid>). Same model as data-spellcheck / data-accordion / data-responsive: pure attribute, no custom element, auto-attaches on DOMContentLoaded.

Replaces the proposed <pager-wc> component for the common case. A future <pager-wc target='#x'> can be a thin ~30-line glue if a real consumer needs decoupled controls, but is NOT in scope here.

This also resolves <content-feed> (vanilla-breeze-future-wc) as 'recipe + data-paged', mirroring the May 2026 Planning consolidation pattern (5 components → 1 primitive + recipes).

Scope:
- src/utils/data-paged.js — upscale script, ~200 lines
- Auto-attaches on DOMContentLoaded; MutationObserver for late-added containers
- Walks DIRECT children, applies hidden outside the active page slice
- Re-paginates when items add/remove via MutationObserver

Attributes (on the host container):
- data-paged (boolean, opt-in)
- data-paged-size (default 10)
- data-paged-page (1-based; default 1)
- data-paged-style (numbered | prev-next | load-more | infinite; default numbered)
- data-paged-window (default 3; numbers around current for 'numbered' style)
- data-paged-controls (before | after | both | none; default after)
- data-paged-url (opt-in URL param name; uses History API)

Behavior:
- Injects <nav aria-label='Pagination'> sibling(s) with native <button>s
- aria-current='page' on active number
- Emits paged:change { page, size, total }
- 'infinite' style reuses IntersectionObserver pattern (parity with <activity-feed data-infinite>)
- prefers-reduced-motion honored
- Idempotent: removing data-paged restores original child visibility

Tests:
- Unit: pagination math, page-clamp, controls-render, URL sync round-trip
- E2E (Playwright): keyboard navigation, aria contracts, MutationObserver re-paginate

Done when:
- works on <ul>, <ol>, <table>, <card-list>, <data-table>, <layout-grid> with no per-element wiring
- all four styles render correctly
- axe-clean per WAI-ARIA pagination pattern
- opt-in URL sync round-trips through browser history
- recipe doc page at /docs/patterns/content-feed/ shows site-search + card-list + data-paged composition
acceptance_criteria: data-paged works on any list-shaped container; numbered/prev-next/load-more/infinite styles; opt-in URL sync via History API; emits paged:change; axe-clean; documented as the canonical resolution for content-feed and pager-wc.
close_reason: data-paged shipped as upscale attribute for any list-shaped container (<ul>, <ol>, <table>, <card-list>, <data-table>, <layout-grid>). Pure pagination math in src/lib/data-paged.js with 19 unit tests; DOM init in src/utils/data-paged-init.js (~310 lines, MutationObserver-driven, four control styles: numbered / prev-next / load-more / infinite). Tables paginate <tbody> rows. Infinite-style sentinel is a SIBLING to avoid host child-observer loop. URL sync via History API (data-paged-url='page' opt-in). Emits paged:change. Native <button>s with aria-current/aria-hidden/aria-live; honours prefers-reduced-motion. Doc page at /docs/attributes/data-paged/; demo at /docs/snippets/demos/data-paged-basic.html. Resolves the proposed <content-feed> component (now a recipe + this attribute) and the in-container <pager-wc> case (decoupled-controls case left as a backlog question).

title: Re-enable needs: quality-gate on deploy build job after ytdj lands green
description:
With vanilla-breeze-ytdj merged, the validator-test npx races on CI are fixed. The build job in .github/workflows/deploy.yml currently has `needs: quality-gate` commented out (line 113-119), so a failing quality gate cannot block a deploy.

WHAT TO DO
1. Watch 3-5 consecutive quality-gate runs on main to confirm all gate checks pass consistently: lint:js, lint:html, typecheck, conformance, theme-tokens, budget, and the validator tests fixed in ytdj.
2. Uncomment `needs: quality-gate` on the build job and remove the surrounding explanatory comment block.
3. Commit + watch a deploy run end-to-end to confirm the gate now actually blocks on failure.

WHY THIS MATTERS
This is the real win called out in vanilla-breeze-ytdj. Until this lands, the quality gate is advisory only — a regression in lint/typecheck/conformance/validators can ship to prod silently. Restoring the gate makes the signal load-bearing again.

WHEN
Don't flip this prematurely. The existing workflow comment explicitly says "Re-enable once all gate checks are known to pass consistently on main." Give CI a few runs first.
notes: 2026-05-19: joq2 typecheck cleared (1178 → 0). lint:html (345), conformance (840), budget (13 overages) regressed since their parent beads closed. Reopened 6gzm, k7sj; filed new it1q for budget. Gate flip blocked on those three.

title: DTCG: theme-picker 'Download as DTCG' link per theme
description:
Follow-up to vanilla-breeze-205h. Surface the .tokens.json artifacts emitted by Phase 2 in <theme-picker> so users can grab the DTCG file with one click.

Manifest already carries .dtcg and .dtcgDark fields per theme. theme-picker (src/web-components/theme-picker/logic.js, ~820 lines) renders the theme list — add a small download icon link next to each theme that hits the manifest URL and triggers a save.

Scope:
- Add a small 'Download DTCG' affordance per theme in the picker UI
- Use manifest.{slug}.dtcg + .dtcgDark URLs
- Native <a download href=...> rather than synthesized blobs (the file already exists at the CDN URL)
- A11y: label as 'Download {theme name} as DTCG tokens.json'
- No core picker logic refactor — surgical addition only

Done when each theme row in the picker has a working DTCG download.
acceptance_criteria: Per-theme DTCG download link in theme-picker; uses manifest URLs; aria-labeled; works for both base and -dark variants where present.
close_reason: Shipped: per-theme DTCG download link in theme-picker's swatch grid. Module-level singleton manifest fetch, label-wrapping approach (avoids label-click radio activation), native <a download>, mode-tracking href that flips between .dtcg and .dtcgDark per ThemeManager state. Smoke-tested on the popover demo: 43 wrappers across 45 brand inputs (2 unwrapped are system pseudo-themes without manifest entries), every wrapper holds 1 link, hrefs correctly track light/dark mode toggle, zero console errors. Surgical addition — no core picker logic refactor.

title: vb:versions-manifest meta tag + frontmatter wiring (build-pipeline)
description:
Build-pipeline support for version-switcher's meta-tag fallback (Phase 3 dep).

Two pieces:
1. Extend admin/specs/meta-tag-contract-v1.md to document vb:versions-manifest (URL of the per-page versions JSON list).
2. Extend site/plugins/generate-provenance-meta.js to read frontmatter 'versionsManifest' and emit <meta name="vb:versions-manifest" content="…">.

Optional but recommended:
- Frontmatter 'versions' array → site/plugin collapses into a JSON file at dist/data/versions/<page-id>.json + auto-emits the meta tag pointing at it.
- Future: cook plugin that reads page git history and generates a default mode-B manifest. Out of scope for v1.

Lives outside the version-switcher component itself; unblocks Phase 3 of the component.
close_reason: Shipped: meta-tag contract documents vb:versions-manifest as the per-page versions-list URL (companion to vb:version / vb:version-url). generate-provenance-meta.js wired to read frontmatter versionsManifest and emit the meta tag. Incidentally caught + fixed a Phase 1 alignment bug — version-switcher was reading/writing meta[name=vb:version] but the provenance system uses meta[itemprop=version]; both #deriveCurrentId and #actionSwap now use the standards-aligned tag. Plan updated to match. Verified meta tag emission via build + grep on rendered HTML. Phase 3 (j6uo) data-source fallback now ready.

title: version-switcher Phase 3: provenance integration (page-info + banner + meta fallback)
description:
Phase 3 of the version-switcher plan (admin/r-n-d/version-switcher.md).

Wire version-switcher into the existing provenance system:
- data-page-info-target=ID: switcher mounts as a section inside the targeted <page-info>'s expandable detail panel rather than a standalone trigger
- data-banner: full-width archived-version banner above the page ("You are viewing v1.x — Latest is v2.1"). role=region + aria-live=polite.
- Meta-tag fallback: if no inline data and no data-src, read <meta name="vb:versions-manifest"> URL and fetch from it. Resolves the new vb:versions-manifest meta (companion to existing vb:version / vb:version-url).
- Cross-link from page-info doc page ("for switching between versions, see version-switcher")
- Cross-link from time-index doc page ("for per-page history, see version-switcher")

Depends on Phase 1 + the build-tooling bead (vb:versions-manifest meta wiring).
close_reason: Shipped Phase 3 of version-switcher: data-src URL fetch + vb:versions-manifest meta fallback (with Loading state and out-of-order fetch protection), data-banner archived-version warning aside (role=region+aria-live=polite, links to latest non-archived), data-page-info-target mount-inside-panel mode (with MutationObserver for page-info auto mode + 5s safety timeout). Cross-links added on page-info and time-index doc pages. Smoke-tested all three on a demo page that exercises meta fallback + banner + panel mount simultaneously. Closes the version-switcher 4-bead chain.

title: DTCG: documentation pass (theme-library page, dtcg explainer, theme-composer updates)
description:
Documentation track for vanilla-breeze-jxlv (DTCG theme pipeline). Lands incrementally as each implementation phase ships.

Pages to add or update:
- /docs/tools/theme-composer (existing) — document import + export flows; add screenshots of the DTCG UI.
- /docs/tools/theme-library (new) — list all themes with CSS + DTCG download links; copy-link for CDN URLs; filter by type (brand/extreme/access).
- /docs/explore/dtcg (new) — explain what DTCG is, what VB supports (and the OKLCH / relative-color caveats), pointers to the spec + Tokens Studio + Style Dictionary.
- Per catalog entry (Phase 4) — short 'About this system' blurb with license + attribution + source link.

Includes copy for:

- Trademark disclaimer template for brand themes.
- 'Why DTCG?' page-level pitch (interop, exchange, Figma/Tokens Studio compatibility).

Done when all pages are live, examples render, and copy is reviewed.
acceptance_criteria: theme-library page live with all 50 themes; theme-composer docs cover import/export; /docs/explore/dtcg published; per-catalog blurbs in place.
close_reason:
Three doc surfaces shipped:
1) /docs/concepts/dtcg/ — explainer covering what DTCG is, why it matters for VB, what's supported (export/import/catalog/library), parametric round-trip (relative-color + light-dark preserved via $extensions), composite typography handling, tooling pointers. Wired into Concepts sidebar.
2) /docs/tools/theme-library/ — new tool page rendering every shipped theme as a filterable table from /cdn/themes/manifest.json with CSS + DTCG (base + dark) downloads. Tier filter + DTCG-only checkbox. Brand-theme trademark note included.
