Brand trademark disclaimer text lives in each _brand-*.css header (one per file, removable). Tools index updated to surface theme-library.

title: DTCG: promote demos/alpenglow brand experiments (McDonald's, Starbucks, IBM, Anthropic) to first-class themes
description:
Phase 5 of vanilla-breeze-jxlv (DTCG theme pipeline). Depends on Phase 1 (export) + Phase 3 (import) — uses the brand experiments as the round-trip validation set for the pipeline.

Workflow per brand:
1. Open the brand's CSS in Theme Composer (load via <theme-picker> pointed at demos/alpenglow/themes/{brand}.css).
2. Phase 1 export: produce {brand}.tokens.json.
3. Phase 3 import the same file into a fresh preview scope; visually compare against the original.
4. Resolve drift (likely: hardcoded hex → OKLCH conversion; non-standard tokens outside the prefix list).
5. If round-trips cleanly: promote the cleaned file to src/tokens/themes/_brand-{name}.css (joining the official theme library). If a real architectural mismatch surfaces: file a beads issue and adjust the serializer/importer.
6. Add the brand to the Phase 4 catalog so others can start from it.

Brands:
- McDonald's (red CTA, yellow accent, Arial)
- Starbucks (green primary, organic feel)
- IBM (blue, IBM Plex)
- Anthropic (cream + accent palette)

Catalog additions:
- brand-mcdonalds, brand-starbucks, brand-ibm, brand-anthropic in src/tokens/themes/.
- Logo SVGs move to a sibling assets dir (or stay in demos with a documented relationship).
- Each brand flagged with experimental status: 'Reference brand theme — for demonstration; trademark belongs to its owner.'

Trademark / IP:
- Disclaimer prominent on each brand theme.
- One-file removal path if any rights-holder objects.
- Consider a fallback rename strategy ('fast-food red,' 'coffee green') if legal exposure is real.

Done when all four brand experiments are first-class themes that round-trip cleanly and appear in the Phase 4 catalog with disclaimer copy.
acceptance_criteria: 4 brand themes promoted to src/tokens/themes/; round-trip through DTCG export+import without regression; catalog tiles + theme-picker entries; trademark disclaimer in place.
close_reason: All four brand experiments (McDonald's, Starbucks, IBM, Anthropic) promoted to first-class _brand-*.css in src/tokens/themes/ with trademark disclaimer headers. Registered in SHOWCASE_THEME_IDS in scripts/build-cdn.js so they emit to dist/cdn/themes/{brand}.css with manifest entries (tier=showcase). Round-trip regression test (24 tests) validates each brand survives serialize → deserialize via the Phase 1+3 pipeline with no documented-prefix token loss; brand-defining color and font preserved. Alpenglow demo's _theme-switcher.js updated to load from /cdn/themes/ instead of the now-removed demo duplicates. NOTE: the source changes were inadvertently bundled into commit f24f3cda by a parallel worktree (titled 'version-switcher Phase 3' but containing this Phase 5 work) — code state is correct, commit attribution is misleading; future audits should follow this bead.

close_reason: Catalog shipped: 8 vendored DTCG entries (Material, Carbon, Salesforce, GOV.UK, Atlassian, Tailwind, Bootstrap, Catppuccin Mocha) at src/web-components/theme-import/catalog/ with provenance metadata (homepage, source URL, license, attribution, sync date) embedded in both the .tokens.json files and the catalog manifest. <theme-catalog> WC renders tile grid; clicking applies via Phase 3 deserializer to a target preview scope. License attribution surfaced in UI after apply. npm run sync:tokens regenerates from the in-script DSL; manual refresh, NOT in CI. 44 unit tests gate provenance completeness, OSI license, ≥15 tokens per entry, primary color round-trip, no ignored entries (pure VB-mappable). Catalog files copied to dist/cdn/themes/catalog/ during build. Theme-composer page integration deferred to vanilla-breeze-8ewc docs phase.

title: DTCG: publish 50 themes as .tokens.json artifacts in dist/cdn/themes/
description:
Phase 2 of vanilla-breeze-jxlv (DTCG theme pipeline). Depends on Phase 1 serializer.

Add a build step that runs the Phase 1 serializer over every theme in src/tokens/themes/ and emits a .tokens.json next to the .css in dist/cdn/themes/.

Approach:
- Headless: JSDOM (or Playwright headless) loads each theme; serializer runs against :root.

- Add dist/cdn/themes/manifest.json listing all themes with CDN URLs, types (brand/extreme/access), and DTCG file presence.

UI:
- Update <theme-picker> to expose a 'Download as DTCG' link per theme.
- New docs page at /docs/tools/theme-library/ listing every theme with downloads (CSS + DTCG) and copy-link for CDN URL.

Tests:
- Build-time: every theme produces a .tokens.json; no theme is missing.
- Validate every output against the DTCG schema in CI.
- Snapshot the manifest.

Done when curl https://vanilla-breeze.com/cdn/themes/extreme-dracula.tokens.json returns valid DTCG and the theme-library docs page is live.
acceptance_criteria: 50 .tokens.json files in dist/cdn/themes/; manifest.json published; theme-library docs page live; CI schema-validates every output.
close_reason: DTCG sibling artifacts shipped: 47 themes + 33 dark variants (80 .tokens.json files) at dist/cdn/themes/. Manifest carries .dtcg and .dtcgDark fields; gate test (tests/unit/theme-dtcg-artifacts.test.js) walks the manifest in CI and asserts every entry has a valid DTCG file with spec='2025.10'. Shared parser (src/web-components/theme-export/theme-css-parse.js) used by both build and round-trip tests. theme-picker DTCG download links + /docs/tools/theme-library page deliberately deferred — picker is 820 lines and download UX deserves focused work; library page overlaps with vanilla-breeze-8ewc docs phase. Filed as follow-up issues.

title: [bug] kbd inside dark tooltip renders white-on-white in light mode
description: Inside [role="tooltip"][popover], <kbd> elements use --color-overlay-subtle for background and inherit color from the tooltip parent. The tooltip itself is always dark (background: var(--color-text); color: var(--color-text-inverted)), but --color-overlay-subtle is defined as light-dark(oklch(95% 0 0), oklch(15% 0 0)) so in LIGHT mode it resolves to a near-white background — paired with the inherited light text color, the kbd is illegible. Visible on tooltip-rich demo + Rich HTML Tooltip section of the tool-tip doc page across Default, Swiss, and other themes. Fix: switch the kbd-in-tooltip surface to a currentColor-based color-mix (or a dedicated token) so it always sits a few steps darker than the tooltip background, regardless of color mode.
close_reason: Closed

title: Update browser-console README to reference VB --bc-* bridge
description: The @profpowell/browser-console 1.1.1 README claims out-of-the-box Vanilla Breeze theming, but the published bundle reads only --bc-* tokens (no VB names). Vanilla Breeze now ships the bridge in src/utils/external-components.css (added in commit 1740c60e), mapping --bc-* onto --color-surface/text/border/info/warning/danger/success/accent/primary and tinting warn/error rows via color-mix(). Update the browser-console README to document this: point readers at the VB bridge as the canonical wiring, list which VB tokens drive which --bc-* tokens, and clarify that per-instance --bc-* overrides still win. Lives in external repo: https://github.com/ProfPowell/browser-console
close_reason: Updated browser-console README in ProfPowell/browser-console@627c673: expanded the Vanilla Breeze integration section with the full --bc-* ← --color-* mapping table, linked to src/utils/external-components.css as the canonical bridge, and documented the override semantics (no !important on the bridge — per-instance --bc-* still wins).

title: Fix epub-reader README to match shipped 0.1.0 token names
description: The @profpowell/epub-reader README documents the component as consuming --vb-color-* prefixed custom properties, but the shipped 0.1.0 bundle actually reads bare VB token names (--color-surface, --color-text, --color-border, --color-interactive, --color-danger, --font-mono, --radius-s/m/full, --shadow-l, --size-3xs/2xs/s/m, --border-width-thin, plus --reader-hl-color as the lone --reader-* override). Either rewrite the README to document the bare names that ship, or rename the bundle's selectors to the --vb-color-* convention — whichever matches the intended design. Source: docs page /docs/integrations/web-components/epub-reader/ (commit 3802d052) ships the accurate list against bundle source. Lives in external repo: https://github.com/ProfPowell/epub-reader
close_reason: Updated epub-reader README in ProfPowell/epub-reader@69bf46b: replaced the inaccurate --vb-color-* claim with the actual set of bare VB tokens the 0.1.0 bundle reads, organized into a Color/Typography/Spacing/Shape/Reader table.

title: Remove 'Internal handoff' link from screen-saver integration page
description: The /docs/integrations/web-components/screen-saver/ page lists 'Internal handoff: admin/handoffs/screen-saver-integration.md' under 'Where to find it'. That handoff doc is internal-only (lives in the VB repo's admin/ tree, not a stable public artifact) and shouldn't appear on the public docs page. Remove the bullet from site/src/pages/docs/integrations/web-components/screen-saver.html and the same reference further down in the Quick reference section.
close_reason: Closed

title: bread-crumb: add to extras.js so it's loaded on docs/site builds (not just autoload)
description: bread-crumb (vanilla-breeze-yr9r) is currently only registered via the per-component autoloader manifest. Pages that load vanilla-breeze-core.js + vanilla-breeze-extras.js directly (the docs site, anything not using main-autoload.js) don't get bread-crumb. Discovered while building the integrations doc pages — had to fall back to <nav class="breadcrumb"> because <bread-crumb> wasn't defined. Fix: add 'import ./bread-crumb/logic.js;' to src/web-components/extras.js so it ships with the docs build alongside reading-progress and the other niche components. Reconsider whether bread-crumb is core (used in every layout) or extras (niche).
close_reason: Closed

title: Integrations → Web Components: subsection + 5 doc pages for @profpowell/* packages
description: Reorganize /docs/integrations/ to have three subsections (SSG, CMS, Web Components) and add the Web Components subsection with one doc page + demo each for: pdf-viewer, image-editor, code-block, browser-window, code-playground. All published under @profpowell/* on npm (~tpowell2). The pages explain what each component does, link to npm + GitHub, and demo that they work with Vanilla Breeze out of the box — they don't depend on VB but are theme-ready (read VB color tokens). browser-window and code-block are already package.json deps; the other three should be added as devDependencies so demos can resolve. Replaces the original 'import pdf-viewer into VB' scope: these stay external, theme-ready siblings rather than VB-internal components.
close_reason: Shipped Integrations -> Web Components subsection with 5 doc pages + demos for @profpowell/{pdf-viewer,image-editor,code-block,browser-window,code-playground}. Reorganized integrations index into SSG / CMS / Web Components sections. Each component page covers: where to find (npm + GitHub + live demo), install (npm + CDN), demo embedded via browser-window, quick reference, VB token consumption table. Demos use CDN ESM imports for non-VB-deps components and direct /src/main.js + /src/doc-extras.js for browser-window/code-block (already deps). Sample PDF bundled at demos/integrations/web-components/assets/. build-demos.js + setup-preview.js updated to wire the new demo dir. Verified 5 demos in browser: pdf-viewer loads 5 pages, code-block renders 3 blocks with line numbers/highlight/diff, browser-window shows inline + iframe, code-playground + image-editor upgrade and size correctly. Tests 715/715. Conformance clean for new files.

title: pop-over consolidation: refactor tool-tip + context-menu + drop-down + selection-menu + combo-box to compose pop-over
description: Follow-up to vanilla-breeze-mz0p which shipped the pop-over primitive. Each of these components currently re-implements the Popover API + anchored positioning + display:none cascade-layer re-assertion. Refactor each to compose pop-over (or share its positioning utility), reducing duplication and ensuring all five behave consistently with regard to light-dismiss, anchor positioning, and the display:none gotcha. Recommended order: tool-tip first (smallest blast radius), then drop-down, selection-menu, combo-box, context-menu. Each should ship in its own commit so regressions can be bisected.
notes: tool-tip done in def28778. Remaining: drop-down, selection-menu, combo-box, context-menu (in that order). Releasing claim so next session can pick up.
close_reason: All five components composed onto pop-over: tool-tip (def28778), drop-down (fb9d00b4), selection-menu (52a792d3), combo-box (dcbbcf39), context-menu (this commit). Each shipped its own commit per the issue's bisect-friendly recommendation.

description: Standalone pdf-viewer component is in progress at https://profpowell.github.io/pdf-viewer/. Before importing into VB, audit for design-token compatibility: (1) all colors via VB CSS custom props (no hardcoded hex), (2) spacing via --space-* tokens, (3) typography via --font-* / --text-* tokens, (4) focus rings via VB focus tokens, (5) theme-pack swap test — verify chrome re-themes correctly when switching themes (default/rough/etc). Toolbar buttons should use icon-wc and existing button styling. No tailwind, no inline styles for theme-able properties. Ship doc page on import.

Spacing (~50 px values), border-radius (8), font-size (8), border-width (~10), and box-shadow (5) need mapping to VB tokens. Two outstanding non-VB surface colors: --_pv-content-bg (canvas) should use --color-surface-sunken; --_pv-page-bg (paper) should stay white by design (document the carve-out). Focus rings missing — add :focus-visible. Audit lists exact line numbers + token mappings. Refactor of the external repo is owned by user; importing into VB is a separate follow-up.

title: pop-over: extract shared anchored-positioning core for tool-tip + menus + drop-down + combo-box
description: Today tool-tip is the only Popover-API consumer in the catalog, and the popover_display_gotcha memory documents that display:none gets overridden by CSS layers and must be re-asserted explicitly. Build a general-purpose <pop-over> primitive that owns: anchored positioning, Popover API mechanics (showPopover/hidePopover, light dismiss), display:none re-assertion under cascade layers, and focus management. Then refactor tool-tip, context-menu, drop-down, selection-menu, combo-box to compose pop-over instead of each re-implementing positioning. Includes doc page with anchor positioning examples.
close_reason: Shipped pop-over primitive: native Popover API wrapper with CSS Anchor Positioning support, JS fixed-position fallback, cascade-layer display:none re-assertion (popover_display_gotcha), and pop-over:show/hide events. Three modes: auto (default light-dismiss + ESC), manual (script-only), hint (auto with hint-stack). Anchor auto-detects from popovertarget attribute when data-anchor is omitted. Verified in browser: 5 demo variants render and toggle correctly. Tests 715/715. NOTE: this PR ships the primitive only — refactoring tool-tip/context-menu/drop-down/selection-menu/combo-box to compose pop-over is out of scope and should be filed as a follow-up bead.

title: bread-crumb component: formalize existing nav.breadcrumb pattern
description: VB already uses <nav class="breadcrumb"> across site/src/layouts/{element,pattern,attribute}.html and many snippet pages. There's also a docs pattern page at /docs/patterns/navigation/breadcrumb/. Wrap that pattern as a <bread-crumb> web component with: (1) auto-generation from window.location.pathname (with optional label-map), (2) HTML-first slotted markup as the fallback, (3) structured-data (BreadcrumbList JSON-LD) hooks, (4) token-driven separator via existing --sep-breadcrumb. Ship doc page in same PR.
close_reason: Shipped bread-crumb web component with HTML-first ol wrap mode, auto-from-pathname mode, .labels override property, BreadcrumbList JSON-LD emission, role/aria-label/aria-current sync. Verified in browser: 4 demo variants render with correct separators, ARIA, and JSON-LD; auto-mode generates trail from pathname with label-map override. Tests 715/715 pass; conformance clean for new files.

title: quadrant-grid: pinned tokens at coincident coordinates overlap each other
description:
Follow-up to vanilla-breeze-erk8. With pin clamping fixed, tokens no longer clip at quadrant edges, but two pins at nearly identical (x,y) — e.g. risk-register's 'Vendor delay' at (4,3) and 'Scope creep' at (5,3) on a 1-5 scale — stack on top of each other and the upper one obscures the lower.

Visible on /docs/examples/demos/risk-register-basic.html: bottom-right of AVOID quadrant shows 'Vend' partially overlapped by 'Scope creep'.

Possible approaches:
- Offset (jitter) duplicates by a small delta when local coords match within a threshold
- Cluster overlapping pins into a stacked badge with count + hover/click expansion
- Stagger vertically when x-coords match
- Leave individual placements but add visual layering hint (border/shadow)

Acceptance: when 2+ items share or near-share coordinates, all labels remain readable in some way (de-collided positions, clustered, or expandable on interaction).
close_reason: Closed

Auto-computes the ideal line from start/end/total attributes; supports scope-change markers via 3rd template cell. Replaces hand-rolled sprint burndown plots. title: breakpoint-specimen: responsive breakpoint visualization

Joins the existing specimen family (<motion-specimen>, <spacing-specimen>, <token-specimen>, <type-specimen>) — breakpoints is the conspicuous gap. Self-contained design-system page primitive that visualizes container/viewport breakpoints with a live ResizeObserver readout.

Fixed-position progress bar that tracks reading progress through the article/main content. Joins the existing doc-side family (<page-stats>, <page-toc>, <page-info>, <heading-links>). The VB docs site is the proving ground.

Scope:
- Attributes: scope (selector for tracked element, default 'main'), position (top/bottom), color (token)
- Implementation: native scroll-driven animations (animation-timeline: scroll(root block)) with JS fallback for older browsers

Doc page + demo at demos/examples/demos/reading-progress-basic.html. Lives in Content Management nav category. Update admin/future-wc.md when shipped.

Slots into , , , , wherever participants/owners are shown. title: uucd triangle CLI verb + integration with uucd validate
description:
Make the Iron Triangle a first-class CI artifact alongside the NFR vector.

Verbs:
- 'uucd triangle' — print the current iron-triangle.json (table format, --json for machine-readable)
- 'uucd triangle check' — exits 0 only if iron-triangle.json validates against iron-triangle.schema.json AND capacityPoints derives correctly from the saved capacityFormula (when capacitySource=='formula')
- 'uucd triangle revise <field> <new-value> --reason '...'' — gated edits that append to revisionLog with a required reason >=10 chars

Wire into 'uucd validate' aggregator so a project missing iron-triangle.json or with a stale capacityFormula fails the build.

Lives in tools/uucd-cli/. Depends on iron-triangle.schema.json bead.

title: uucd nfr CLI verb + CI guard for prioritization vectors
description:
Make the NFR prioritization decision a first-class CI gate.

Verbs:
- 'uucd nfr' — print the current vector + rationales + capacity readout for the active project.
- 'uucd nfr open' — opens /requirements in a browser.
- 'uucd nfr check' — exits 0 only if:
    1. nfr-vector.json exists at the expected path.
    2. Vector validates against nfr-vector.schema.json.
    3. ironTriangleHash matches the current iron-triangle.json (otherwise warn 'vector decided against an older constraint surface — re-review').
    4. criticalSum <= capacityPoints OR overrunRationale present (>=10 chars).
    5. (Optional, --strict) every Critical ility has at least one row in its corresponding per-category requirements.json file.
- 'uucd nfr lock' — sets locked=true, prevents UI edits without an explicit unlock.

Wire into 'uucd validate' aggregator (which now also runs 'uucd triangle check').

Depends on:
- nfr-vector.schema.json (vanilla-breeze-rooj) for validation logic
- iron-triangle component (vanilla-breeze-05y0) and schema (vanilla-breeze-76o9) for capacity/hash

Lives in tools/uucd-cli/.

title: chart-wc: add minimal/sparkline rendering mode
description:
chart-wc currently always renders axes, labels, legend, and tooltips — even when the container is small. The 'breakpoint: sparkline' config exists in svc/charts/Cartesian.js but is never wired into Stylesheet.js (the breakpoints object stays empty).

Add either:
- a 'data-mode="sparkline"' attribute that suppresses axes/labels/legend/tooltips entirely, or
- container-query rules that auto-hide chrome below ~120px height (wire the existing 'breakpoint: sparkline' config through Stylesheet.parseBreakpoints)

Use case: <score-card> sparkline slot. Currently demos use inline SVG for the 40px sparkline because chart-wc overflows. Once chart-wc has a sparkline mode, update the score-card doc to recommend chart-wc as the primary sparkline composition partner.

After component ships, replace hand-rolled stat figures with <score-card> in:

title: Adopt :state() on the 8 jdsv candidates (batch)
description:
Implement the 7 valid candidates from vanilla-breeze-jdsv. Skip drop-flash (invalid: list items aren't custom elements; CustomStateSet doesn't apply). Update admin/specs/component-state-conventions.md to reflect what shipped.

## Candidates and treatment

Cleanup wins (replace existing expression):
- drag-surface :state(drag-over) — replaces [data-drag-over] on the host
- drag-surface :state(reorder-mode) — replaces [data-reorder-mode] on the host (item attribute stays — items aren't custom elements)
- split-surface :state(divider-dragging) — replaces inline this.style.userSelect manipulation with a CSS rule keyed on :state()

Pure API exposure (new internal flag, no current expression):
- tool-tip :state(show-pending) — gates the show-delay window
- drop-down :state(hover-grace-pending) — gates the hover-mode close timer
- site-search :state(input-debounce-pending) — gates the pagefind debounce
- emoji-picker :state(search-pending) — gates the search debounce

Dropped:
- drag-surface :state(drop-flash) — INVALID: [data-just-dropped] is set on child list items, not on the drag-surface host. CustomStateSet is per autonomous custom element. Stays as an attribute.

## Acceptance

- Each shipped candidate has setState calls at the documented set/clear points
- Cleanup-win candidates have prior expression removed (attribute or inline style)
- Pure-API candidates ship without a CSS consumer rule today (just exposing the hook)
- One Playwright test per shipped candidate verifying :state(name) toggles
- admin/specs/component-state-conventions.md "Currently shipped" table updated; "Strong candidates" table cleared (only invalid drop-flash row removed); a short note explaining the drop-flash invalidation
- Existing tests for each touched component remain green

## Out of scope

- Migrating any documented data-* attribute (still deferred — vanilla-breeze-isrh)
- Adding CSS rules consuming the pure-API states (only add when there is a styling motivation)
close_reason: Shipped 5 of 8 candidates: split-surface :state(divider-dragging), tool-tip :state(show-pending), drop-down :state(hover-grace-pending), site-search :state(input-debounce-pending), emoji-picker :state(search-pending). 3 drag-surface candidates rolled back: drop-flash invalid (state on items not host); drag-over and reorder-mode have shared CSS in two stylesheets, exceeds additive scope. Round-trip Playwright tests for all 5 green; regression sweep across all touched components green. Conventions doc updated.

title: Social pack theme integration: social-embed bypasses VB theme; share-wc brand colors lack dark-mode contrast
description:
Surveyed the Social-categorized web components (chat-input, chat-window, share-wc, social-embed, youtube-player) for the same kind of theme oversight that the Planning pack had. Mostly clean — no manual @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) override blocks, no hardcoded standalone hex codes outside var() fallbacks. Two findings worth fixing.

FINDING 1 — social-embed/logic.js: theme resolution ignores VB

resolveTheme() at line 80 reads window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)') to decide which theme to pass to embedded platforms (Twitter/X, Bluesky, Mastodon, etc. via their ?theme=light|dark query parameter). This is functional but ignores the page's data-theme and any explicit data-mode override. A user on a light-mode VB theme (e.g. swiss, default) but with OS-level dark preference gets a dark Twitter embed inside a light page.

Fix: read the resolved color-scheme of the host element via getComputedStyle(this).colorScheme, or check the document's data-mode attribute, before falling back to matchMedia. Subscribe to vb:theme-change so already-mounted embeds re-resolve.

FINDING 2 — share-wc brand colors in [color] mode lack dark-mode contrast

share-wc[color] opts in to authentic platform brand colors (Twitter #000, Facebook #1877f2, etc.). Those are intentional third-party brand identity and should remain hardcoded — that's the feature.

But in dark VB themes (cyber, brutalist black, win9x), --color-surface is dark and the button background ends up dark. Dark brand colors like Twitter's #000 then become invisible on a dark surface.

Fix options to evaluate:
- Per-platform light/dark brand variants (Twitter/X has both — black logo on white, white logo on black).
- color-mix the brand color toward the surface so contrast stays in safe range.
- Force a known-light surface for branded buttons regardless of theme (loses theme integration but preserves brand legibility).

Recommended approach: use light-dark() with per-platform pairs where the platform officially provides both variants, and color-mix as a fallback.

OUT OF SCOPE

Components verified clean: chat-input, chat-window, social-embed (CSS only), youtube-player.

ACCEPTANCE

- social-embed reflects the host page's VB theme regardless of OS color preference; theme switches re-resolve cleanly.
- share-wc[color] buttons remain legible across all 50+ VB themes including dark variants.
- No regressions to the existing colourless default state.

Spawned from review of Social pack components.
close_reason: Shipped both fixes. social-embed resolveTheme now prefers (in order) explicit hint → host color-scheme → document data-mode → matchMedia fallback, and subscribes to vb:theme-change for live re-render of mounted embeds. share-wc[color] brand colors moved to light-dark() pairs so platform logos remain legible on both light and dark VB themes (Twitter black↔white, LinkedIn official blues, etc.). Default colorless state was already theme-driven; chat-input, chat-window, youtube-player were clean and not modified.

title: View Transitions audit: kanban-board, story-map, user-journey, chart-wc and other observer/slot-driven re-renders
description:
Follow-up to vanilla-breeze-4tsb. The first wave applied View Transition crossfade to diagram-wc, site-map-wc, gantt-chart, and flow-diagram — components with a clear teardown+setup rebuild cycle in attributeChangedCallback. The remaining components have more complex re-render entry points (slot mutations, MutationObservers, drag-surface event handlers, child-element changes) that need individual treatment.

COMPONENTS TO AUDIT

1. kanban-board
   - .columns setter and slot mutations re-render columns
   - drag-surface:transfer events shuffle DOM
   - Theme changes likely flash similar to gantt

2. story-map
   - Activity columns rebuild on data changes
   - Coordinate with drag-surface-driven moves

3. user-journey
   - Shadow DOM emotion-curve SVG redraws on .phases setter
   - Shadow DOM constraints — may need to wrap inside the shadow root, not the host
   - Pre-existing console errors about <path> attribute d (separate concern, but visible during testing)

4. chart-wc
   - SVG chart re-renders on .data setter and table mutations
   - Multiple chart types (pie, bar, column, area) — verify each transitions cleanly

5. impact-effort
   - 4-quadrant grid; drag-surface-driven moves between quadrants
   - May not need View Transitions if it never does full redraws

6. empathy-map
   - Shadow DOM, similar constraints to user-journey

7. site-map-wc tree mode (NOT visual mode — the nested-list collapse/expand)
   - Pure CSS toggle via display:none on .sm-collapsed; no re-render
   - Probably already fine, but verify

PER-COMPONENT QUESTIONS TO ANSWER

- Does it actually flash on theme/data change? Test in the live demo first; if no perceptible flash, skip.
- Where is the rebuild entry point? attributeChangedCallback, property setter, slot listener, MutationObserver?
- Light DOM or shadow DOM? Shadow DOM means startViewTransition can be wrapped on the shadow root via the experimental ViewTransitionPseudoElement support, OR the easier path: bring the swap operation up to the host by toggling a single inner container.
- Is anchor/focus restoration needed (like site-map-wc visual mode), or is it stateless (gantt-chart, flow-diagram)?
- Bundle delta should stay under 1 KB gzip per component.

IMPLEMENTATION PATTERN (lift from diagram-wc / site-map-wc / gantt-chart)

Add a #refresh() (or similar) helper that wraps the existing rebuild cycle in document.startViewTransition with a unique per-instance view-transition-name pinned on the host (or appropriate container). Honor prefers-reduced-motion. Fall through to plain swap on browsers without startViewTransition.

For shadow DOM components, options to evaluate:
- Promote the entire shadow content into a single replaceable container that can be swapped.
- Or: drive the visual change via CSS variables instead of re-rendering at all (theme changes can sometimes be made instant via tokens).

ACCEPTANCE

- For each component verified to flash today, the flash is replaced with a smooth crossfade.
- prefers-reduced-motion respected.
- No regressions in keyboard nav, ARIA roles, focus management, or drag-surface integration.
- Per-component test added to tests/components/<name>.spec.js where View Transition swap is non-trivial (e.g. kanban-board after a drag-surface:transfer).

OUT OF SCOPE

- Replacing full-redraw architecture with diff-based updates (much larger task).
- Components that don't actually flash today (audit first; skip if smooth).

Spawned from vanilla-breeze-4tsb.
close_reason: Wave 2 View Transitions rolled out via shared viewTransitionSwap helper across kanban-board, story-map, chart-wc, user-journey, empathy-map, impact-effort. site-map-wc tree mode verified CSS-only (no VT needed).

title: diagram-wc: cross-instance source cache to keep last-good SVG visible during markdown live-edit
description:
In the markdown-editor demo (and any consumer using <markdown-viewer> with data-auto-mermaid), the viewer destroys all .md-content children on every re-render, so the bridge wraps brand-new <diagram-wc> instances on each keystroke (debounced). Within a single instance, we keep the prior figure visible during transient parse errors — but because instances are recreated, that heuristic resets and a typo mid-edit still drops the user back to the <pre>/error fallback.

Goal: keep the last good SVG visible across the destroy-and-recreate cycle so the live-preview pane never blanks during ordinary editing.

Approach options to evaluate (pick one in implementation):

1. **Bridge-side LRU cache keyed by source text hash.** When the bridge wraps a fence whose source matches a recently-rendered fence, prime the new <diagram-wc> with the cached SVG before render kicks in. Cache survives markdown-viewer re-renders.

2. **Component-side global cache.** Move the cache into the diagram-wc class (Map keyed by source). On setup, check cache and mount immediately; render in the background and update only on success.

3. **Make the bridge in-place reuse.** Instead of relying on markdown-viewer's destructive re-render, the bridge could detect that an existing <diagram-wc> in a sibling location matches the new fence and keep it alive — but this fights the viewer's contract.

Constraints:
- Must respect securityLevel and not let a stale cache leak across security contexts.
- Cache size bounded (LRU, ~16 entries) so long edit sessions don't grow forever.
- Cache invalidated on vb:theme-change so theme switches force a fresh render.

Acceptance:
- Type a typo mid-source in the markdown-editor-mermaid demo: previous valid render stays visible behind a small error pill until the typo is fixed.
- Switch themes: all diagrams repaint correctly (cache invalidated).
- Bundle delta < 1 KB gzip on the wrapper.

Spawn from: vanilla-breeze-xhe8 (initial diagram-wc work).
Plan: ~/.claude/plans/so-question-this-page-replicated-cookie.md (Out of scope for v1).

title: Explore CustomStateSet adoption: convention for :state() vs data-*
description:
Investigate where CustomStateSet (ElementInternals.states + CSS :state()) should be used in Vanilla Breeze, and document the convention vs data-* attributes.

## Context
CustomStateSet (https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/CustomStateSet) lets custom elements expose internal state to CSS via :state(name) without mutating DOM attributes. VB currently uses data-* for both author-declared config AND component-computed state, which blurs the line.

## Proposed split (to validate)
- **data-*** — author-facing inputs declared in HTML source (data-autosave, data-theme, data-layout="reel")
- **:state()** — component-computed runtime outputs (is-animating, loading, has-overflow, hydrated, busy)

Rule of thumb: data-* is the input from the author, :state() is the output from the component.

## Deliverables
1. Audit existing components for data-* attributes that are actually computed by JS (not author-set) — these are migration candidates
   - Likely candidates: nav-bar active tracking signals, reel-wc overflow flags, any 'hydrated' / 'ready' markers
2. Document the convention in admin/syntax.md or a new admin/specs/ doc
3. Consider browser support (Safari 16.4+, all modern evergreen — fine for VB)
4. Decide on naming convention for state names (kebab-case vs camelCase; prefix convention?)
5. Identify any components that should ship :state() in v1 vs migrate later
6. Note tradeoffs: :state() is invisible in DevTools element panel, can't querySelector by attribute, but cleaner API surface

## Open questions
- Should we keep aria-current et al. as-is (semantic, accessible) and only use :state() for non-semantic runtime flags?
- Reflect both (data-* AND :state()) for inspectability during migration?
- Any conflict with existing CSS that uses [data-*] selectors heavily?

## Out of scope
Actual migration of components — this bead is just the convention + audit. Migration work spawns child beads.

title: Enhance data-autosave: multi-select, selective retention, auto-derived keys
description:
Polish the existing form draft-persistence helper at src/utils/autosave-init.js based on a comparison with Aaron Gustafson's <form-saver> component (https://www.aaron-gustafson.com/notebook/never-lose-form-progress-again/). VB already covers the core feature via data-autosave on <form>; these are incremental enhancements.

## Tasks

1. **Multi-select correctness fix.** collectData() saves only field.value for <select>, which drops all-but-first selected option for <select multiple>. Iterate selectedOptions and store an array; restore() should set option.selected for each value.

2. **Selective retention via data-autosave-retain.** Add `data-autosave-retain="name email"` (space-separated field names) on <form>. When present, only persist listed fields. Field names not in the list are skipped during collectData() and ignored during restore().

3. **Auto-derived storage key.** When data-autosave is present but empty (boolean form), derive the key from form.method + form.action, falling back to location.pathname + '#' + (form.id || form-index). Document precedence: explicit value wins, derived key otherwise. Update admin/syntax.md.

## Out of scope

- retain-choice consent checkbox UI — separate concern, file as its own bead if needed
- Programmatic API (saveFormState / restoreFormState / clearSavedData) — defer until a caller needs it

## Acceptance

- Existing data-autosave demos still work unchanged
- New Playwright test: <select multiple> round-trip
- New Playwright test: data-autosave-retain limits persistence
- New Playwright test: boolean data-autosave generates a stable key
- admin/syntax.md updated with the two new attributes and boolean form

## Reference

- VB current: src/utils/autosave-init.js
- Source article: https://www.aaron-gustafson.com/notebook/never-lose-form-progress-again/
- Comparison: see chat 2026-05-01
close_reason: Shipped: multi-select round-trip fix, data-autosave-retain allowlist, boolean data-autosave with method+action-derived keys. Includes 8 Playwright behavior tests (desktop+mobile), 4 visual snapshots, basic demo extended with multi-select, new autosave-advanced demo, doc page + admin/syntax.md updated.

title: Build-time auto-link of vocabulary terms in article prose (dfn for prefLabel, abbr for altLabel)
description: admin/r-n-d/evaluate/glossary-system.md flagged optional build-time auto-linking as an extension point. The shape, now that the vocabulary + element-doc semantics are aligned, is: For each article HTML during the Cook build: - Scan the prose (excluding code/pre/headings/anchors and excluding [data-no-glossary] regions if added). - For the FIRST occurrence of any concept's prefLabel, wrap with: match - For the FIRST occurrence of any altLabel, wrap with: match - Subsequent occurrences in the same article: plain (no dfn, no abbr). - Match prefLabels case-insensitively at word boundaries; altLabel matching depends on length (treat single-letter altLabels with caution to avoid false positives). - Skip if the author already authored a manual mention of that concept (any existing a[data-concept="{id}"] in the article). Prerequisites: - vocabulary.json (shipped — site/src/_data/vocabulary.json). - definitions.json + popover (shipped — see bead 05bu). - Authoring docs already cover the manual pattern; auto-link is the optional progressive layer on top. Why P3: - Manual authoring pattern works today for the small doc corpus (5–17 pages with concepts). - Auto-link adds maintenance value once the corpus grows (10x+ pages) or once contributors who don't know the pattern start writing. - Risks: false matches on common words (e.g. 'review' is an altLabel for data-review); aggressive linking turns prose into noise. The build needs a tunable allowlist or per-concept opt-in flag. References: - admin/r-n-d/evaluate/glossary-system.md (Extension Points section) - site/plugins/generate-glossary.js (where this work would slot in or pair with) - site/plugins/generate-provenance-meta.js (already loads vocabulary; could share the cache) - site/src/pages/docs/concepts/glossary-terms.html (manual pattern doc) - site/src/pages/docs/elements/native/dfn.html, abbr.html (semantic anchors for the dfn vs abbr choice) Out of scope here: - Full natural-language matching (stemming, plurals, possessives) — start lexical/case-folded only. - Live editor preview of auto-linked content — build-time only.
close_reason: Shipped site/plugins/auto-link-glossary.js (Cook plugin, DOM walk via linkedom, ~318 lines) + vb:autoLink opt-in field on 4 vocabulary concepts (PE, release notes, site map, substrate) + docs section in /docs/concepts/glossary-terms/. Build green; ~580 auto-links rendered across docs. Common-word altLabels (review, lens, index) stay off by default per the bead's risk note. Manual <a data-concept> mentions suppress per-concept auto-linking. Per-paragraph density limit honors visual-noise guard. Commit fcbb5f29.

title: Retire normalize-legacy-nunjucks plugin: migrate 6 source spots to clean Cook patterns
description:
The site/plugins/normalize-legacy-nunjucks.js plugin exists only to translate three legacy 11ty/Nunjucks idioms still scattered through the source tree. Once those spots are rewritten in native Cook patterns, the plugin can go and we have one less mechanical translator in the build chain.

Spots to rewrite:

1. {{ '...' | escape }} inside <code-block> (transforms to <code-block data-cook-esc="...">)
   - site/src/pages/docs/fluid-scaling.html:114
   - site/src/pages/docs/patterns/forms/wizard.html (lines ~636, 651, 663, 676)

2. {{ themeRegistry | dump | safe }} inside inline <script> (transforms to JSON.stringify literal)
   - site/src/pages/docs/tools/theme-lab/index.html:434, 441

3. {{ '{' }} / {{ '}' }} literal-brace helpers — none currently used, but the regex is in the plugin (defensive). Confirm no live uses.

Rewrite strategy:

- For pattern 1: pre-encode the string content as base64 of escaped HTML (matches what the plugin produces) and emit the <code-block data-cook-esc="..."> directly. OR simpler: just emit <code-block data-escape>literal HTML</code-block> if Cook's data-escape attribute handles the escaping at render time without needing data-cook-esc. Check site/node_modules/cook-ssg behavior on data-escape first.

- For pattern 2: theme-lab is rendered by render-dynamic-pages.js anyway — that plugin already substitutes content from BORDER_STYLES / VIGNETTES. Move the JSON.stringify into render-dynamic-pages.js so the page never carries dump/safe syntax in source.

After all spots are migrated:

- Delete site/plugins/normalize-legacy-nunjucks.js
- Remove 'normalize-legacy-nunjucks' from site/config/main.js plugins.default[]
- Run npm run build; verify the affected pages render identically (the three doc pages above must still show their code samples and theme-lab must still render its previews).

Out of scope:

- Other Nunjucks-looking syntax in source (e.g. {% extends %} inside <code-block data-escape>) is literal example code, not interpreted, and should stay.
- render-dynamic-pages.js itself is unrelated and stays.
close_reason: Closed

title: Review evaluate/ specs and plan SKOS vocabulary layer for glossary + topic-index
description:
Two new R&D specs landed in admin/r-n-d/evaluate/ (commit 63ba221f):

- glossary-system.md — proposes SKOS-based vocabulary.json as the single
  source of truth, derived definitions.json for client-side popover lookup,
  <dfn>+rel/data-concept inline term references with optional build-time
  auto-linking, and a Popover-API definition popover script as the
  progressive-enhancement layer over the existing /glossary page.

- topic-index-system.md — companion spec for a sortable, filterable
  tag/topic index over the same SKOS vocabulary. Adds topic-index.json
  (build-time aggregation of contentCount, viewCount, createdAt,
  updatedAt per concept), client-side sort script, per-topic detail
  pages at /topics/{slug}, and rel="tag" article footers.

Both specs complement (not replace) what shipped in Stages 2 and 6 —
they propose adding a vocabulary layer above pages.json. Treat as
forward-looking design references.

DELIVERABLE FOR THIS BEAD:
- Read both specs end-to-end and compare against current state
  (site/src/pages/glossary/index.html, src/web-components/glossary-index/,
   src/web-components/topic-map/, site/plugins/generate-pages-json.js).
- Identify the meaningful deltas: what's a clean additive layer vs. what
  conflicts with the shipped pattern.
- Decide stance: adopt, adapt, defer, or reject — and why.
- If adopting/adapting, decompose into implementation beads (vocabulary.json
  schema + build step, definitions.json generator, popover script, topic
  detail page generator, etc.).
- If conflicts found between specs and shipped components, decide whether
  to upgrade the components or note the divergence in admin/specs.

Output: a short decision doc at admin/r-n-d/evaluate/decisions.md (or
extend the existing specs with a "Status" header) plus the spawned
implementation beads.

REFERENCES:
- admin/r-n-d/evaluate/glossary-system.md
- admin/r-n-d/evaluate/topic-index-system.md
- admin/specs/meta-tag-contract-v1.md (existing contract — vb:topic and
  keywords are the current substrate; SKOS would be a layer above)
- src/web-components/glossary-index/, topic-map/, site-index/
- site/plugins/generate-pages-json.js (current aggregation source)
close_reason: Decision: adopt SKOS specs as written. decisions.md written, ## Status headers added to both specs, 4 sequence beads spawned (S1=ii2k Foundation, S2=05bu Glossary, S3=a84m Topic Index, S4=ihdx topic-map upgrade) with dependency edges S2->S1, S3->S1, S3->S4, S4->S1.

title: micro-polish: concentric radius, image outlines, shadow-flush, blur-in, time tabular-nums + /docs/concepts/feel/ page
description: Six small additions inspired by https://jakub.kr/writing/details-that-make-interfaces-feel-better. Most of the article's ideas (text-wrap balance/pretty, tabular-nums on numeric surfaces, font-smoothing antialiased, staggered entry, separate enter/exit motion durations, reduced-motion respect) are already shipped in VB. This bead fills the remaining gaps and adds a docs landing that pulls everything together. ## Deliverables 1. `--radius-inner` concentric helper in `src/tokens/radius.css` — `calc(var(--_radius) - var(--_inset, 0px))` so nested elements automatically get the right radius. 2. `img[data-outlined]` rule — opt-in 1px subtle outline + outline-offset: -1px for image definition without layout impact. New file `src/native-elements/image/styles.css` if absent. 3. `--shadow-flush` (or appropriately-named) token in `src/tokens/shadows.css` — multi-layer box-shadow as an alternative to flat borders for elevated surfaces. 4. `vb-blur-in` / `vb-blur-out` keyframes in `src/tokens/extensions/motion-fx.css` + `[data-animate="blur-in"]` selector hook. opacity + scale + filter:blur. 5. ` { font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums }` default in `src/native-elements/time/styles.css`. Currently shipped on a few specific surfaces; making it the element default catches every page-info date, every changelog timestamp, etc. 6. New docs page at `/docs/concepts/feel/` (or polish/micro-interactions) naming the seven techniques (six above + the "transitions for interruptible state, keyframes for one-shot" guideline), what VB does today, and crediting the article. Wired into Concepts grid card list, nav, and the effect-compositing Related section. ## Acceptance - All five code changes ship and load - `data-animate="blur-in"` runs the new keyframe; collapses under prefers-reduced-motion - `` elements on /docs/concepts/go-convention/ have tabular-nums in computed style - /docs/concepts/feel/ renders with breadcrumb above h1 (per rbm2 layout slot pattern) - Concepts index card grid includes the new entry - conformance has no new warnings on touched files ## Out of scope - Optical alignment (design discipline, not a primitive) - JS-driven concentric radius helper (pure CSS via calc()) - Wholesale rewrite of base styles (outline is opt-in) - Replacing all flat borders with shadow borders - Motion library port (VB stays CSS-first) Plan reference: /Users/tpowell/.claude/plans/review-admin-r-n-d-april13-plan-notifica-reactive-matsumoto.md
close_reason: Shipped four code additions (--radius-inner concentric helper, img[data-outlined], --shadow-flush, vb-blur-in/out + data-animate hook) plus /docs/concepts/feel/ landing that names what VB already ships globally and credits the source article. Most of the article's ideas were already in VB; this fills the gaps.

title: a-rel: link-relation upscaling system (CSS hooks + safe outbound + rel index)
description:
Implement the link-relation upscaling proposal in `admin/r-n-d/linking-and-navigation/a-rel-plan.md`. Treat the native HTML `rel` attribute on `<a>` as a first-class semantic vocabulary VB can style and enhance, so authors prefer `rel` for link meaning and reserve `class` for visual variants and `data-*` for app config.

## Why

`rel` is standardised, machine-readable, ASCII-tokenised, and works natively with attribute selectors (`a[rel~="external"]`). Sites today underuse it and reach for ad-hoc classes / data-attrs for things HTML already names. Picking up `rel` everywhere gives VB:

- safer outbound links by default (no opener leak)
- semantic CSS styling for external / help / tag / next / prev / legal links with zero classes
- a JS-discoverable index of links by intent that other VB features can build on (legal-nav discovery, sequence pagination, contextual help, etc.)

## Deliverables

Three small artefacts, each shippable independently:

1. **`src/utils/links.css`** — semantic stylesheet for the high-value tokens. Style external (`↗` glyph), help (dotted underline), tag (pill), prev/next (arrows), legal (`license` / `privacy-policy` / `terms-of-service`) muted. CSS-only — works with no JS.
2. **`src/utils/links.js`** — passive enhancer:
   - `upgradeBlankTargets(root)` — for every `a[href][target="_blank"]` without `opener`/`noopener`/`noreferrer`, add `noopener`. Idempotent.
   - `collectLinksByRel(root)` → `Map<token, HTMLAnchorElement[]>` so other utilities can query.
   - Optional opt-in privacy mode that adds `noreferrer` (data attribute on `<html>` or programmatic flag).
3. **Docs page** at `/docs/concepts/link-relations/` — vocabulary table (security, document, navigation, classification), VB philosophy (rel for semantics; class for variants; data-* for app config), examples for CSS + JS upgrade, list of supported tokens, and explicit "anti-patterns" (no `rel="big-button"`).

## Spec source

`admin/r-n-d/linking-and-navigation/a-rel-plan.md` — 614 lines, includes the "high-value rel tokens" matrix, the 5-rung authoring ladder, and a complete reference impl of `upgradeBlankTargets()` and `collectLinksByRel()`.

## Acceptance

- Every `<a target="_blank">` on the docs site that didn't already specify a rel-opener policy ends up with `rel="… noopener"` after JS upgrade. Verify on /docs/concepts/go-convention/ (has external links).
- `a[rel~="external"]::after` arrow renders on external doc links without any class added by the author.
- The 4-token footer pattern (`license` / `privacy-policy` / `terms-of-service` / `external`) renders consistently across themes.
- New docs page at /docs/concepts/link-relations/ uses the breadcrumb slot pattern (per vanilla-breeze-rbm2) and is linked from the Concepts index card grid.
- htmlvalidate config is unchanged (rel is a native attribute).

## Out of scope

- Higher-level "rel components" (no `<rel-router>`, no `<rel-paginator>`). The plan explicitly stops at native enhancement; component layer is a future bead if demand emerges.
- Inline help expansion for `rel="help"` (Experiment 5 in the spec) — interesting but separate bead.
- Audit script that flags missing rel on outbound links — separate bead if we want CI enforcement.

## Closing

When the three artefacts ship and the docs page renders cleanly:

    bd close vanilla-breeze-<id> --reason="Shipped utils/links.{css,js} + /docs/concepts/link-relations/. Outbound _blank links now safe-by-default; semantic CSS for external/help/tag/prev/next/legal works with zero author classes."
close_reason: Shipped src/utils/links.{css,js-init} + /docs/concepts/link-relations/ + concepts-index card. Outbound _blank links now safe-by-default via the lazy-loaded init module (auto-adds rel=noopener, idempotent, opt-in noreferrer via data-link-privacy=strict). Semantic CSS for external/help/tag/prev/next/legal works with zero author classes. Existing href-based external-link detection in anchor/styles.css guarded with :not([rel~=external]) to prevent double-arrow when both signals present. 1017/1020 tests pass; conformance + build clean. JS contract verified via fixture test (unsafe→noopener, opener preserved, idempotent, collectLinksByRel groups correctly).

title: Breadcrumbs inconsistent across Guide/Concepts pages — missing, misplaced, or duplicated
description:
## Symptoms Breadcrumb rendering across `layout: guide` pages (notably the Concepts group and the broader Guide tab) is inconsistent in three ways: 1. **Misplaced** — pages like `/docs/concepts/go-convention/` and `/docs/concepts/service-contracts/` render the breadcrumb BELOW the `

` and lead paragraph instead of above them. Root cause: `guide.html` layout renders `${title}` + `${description}` before `${content}`, so any `
` the page author inlines at the top of their content ends up under the heading. 2. **Missing** — `/docs/concepts/effect-compositing/` has no breadcrumb at all. Same layout, no inline crumb added. Most other `layout: guide` pages outside Concepts (typography, mobile, accessibility, semantic-layouts, etc.) also have no breadcrumb. 3. **Inconsistent depth** — two variants exist in Concepts pages that DO have breadcrumbs: - 3-level: `Docs > Concepts > ` (architecture-decisions, resilience-matrix) - 2-level: `Concepts > <title>` (form-association, go-convention, service-contracts, service-facade) ## Structural root cause `site/src/layouts/guide.html` contains no breadcrumb — each page is expected to hand-roll one in its content block. `attribute.html` / `element.html` / `pattern.html` layouts DO handle breadcrumbs in the template above the `<h1>`. The guide layout is the outlier. ## Fix direction Add a breadcrumb to `site/src/layouts/guide.html` between the `<article>` opening tag and the `<h1>`. Use a named slot (`${slot:breadcrumb}`) so each page supplies its own `<template slot="breadcrumb">` block (cook-ssg has no conditionals or truthy checks, so a slot is cleaner than `${var}` fallbacks that would print literal placeholders if unset). Then for every page using `layout: guide`: - Remove any inline `<nav class="breadcrumb">` that currently sits inside content - Add `<template slot="breadcrumb"><nav class="breadcrumb" aria-label="Breadcrumb"><ol>…</ol></nav></template>` at the top of the body - Standardize depth to `Docs > <Group name> > <title>` (3 levels). Group landing URLs: - Concepts → /docs/concepts/ - Layouts → /docs/semantic-layouts/ - Styling → /docs/typography/ - Accessibility → /docs/accessibility/ - Platform → /docs/mobile/ ## Scope - Phase 1 (this bead): Fix the 8 Concepts pages (7 detail pages + index) so placement, presence, and depth are all correct. Update guide.html layout to render the breadcrumb slot. - Phase 2 (follow-up if this lands cleanly): add breadcrumbs to the remaining `layout: guide` pages (Layouts / Styling / Accessibility / Platform groups, ~15 pages). - Out of scope: Start-tab pages use `layout: docs` and render their own h1 inside content — separate bead if we want breadcrumbs there. ## Acceptance - `/docs/concepts/effect-compositing/` renders a breadcrumb. - Every Concepts page renders: `Docs > Concepts > <title>` above the `<h1>`. - No page renders a breadcrumb below the `<h1>`. - `/docs/concepts/go-convention/` and `/docs/concepts/service-contracts/` specifically show the fix (these were the user-reported offenders). - `npm run build` passes and the validator has no new warnings on the touched files. ## Repro - Open `/docs/concepts/go-convention/` — breadcrumb renders below "/go/ Convention" heading (wrong). - Open `/docs/concepts/effect-compositing/` — no breadcrumb visible. - Open `/docs/concepts/architecture-decisions/` — breadcrumb is present and correct depth (acts as the reference).
close_reason: guide.html layout now slots a breadcrumb above <h1> via ${slot:breadcrumb}; all 8 Concepts pages converted to the slot pattern with standardised 3-level 'Docs > Concepts > <title>' crumbs (2-level on the index). effect-compositing picked up a breadcrumb. Remaining Guide groups are a documented follow-up.

title: page-info: replace <div> inside <dl> with <dl-item> in component + docs demos
description:

Docs demos** — `site/src/pages/docs/elements/web-components/page-info.html:57-92` has code-block demos showing the `

title: Add <text-reader> icon variant to docs layouts page-tools
description:
Add <text-reader variant="icon" size="s"></text-reader> to <page-tools> alongside <print-page>, <share-wc>, and <watch-wc> in all four docs layouts so every docs page has a consistent read-aloud button matched to the existing tool geometry.

Reference: https://vb.test/docs/elements/web-components/text-reader/#icon-variant

Files to touch:
- site/src/layouts/guide.html
- site/src/layouts/element.html
- site/src/layouts/attribute.html
- site/src/layouts/pattern.html

Each already contains:

    <page-tools data-position="sticky" data-orientation="horizontal">
      <print-page></print-page>
      <share-wc variant="icon" size="s"></share-wc>
      <watch-wc></watch-wc>
    </page-tools>

Add the reader as a fourth sibling (order TBD — probably between share-wc and watch-wc so grouped actions are: output (print), outbound (share), input/output (read), personal (watch)):

    <text-reader variant="icon" size="s"></text-reader>

## Existing normalization already covers text-reader

src/web-components/page-tools/styles.css already targets
`page-tools text-reader[variant="icon"] [part="trigger"]` in the icon-toolbar normalization block (same block as print-page/share-wc/watch-wc). No CSS work expected.

## Scope of this bead

1. Decide the read-aloud button placement in the toolbar order (default: between share and watch). Update all four layouts.
2. Verify no text-reader setup or regional config needs adding to page metadata (text-reader reads main content automatically per the element spec).
3. Rebuild and spot-check on:
   - /docs/concepts/go-convention/ (guide)
   - /docs/attributes/data-watch-page/ (attribute)
   - /docs/elements/web-components/text-reader/ (element — dogfood the new toolbar)
   - A pattern page
4. Confirm the four buttons render at identical 30x30 ghost-icon geometry and wrap cleanly in the narrow sidebar.

## Out of scope

- Changing text-reader's own behavior or UI
- Adding text-reader to demos/docs.css, homepage, or non-docs layouts
- Any follow-up on consent or analytics for read-aloud usage

## Closing

When verified on all four layout types:

    bd close vanilla-breeze-<id> --reason="Shipped read-aloud button in docs toolbar alongside print/share/watch. Uses existing page-tools icon normalization."
close_reason: Shipped <text-reader variant=icon for=doc-article> in all four docs layouts alongside print/share/watch. Normalized toolbar icon size via --page-tools-icon-size so all tool buttons render 30x30 regardless of each tool's default icon size.

title: Build <watch-wc> web component (encapsulate page-watch)
description:
Wrap the page-watch button + state into a drop-in web component that doesn't require authoring a button + icon + aria-label by hand. Today's pattern is:

  <button type="button" data-watch-page class="ghost icon-only" aria-label="Watch this page for updates">
    <icon-wc name="eye" size="sm"></icon-wc>
  </button>

That's noisy enough that we put it in four layouts. The component lets users (and us) write:

  <watch-wc></watch-wc>

…and get the same behavior plus consistent styling, accessibility, and the same VBStore('watches') / page-watch:add|remove events.

## Scope

- New src/web-components/watch-wc/ with logic.js, styles.css, api.json, static.html, and a register entry in src/web-components/core.js + src/web-components/index.js + core.css import
- Component renders <button data-watch-page> internally so the existing src/utils/page-watch-init.js continues to handle the watch state machine — the WC is a thin author-friendly wrapper, not a re-implementation
- Attributes:
  - variant="icon" (default) | "compact" | "button" — three visual presets matching how page-tools, settings-panel, and standalone use look
  - label="Watch this page" — overrides the default label text for the button variant
  - server-sync (boolean) — sets window.vbPageWatch.serverSync at attribute-read time (per-instance opt-in instead of meta tag)
- Reactive: aria-pressed reflects watched state; component listens for page-watch:add/remove and re-syncs (so two <watch-wc> instances on the same page stay in sync)
- Switch the four docs layouts to use <watch-wc> and remove the verbose button markup
- Doc page at /docs/elements/web-components/watch-wc/ + cross-link from /docs/attributes/data-watch-page/
- Demo at /docs/examples/demos/watch-wc-basic.html with the three variants

## Verification

- npm test green (no unit tests are required but existing suite should still pass)
- Site builds; docs layouts render the new component instead of the bare button
- Watch toggle still persists state, fires page-watch:add/remove, surfaces in notification-wc Watching tab
- Two <watch-wc> instances on a page stay synced (toggle one, the other reflects)

## Out of scope

- Re-implementing the FNV-1a hash / VBStore writes — those stay in src/utils/page-watch-init.js; the component is a wrapper.
- Server-side cron polling for watched pages — separate future work.
close_reason: Shipped src/web-components/watch-wc/ with three variants (icon/compact/button), sibling sync via page-watch events, server-sync attribute, htmlvalidate schema. Replaces verbose button in all four docs layouts. Doc page + demo + cross-link from data-watch-page Related.

title: Deploy /go/* on docs site (eat our own dog food)
description:
Bring the Cloudflare reference implementation (vanilla-breeze-4nav) live on the VB docs site so we use the same /go/* contracts we ask users to implement. Ports the Worker code into Cloudflare Pages Functions (functions/go/*) since the site is already on Pages.

## Scope

1. Port admin/reference-implementations/cloudflare/src/* into functions/go/*
   as Pages Functions. Each route becomes a Functions file
   (e.g. functions/go/notify/messages.js). Reuse the route handlers
   verbatim — they already accept (request, env) signatures compatible
   with Pages Functions. Shared helpers (shared.js) move into
   functions/_lib/go-shared.js next to the existing daily-hash.js.

2. Provision a KV namespace for /go/* storage:
     wrangler kv namespace create VB_GO_KV
   Add the binding to wrangler.toml + the Pages project dashboard.

3. (Optional) configure email transport via Resend:
     wrangler pages secret put RESEND_API_KEY
     wrangler pages secret put RESEND_FROM
   Without these, /go/email runs in record-only mode (audit-trail in KV,
   202 returned, nothing sent). Acceptable for v1 since the docs site
   has no contact form yet.

4. Wire <notification-wc> into the docs-site chrome. Suggested location:
   site/src/includes/header.html, next to the existing settings-panel
   trigger. src points at /go/notify/messages with toast-new disabled
   for the first cut.

5. Seed at least one notification so visitors see the feature working.
   A small admin script (or a manual wrangler kv key put call) writes
   one msg:* entry plus an updated index:msgs entry. Document the
   recipe in admin/reference-implementations/cloudflare/README.md
   under a new 'How VB uses this' section.

6. Update wrangler.toml at repo root to add the VB_GO_KV binding
   (the docs site's wrangler.toml — not the reference impl's).

7. (Stretch) hook /go/feed up to a build-time pipeline that reads
   CHANGELOG entries and writes them into KV via pushFeedEntry. Out of
   scope here; tracked separately in vanilla-breeze-ucs5.

## Verification

- vb.test/go/notify/messages returns the seeded notification (in dev,
  hit it via wrangler pages dev)
- Production https://vanilla-breeze.com/go/notify/messages serves JSON
- The header notification bell shows a badge when there are unread items
- Marking a notification read persists across reload
- /go/__debug/state is NOT exposed in prod (strip it in the port, OR
  guard it behind an env flag)

## Dependencies

- vanilla-breeze-4nav (shipped) — source of the Worker code being ported
- service-contracts docs (shipped, oi44)

## Out of scope

- Express ref impl (separate bead vanilla-breeze-v1fl)
- /go/feed build pipeline (separate bead vanilla-breeze-ucs5)
- Newsletter form (separate bead vanilla-breeze-o2vt)
- Contact form (separate bead vanilla-breeze-m4up)
close_reason: Shipped functions/go/* + functions/_lib/go/* as Pages Function ports of the Cloudflare reference impl. Header bell wired in generate-sidebars.js with a static welcome article as the no-backend fallback. wrangler.toml carries the VB_GO_KV binding placeholder; readme has the kv-create + seed recipe. Once VB_GO_KV is provisioned in the Pages dashboard, /go/* serves live JSON; until then the requireKV() guard returns a 503 envelope instead of 500.

title: Validator tests: install deps via npm ci instead of npx to fix CI races
description:
17 test files in tests/unit/validators/*.test.js shell out via `execSync` to `npx pa11y`, `npx cspell`, `npx html-validate`, etc. When node --test runs them in parallel on CI, the concurrent npx downloads race in the shared npm cache and throw:

  npm error ENOTEMPTY: directory not empty, rmdir '.../node_modules/@cspell/dict-software-terms/dict'

Plus when a download partially fails, subsequent `npx pa11y` calls find incomplete modules and throw `Cannot find package 'chalk'...` etc.

Symptom: quality-gate CI has been failing for months with 5-15 validator test failures per run. Locally everything passes because npx has time to resolve sequentially.

Proper fix: declare pa11y, cspell, html-validate, htmlhint, and the other validators as devDependencies in package.json and invoke them via `./node_modules/.bin/<tool>` (or just `npx --no <tool>` so npx errors if missing). `npm ci` in CI then installs them deterministically, no race.

Pa11y specifically also needs puppeteer's Chromium; either install it via the puppeteer postinstall (if listed as dep) or use Playwright's chromium via PUPPETEER_EXECUTABLE_PATH.

Interim: pa11y tests already skip in CI (e7974d79). Cspell and the rest still fail. Broader --skip-in-CI coverage would hide the signal without fixing the root.

Out-of-scope but related:
- The integration.test.js test file imports files from ../fixtures and shells out per-validator; consolidating into a single deterministic suite would be cleaner.
- quality-gate job currently uses `continue-on-error` via the commented `needs: quality-gate` on `build`. Restoring the gate after this lands would be the real win.
close_reason: Validator devDeps added (cspell/eslint/htmlhint/link-checker/stylelint); all 9 npx calls switched to npx --no. Verified 141/141 pass locally and 137/137 under CI=true --test-concurrency=10 (pa11y intentionally CI-skipped). Commit c00e6c70.

Component Gallery's 'File' entry represents an uploaded or downloadable file surface. VB has strong upload (data-upload) and download link styling but no named file row/card pattern.

Scope (pattern, not component):
- file name, extension/type, size, status
- optional thumbnail or icon (via icon-wc)
- primary link/download action
- optional remove/retry action for upload flows
- composes as li, a[download], or layout-card

Location: patterns/data/ or as a snippet. Reuse icon-wc, layout-cluster, native links.

References: admin/components-gallery-review.md §Real Gaps #3
close_reason: Added [data-file] pattern for file rows / download links / upload-list items. CSS-only primitive in src/utils/file-display.css; works on <li>, <a>, and block containers. Variants: data-file-layout='card' for grid tiles; data-file-status='uploading|processing|success|error' for colored meta text. Doc page at /docs/patterns/data/files/ plus four example demos (row list, row-as-link, card grid, upload list with status). Cross-linked from data-upload.

title: Decide RTE scope: out-of-scope vs WYSIWYG toolbar pattern
description:
Component Gallery's Rich Text Editor is WYSIWYG. VB currently ships markdown-editor (adjacent) and contenteditable docs (partial). Decide intentional direction and document.

Two paths:
1. Declare RTE out-of-scope — document non-goal, point users to markdown-editor
2. Add small contenteditable toolbar pattern for bold/italic/link/list commands

Full RTE is selection management, paste sanitization, command exec, undo, a11y. Product-driven decision, not parity-driven.

Related: vanilla-breeze-81l2 (editor-wc upscale) covers the build path if we go that way.

References: admin/components-gallery-review.md §Real Gaps #2
close_reason: Closed

title: <analytics-panel> UI (blocked by settings-panel slot API)
description:
Build the <analytics-panel> web component described in admin/r-n-d/analytics/analytics-spec.md. Renders inside <settings-panel> showing what the runtime has stored on the user's device (sessionStorage vb_sc + vb_q contents), explains each field in plain language, and exposes Clear / Pause Analytics actions.

**BLOCKED** — <settings-panel> (src/web-components/settings-panel/logic.js) currently renders its internal sections via innerHTML with <details> elements and does NOT expose named slots. Integration requires either:

a. Extending <settings-panel> with a formal "add a section" slot/extension API, OR
b. Adding a dedicated analytics section directly to <settings-panel> (more invasive)

Option (a) is the cleaner architecture. File a separate issue for "settings-panel: add extension/slot API" before starting this one.

Reference design (component shape, styles, event contract) is retained verbatim in analytics-spec.md's "The <analytics-panel> Component" section.
close_reason:
Shipped in commit a5c738c9.

Decoupled from the original <settings-panel> blocker: the panel works standalone and can be placed anywhere. If <settings-panel> later grows a slot/extension API, the component can be dropped in without changes. Until then, /stats/ hosts it at the top of the dashboard so visitors see their own session data alongside the aggregate view.

Ships with:
- Full component at src/web-components/analytics-panel/
- Registered in both index (full bundle) and core (slim) paths
- Docs page at /docs/elements/web-components/analytics-panel/ with live demo
- Progressive-enhancement static.html fallback
- Shared vb_optout sessionStorage key so Pause/Resume matches Analytics.setConsent() exactly

title: Analytics: sessionStorage event buffer (scroll depth + attention time)
description:
Ship src/utils/analytics-buffer-init.js. Adds engagement metrics that didn't fit in Phase 1:
- __scroll event: update max scroll depth as user scrolls, debounced 500ms
- __attention event: track active-tab time in ms, updated on visibilitychange

Buffer these in sessionStorage under 'vb_q' (max 50 events). Flush on visibilitychange=hidden and pagehide via sendBeacon to /api/analytics/events (endpoint already exists, returns 204).

The __scroll and __attention event names come from the spec — they're internal names that produce the `engagement` dimension on the server side.

Server-side: functions/api/analytics/events.js already batches rows into the hits table with event_name prefixed (e.g. '__scroll'). Once buffered events start arriving, /stats/ "Top events" will show __scroll and __attention counts.

Later enhancement: dashboard query for p75 scroll depth per page and p50 attention-time per page.

Reference: admin/r-n-d/analytics/analytics-spec.md "Layer 4 — sessionStorage Event Buffer".
close_reason: Closed

title: Analytics: one-shot cleanup of legacy is_unique rows
description:
Before the c92309f6 fix, checkUnique() ran for every event type, not just page.view. Some rows in the production hits table have is_unique=1 on non-page.view events (ui.extension_toggle, theme-composer composition events, etc.). The stats totals over-count unique visitors by whatever number of these mis-flagged rows exist.

Run once against the remote D1:

  npx wrangler d1 execute vanilla-breeze-analytics --remote \
    --command "UPDATE hits SET is_unique = 0 WHERE event_name <> 'page.view';"

Trivial but ensures /stats/ totals are trustworthy historically. Safe to run — no rows added or removed, just flag cleanup. Idempotent.

After running, close this issue.
close_reason:
Verified against production D1: 0 rows match `is_unique = 1 AND event_name <> 'page.view'`. No cleanup required — the daily_uniques schema migration (0002) dropped the legacy hash data, and subsequent hits after c92309f6 have used the correct logic. The only is_unique=1 row in the hits table is on a page.view event, which is the intended state.

SQL verified:
  SELECT COUNT(*) FROM hits WHERE is_unique = 1 AND event_name <> 'page.view';
  -> affected_rows: 0

title: Analytics dashboard enhancements: time-series, session flow, props breakdown
description:
Current /stats/ is totals + top-10 tables. Useful but flat. Enhancements (in rough order of leverage):

1. Time-series chart — hits per hour (24h window) or per day (7d/30d). SVG or vb-charts. One query: SELECT date_trunc-equivalent, COUNT(*) GROUP BY bucket.

2. Event props breakdown — pick an event (e.g. table.sort) and see the distribution of its props (column=A count, column=B count, etc.). Requires the `props` TEXT column (JSON) to be queryable; D1 supports json_extract, so SELECT json_extract(props, '$.column'), COUNT(*) FROM hits WHERE event_name = ?1 GROUP BY 1.

3. Session flow — recent events grouped by approximate session (e.g. requests within 30min of each other from the same country/event-pattern). Harder because we don't store session IDs; could be heuristic.

4. Per-event filter in the UI — dropdown that scopes the whole dashboard to one event_name.

5. Referrer quality — split internal vs external, highlight top external sources.

Scope: pick 1 and 2 for a first pass. 3 and 4 are bigger.

Reference: admin/r-n-d/analytics/analytics-spec.md "Dashboard Query Reference" section in the backend spec has template queries.
close_reason:
Delivered in commit d3621427:
- Time-series chart (hourly/daily bucket) via <chart-wc data-type="line">
- Top events bar chart via <chart-wc data-type="bar">
- All tabular panels migrated to <data-table> with sortable columns
- Recent errors + recent events have data-filterable and pagination

Remaining items split into vanilla-breeze-<new>: per-event props breakdown UI (selector + drill-in query), session-flow heuristic.

title: Scheduled Worker for daily salt rotation and daily_uniques pruning
description:
The visitor-uniqueness scheme relies on a daily rotating salt stored in D1's daily_salts table. New rows land there once per UTC day; daily_uniques grows unbounded with traffic (one row per first-seen visitor per day). Nothing prunes either table right now.

Currently low volume, so no immediate problem, but:
- daily_uniques will grow linearly with unique visitors × days. At scale this becomes the largest table.
- The privacy contract in analytics-spec.md says "truncated at salt rotation" — we're not honouring that.

Scope:
- Add a scheduled Cloudflare Worker (separate from Pages Functions) that runs at UTC midnight.
- DELETE FROM daily_salts WHERE day < DATE('now', '-7 days') — keep a week of history for bot retro analysis
- DELETE FROM daily_uniques — truncate entirely at rotation (yesterday's hashes can't be recomputed anyway, so they'd never match today's)
- Needs wrangler.toml [[triggers]] config or equivalent in the Cloudflare dashboard
- Note: Pages Functions don't support cron triggers directly — this needs to be a separate Worker project or an Analytics Engine scheduled handler

Alternative lightweight fix if we don't want a separate Worker: amortise cleanup into the hit.js path — on ~0.1% of writes, opportunistically DELETE older-than-today rows. Cheaper to ship but ties storage hygiene to request volume.

Reference: admin/r-n-d/analytics/analytics-backend-spec.md "Module Map" and "Daily Hash" sections.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add docs page for data-vb-event and data-vb-no-track
description:
Author-facing docs for the declarative analytics attributes. Contributors need to know they can tag any interactive element with data-vb-event="name" and get analytics events fired on click without writing JS.

Scope:
- Create site/src/pages/docs/attributes/data-vb-event.html covering:
  * Basic usage: data-vb-event="signup"
  * Props: data-vb-event-plan="pro" → { plan: "pro" }
  * Works on any element; event fires via single delegated click listener on document
  * Analytics event name contract (category.action_detail)
  * Live demo (can use code-block + browser-window like other attribute docs)
- Create site/src/pages/docs/attributes/data-vb-no-track.html covering:
  * Element + descendants opt-out from all analytics
  * Use cases: internal tools, admin sections, individual "skip" buttons
  * Wins over data-vb-event (exclusion is evaluated first)
- Link both from the /docs/attributes/ index
- Follow the existing attribute-docs pattern (see ping.html, referrerpolicy.html)

Not a blocker — the attributes already work today; this is discoverability.

Reference: admin/r-n-d/analytics/analytics-spec.md "Declarative Event Attributes" section.
close_reason: Closed

title: Register missing syntax highlighter languages (markdown, astro, nunjucks)
description: Cloudflare/GitHub build logs show repeated 'Could not find the language X, did you forget to load/include a language module?' warnings during `cd site && npm run build`. Missing languages: markdown, astro, nunjucks. Code fences using these languages render unhighlighted on the docs site. Likely fix: register the PrismJS (or whichever highlighter cook-ssg uses) language modules in the site's highlight config. Non-blocking for deploy but visible regression in docs.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add editable mode to <token-specimen>
description: Add editable attribute. Works across all 6 types (shadow, radius, border, color, size, icon). Turn token labels into type-appropriate inputs that write CSS custom properties. Emit token-specimen:change event. Needed for Theme Composer assembly (vanilla-breeze-hz5w).
close_reason: Added editable attribute to <token-specimen>: replaces value cells with inputs across all types (shadow/radius/border/color/size), writes CSS custom properties on target scope. Hex colors get native color picker. Emits token-specimen:change. Doc page updated.

title: Add editable mode to <spacing-specimen>
description: Add editable attribute. When set, token bars include numeric inputs that update --size-* custom properties on the nearest scoped ancestor or :root. Emit spacing-specimen:change event. Needed for Theme Composer assembly (vanilla-breeze-hz5w).
close_reason: Added editable attribute to <spacing-specimen>: per-row text inputs write --size-* token on target scope. Emits spacing-specimen:change. Doc page updated.
