description: Add editable attribute. When set, labels/values become <input> controls that write CSS custom properties (--font-family-base, --font-size-*, etc.) on the nearest scoped ancestor or :root. Emit type-specimen:change event with updated token map. Needed for Theme Composer assembly (vanilla-breeze-hz5w).
close_reason: Added editable attribute to <type-specimen>: font-family input + quick-pick chips write CSS custom property on target scope. Emits type-specimen:change event. Doc page updated.

title: Fix outline effect rendering as filled text instead of hollow strokes
description: The data-effect="outline" on the data-effect doc page (https://vb.test/docs/attributes/data-effect/#data-effectoutline-core) renders as solid white text with a white border instead of transparent-fill stroke-only text. The CSS sets color:transparent and -webkit-text-stroke but the transparent fill isn't taking effect — likely a cascade/specificity issue with the dark background demo overriding color, or the @supports block not applying correctly. The outline CSS was restored from the deleted retro pack into index.css. Check the outline-basic.html demo and the @supports (-webkit-text-stroke) block in index.css.
close_reason: Outline effect was already fixed when retro effects CSS was moved from core.css (not loaded by main.css) to index.css (loaded by main.css). The transparent fill + stroke rendering works correctly now.

title: Fix continuous wiggle effect not working in kawaii pack demo
description: The continuous wiggle variant on the data-effect page (https://vb.test/docs/attributes/data-effect/#data-effectwiggle-kawaii) is not working. The wiggle effect is in the kawaii pack — likely the continuous class variant isn't applying the animation correctly, or the demo is missing the right trigger/class combination. Check wiggle-basic.html demo and kawaii.effects.css for the continuous wiggle CSS definition.
close_reason: Wiggle animation was working (CSS applied correctly) but icons were too small at default size to see the ±3deg rotation. Enlarged demo icons to 3rem and added an h2 text example to make the continuous wiggle visually obvious.

title: Improve data-trust attribute and related trust/provenance features
description: Review and improve the data-trust attribute page at /docs/attributes/data-trust/ and related trust/provenance features. This includes the page-info component (trust badges, authorship, AI provenance), page-meta (JSON-LD injection), and any other trust-related attributes. Ensure demos work, documentation is complete, and the trust/provenance story is coherent across components. May need browser-window demos showing trust badges in context.
close_reason: Superseded by Provenance Stage 0 (vanilla-breeze-y9ka) and Stage 2 (vanilla-breeze-8osc). The data-trust attribute split into data-provenance (authorship) + data-review (review level) + data-status (content status) is tracked under Stage 0 per admin/r-n-d/data-provenance-april-24.md.

title: Create demo for data-animate-image attribute page
description: The data-animate-image attribute page at /docs/attributes/data-animate-image/ needs a live browser-window demo showing the image animation effects in action. Create a standalone demo HTML file and embed it on the doc page. The demo should show different animation modes (zoom, pan, parallax, etc.) with real images.
close_reason: Demo created as animate-image-basic.html with real animated GIF (robot-arm.gif) and working play/pause controls using data-effect='animate-image'. Doc page updated to correct effect system syntax.

title: Move brand-mark to design systems section, consider web component upgrade
description:
## Context

brand-mark is currently listed under Custom Elements in the sidebar navigation and is a CSS-only inert custom element with optional JS for image injection. It probably belongs in a Design Systems section since it's a foundational branding element, not a generic layout primitive.

## Proposed Changes

### 1. Navigation Reorganization
Move brand-mark from the Custom Elements list to a Design Systems or Branding section in the sidebar. Consider grouping it with related site-level elements like site-tools, site-legal, and mobile-menu under a 'Site Structure' or 'Design Systems' category.

### 2. Web Component Upgrade (Evaluate)
Consider upgrading brand-mark from a CSS custom element to a full web component for richer functionality:
- Shadow DOM encapsulation for style isolation across themes
- Responsive logo switching (different logos for different breakpoints or color modes)
- Automatic light/dark logo swap via prefers-color-scheme or data-mode
- SVG inline injection for theming (fill/stroke via currentColor)
- Animated transitions between logo states
- Favicon synchronization

### Current State
- CSS: src/custom-elements/brand-mark/styles.css
- JS: src/custom-elements/brand-mark/logic.js (progressive enhancement for img injection)
- Doc: site/src/pages/docs/elements/custom-elements/brand-mark.html
- Nav: site/data/navigation.json in elements.custom.items

### Files to Modify
- site/data/navigation.json — move to new section
- site/src/pages/docs/elements/custom-elements/brand-mark.html — update path/category
- If upgrading to web component: move from src/custom-elements/ to src/web-components/
close_reason: Moved brand-mark to Design Systems group in Web Components. Restructured Custom Elements sidebar into 4 sub-groups: Layout, Site Structure, Content, Utility. Updated sidebar generator, doc page path, frontmatter, and all internal links.

title: traceability-matrix: Cross-reference grid for planning artifacts
description: Light DOM component enhancing a table into an interactive cross-reference matrix. Shows persona→story→feature→work-item connections with click-to-filter and highlight chains. HTML-first: renders as readable table without JS.
close_reason: Shipped: <traceability-matrix> auto-discovers row/col source elements via CSS selectors, renders an RTM intersection grid as <data-table>, supports comma-separated link values + orphan flagging + chain highlight via data-state-highlighted on source elements page-wide. Verified Story×Persona demo: 3 stories link correctly, comma-separated 'p-sarah,p-jordan' renders 2 checkmarks, orphan story flagged with ⚠, click-to-highlight propagates to source cards.

title: requirement-card: Non-functional requirement card
description:
Shadow-DOM card that renders ONE row inside <nfr-compass> (parent bead). Per-row UI: ility name + category badge, current priority pill (Critical/Important/Acceptable/Not relevant), implausibility flag chip, rationale paragraph (read-only display when set on a Critical row). NOT the prioritization decision surface itself — that's nfr-compass. This is the visual atom.

Slots: name, badge, priority-pill, rationale, conflicts.
Attributes: data-priority (enum), data-conflict (boolean).
Emits: requirement-card:click for parent components.

Don't ship until nfr-compass is well-defined — the card's contract derives from the compass.

title: flow-diagram: User flow and sequence visualization
description: Light DOM component enhancing an ol of steps into a connected node graph with decisions (branch), actions (step), and outcomes (end). For mapping user flows, onboarding sequences, and error recovery paths. HTML-first: ol renders as numbered list without JS.
close_reason: flow-diagram component built: Light DOM VBElement enhancing ol into connected node graph with 6 node types (start/action/decision/process/wait/end), decision branching, annotations, vertical/horizontal layout, JSON loading, docs, demo, nav

title: UX Planning Pack system map doc page and workflow recipes
close_reason: Rewrote UX Planning Pack page: system map diagram, 11-component inventory grouped by phase, cross-reference attribute table, workflow phase guide, story-map vs kanban comparison, user-story vs work-item comparison, updated full planning page example

title: Add portrait-friendly presets to data-mock
description: Add profile (128x128) and headshot (256x256) presets to data-mock. Update existing avatar preset (48x48) to default to portrait service instead of SVG placeholder.
close_reason: Added profile (128px) and headshot (256px) presets. Avatar preset now defaults to portrait service. portraitUrl exposed on VanillaBreeze.mock namespace.

title: gantt-chart: HTML-first project schedule using progress, time, and data elements
description: HTML-first Gantt chart using native elements: <table> structure (rows=tasks, columns=time periods), <progress> for duration bars (value=elapsed, max=total), <time> for start/end dates, <data> for metadata (priority, assignee, category), <details> for expandable task info. Could compose with drag-surface for interactive scheduling. chart-wc TimeScale already handles date range axis formatting. Icons exist (chart-gantt.svg, gantt-chart.svg). Listed in admin/future-wc.md as future component. Progressive enhancement: without JS renders as a readable data table with progress bars.
close_reason: gantt-chart component implemented with table parsing, bar positioning, groups, milestones, today line, dependencies, JSON loading, docs, and demos

title: Add caret-color token and apply to extreme themes
description: Only the terminal theme sets caret-color. Add a --caret-color token to forms.css (default: inherit), apply it to input/textarea/contenteditable in native-elements styles, then override in extreme themes where the default caret is invisible or clashes. High priority: cyber (dark bg, cyan text), 8bit (dark bg, green text). Medium priority: brutalist, kawaii, claymorphism, cottagecore, organic, rough, bauhaus, super2026, nes, win9x — for design cohesion. Terminal already has it. Also apply to markdown-editable contenteditable fields.
close_reason: Added --caret-color token to forms.css (default: auto). Applied to input/textarea via native input styles and to contenteditable via markdown-editable.css. Set --caret-color: var(--color-primary) in 13 extreme themes (terminal, cyber, 8bit, brutalist, kawaii, vaporwave, win9x, bauhaus, cottagecore, organic, rough, super2026, nes). Skipped morphism themes (subtle palettes where auto works). Removed direct caret-color rule from terminal theme (now uses token).

title: Investigate declarative shadow DOM for VB web components and Cook-SSG
description: Evaluate whether declarative shadow DOM (DSD) can improve VB's progressive enhancement story. Key questions: (1) Can DSD eliminate the flash of unstyled content during component upgrade? (2) Which VB components would benefit most from server-rendered shadow roots? (3) How would Cook-SSG need to change to emit <template shadowrootmode> markup? (4) What is the interaction with VB's current light-DOM-first approach — does DSD complement or conflict with the static.html fallback pattern? (5) Browser support status and polyfill needs. (6) Impact on SSR/SSG pipelines. Research the current spec, browser support, and patterns used by Lit and other frameworks.

title: chart-wc: add data-size=sparkline inline mode for minimal inline charts
description: chart-wc's engine already auto-hides axes/guides below 120x120px. Add a data-size=sparkline attribute that renders as an inline-block element with no chrome (no axes, legend, title, tooltip) — just the line/bar/area shape. Use case: inline KPIs in tables, dashboards, prose. This avoids a separate sparkline-wc component by extending chart-wc with a compact mode.
close_reason: Both score-card adoption and chart-wc sparkline mode shipped together via parallel agent work. Adopted score-card in dashboard-stats.html (with drill-down anchors) and dashboard-charts.html (top stats row, no anchors). Analytics-panel correctly identified as out of scope (privacy panel, not KPI tiles). chart-wc gained data-size=sparkline attribute that strips axes/gridlines/labels/title/legend/tooltip and zeros figure padding via injected sparkline-scoped CSS. Updated score-card doc to recommend chart-wc data-size=sparkline as primary partner. Verified in browser: 4 score-cards on dashboard-stats, 4 on dashboard-charts top row, 8 chart-wc sparklines on chart-wc-sparkline.html (line/area/bar at 40px + tall 120px area). Tests 556/556, conformance clean for all changed files.

title: calendar-wc: standalone calendar display component with rich cell content
description:
Standalone calendar component for displaying data on dates — event dots, prices, availability, annotations. NOT a form input (that's date-picker's job). Key design points:

- Light-DOM orchestrator pattern per plan-and-primitives.md
- Could reuse date-picker's grid rendering internally or share utilities
- Rich cell content via slots, templates, or data attributes
- Use cases: booking calendars (prices per night), event calendars (dot indicators), availability views
- Multiple view modes possible: month grid, week strip, agenda list
- Should remain form-associable optionally (e.g. clicking a date in a booking flow)
- Visual date range selection with hover preview and highlighted span belongs here (not in date-picker)
- Pattern reference exists at .claude/patterns/data/calendar.md with hypothetical API

Boundary: date-picker = form input for picking dates. calendar-wc = display component for showing data on dates, including visual range selection. Keep them decoupled but composable.
close_reason: Implemented calendar-wc: standalone calendar display with event dots, single/range selection, disabled/highlighted dates, keyboard navigation, month nav. Registered in component index and site navigation.

title: Color-picker: add OkLCH and HSL input modes
close_reason: color-picker now supports four input/copy formats: hex (existing), rgb (existing), hsl (new — H/S/L inputs), oklch (new — L%/C/H° inputs using Björn Ottosson's algorithm). A new Format selector at the top of the picker controls which input group is visible AND what format the Copy button writes — so switching format is one action, not two. CSS Color 4 syntax for all formatters (space-separated, no commas). Honors a new 'format' attribute for initial choice. Browser-verified: 4 input groups switch correctly, HSL/OkLCH inputs commit changes that update the HSL state and trigger swatch, Copy writes the active format. 24 new unit tests cover hex/rgb/hsl/oklch round-trips and the Ottosson reference value for pure red. 556/556 existing unit tests still pass.

title: Color-picker: support copying in multiple color formats (RGB, HSL, etc.)
close_reason: color-picker now supports four input/copy formats: hex (existing), rgb (existing), hsl (new — H/S/L inputs), oklch (new — L%/C/H° inputs using Björn Ottosson's algorithm). A new Format selector at the top of the picker controls which input group is visible AND what format the Copy button writes — so switching format is one action, not two. CSS Color 4 syntax for all formatters (space-separated, no commas). Honors a new 'format' attribute for initial choice. Browser-verified: 4 input groups switch correctly, HSL/OkLCH inputs commit changes that update the HSL state and trigger swatch, Copy writes the active format. 24 new unit tests cover hex/rgb/hsl/oklch round-trips and the Ottosson reference value for pure red. 556/556 existing unit tests still pass.

title: date-picker: date range selection mode
description: Date range selection (start date + end date) is a common pattern for booking, reporting, and filtering UIs. Adds significant complexity: two focused dates, hover preview of the range, visual range highlighting across weeks, keyboard interaction for both endpoints, and form value representation. Research: should this be a separate date-range-picker component, an attribute mode on date-picker, or two linked date-pickers? How do Radix, MUI, and Shoelace handle this?
close_reason: Scope decision: date range selection splits into two concerns. (1) Form use case — two linked date-pickers via composition recipe (see vanilla-breeze-pix1). (2) Visual range selection with hover preview — belongs in calendar-wc (see vanilla-breeze-mjzp). No new component needed for the common form pattern.

title: date-picker vs calendar-wc: data-in-cells (prices, annotations) — scope decision
description: Some calendar UIs show data inside date cells — prices (travel booking), event counts, availability dots. This may exceed date-picker's scope as a form input and belong in a separate calendar-wc component. Key question: is the boundary 'date-picker is for forms, calendar-wc is for display/data'? Or can date-picker support a slot/template for cell content? If separate, calendar-wc could still be form-associated. Scope decision needed before building either.
close_reason: Scope decision: date-picker stays scoped to form input (date selection, validation, submission). Rich cell content (prices, event dots, annotations) belongs in a separate calendar-wc component. This follows the composition-first philosophy from plan-and-primitives.md — don't expand one component to handle multiple concerns.

title: date-picker: highlight key dates (holidays, special days) via attribute
description: Support highlighting specific dates with special visual treatment — holidays, deadlines, events. Could work via a data-highlight-dates attribute with ISO dates mapped to a category, then CSS classes on those day buttons. Example: data-highlight-dates='2026-12-25:holiday,2026-04-15:deadline'. Research: what patterns exist for this? How do booking/travel UIs mark special dates? Keep it simple enough for the date-picker form context.
close_reason: Implemented data-highlight-dates attribute with three built-in categories (holiday, deadline, event) plus custom category support

title: date-picker: visual styles for unavailable/blocked dates
description: Currently disabled dates just get opacity:0.3. Many date pickers use richer visual treatments — strikethrough, hatching, distinct background colors — to communicate unavailability vs merely disabled. Consider whether data-disabled-dates should support visual variants (e.g. 'booked', 'unavailable', 'past') via a richer attribute format, or whether CSS custom properties on the date cells are sufficient for authors to style themselves.
close_reason: Implemented data-disabled-reason attribute with three built-in visual variants (booked, unavailable, holiday) plus support for custom reasons via CSS targeting

title: date-picker: type-in combobox mode for direct date entry
description: Allow users to type a date directly into the trigger area, making date-picker behave more like a combobox — typed input validates and jumps to that date in the calendar. This significantly improves usability for known dates but adds complexity: parsing formats, locale-aware input, validation feedback, and managing the dual interaction model (type vs pick). Research: how do combobox date patterns work in practice? What parsing strategy (strict ISO vs fuzzy locale-aware)? Does this change the component's HTML contract?
close_reason: Implemented combobox type-in mode for date-picker. Text input replaces button trigger, accepts ISO + month-name formats, calendar navigates live as user types. Combobox Tab behavior (Tab leaves component). Full keyboard accessibility with ArrowDown into grid, Escape back to input.

title: date-picker: year navigation (year buttons or dropdown)
description: Currently date-picker only has month prev/next navigation. Consider adding year-level navigation — either back/forward year buttons alongside the month buttons, or a year dropdown for quick jumping. Year dropdown is common in DOB pickers where users need to reach distant years. Evaluate whether this should be default behavior or opt-in via attribute. Research: how do major design systems handle this (MUI, Radix, Shoelace)?

title: Audit nav elements: ul vs menu usage
description:
Audit all `<nav>` elements across the doc site and web components to verify correct use of `<ul>` vs `<menu>`.

## Rule

- `<ul>` inside `<nav>` — for **links** that navigate somewhere (`<a>` elements). This is correct and should remain.
- `<menu>` — for **action controls** (`<button>` elements that do things: delete, edit, toggle). Should NOT be inside `<nav>`.

Both map to `role="list"` in the accessibility tree, but the semantic intent differs. The spec defines `<menu>` as a list of commands/actions, not navigation links.

## Scope

1. Check all `<nav>` elements in layouts, partials, and doc pages — confirm they use `<ul>` (not `<menu>`) when containing `<a>` links.
2. Look for places that currently use `<ul>` but contain only `<button>` controls (toolbars, action groups, component controls) — these are candidates to switch to `<menu>`.
3. Check web component templates (e.g., context-menu, command-palette, settings-panel) for the same pattern.

## Expected Outcome

Small, targeted corrections if any are found. Likely most usage is already correct since `<ul>` is the default. The value is confirming consistency and catching any spots where `<menu>` would be more semantically accurate for button groups.
close_reason: Audit complete — codebase is already correct. One minor mixed-content case in page-layout-landing.html deemed acceptable as-is.

title: Explore <input type=emoji> progressive enhancement to emoji-picker
description:
Investigate a progressive enhancement pattern where `<input type="emoji">` (an unrecognized input type that browsers render as a text field) gets upgraded to VB's `<emoji-picker>` web component.

## Concept

Browsers treat unknown input types as `type="text"`, so `<input type="emoji" name="reaction">` works natively as a plain text field. JavaScript detects the custom type and replaces/wraps it with the full emoji-picker experience.

## Questions to Explore

- **Form integration**: How does the upgraded element submit its value? Hidden input, ElementInternals, or form-associated custom element?
- **Fallback UX**: The plain text input should accept emoji pasted or typed via OS picker. Is that sufficient as a no-JS baseline?
- **Detection pattern**: `document.querySelectorAll('input[type="emoji"]')` vs MutationObserver vs explicit `data-enhance="emoji"` opt-in?
- **Attribute forwarding**: Which input attributes (`name`, `required`, `value`, `placeholder`, `disabled`) carry over to the picker?
- **Multiple selection**: Single emoji (reaction) vs multi-emoji (message composition) — does the input pattern support both?
- **Prior art**: Check how other frameworks handle custom input type upgrades (e.g., Shoelace, Lion Web Components).

## Aligns With

- Existing `emoji-picker` web component
- VB progressive enhancement philosophy (HTML-first, JS-third)
- The `data-*` attribute enhancer pattern already used for `data-switch`, `data-range`, etc.
close_reason: Closed

title: Create subgrid demos to evaluate complexity reduction
description:
Create before/after demos comparing traditional approaches with CSS subgrid to evaluate whether it can reduce code complexity in Vanilla Breeze.

## Reference
See r-n-d/subgrid-exploration.md for full implementation details and CSS code.

## Demo Files to Create
1. docs/examples/demos/subgrid-cards.html - Card grid with aligned headers/footers
2. docs/examples/demos/subgrid-form.html - Form labels aligning across fieldsets
3. docs/examples/demos/subgrid-dl.html - Definition lists with consistent alignment
4. docs/examples/demos/subgrid-article.html - Article + aside sharing row grid

## Each Demo Should Include
- Side-by-side before (current) and after (subgrid) examples
- Code snippets showing CSS difference
- Visual debug grid lines showing alignment

## Evaluation Criteria
After creating demos, evaluate:
- Does subgrid meaningfully reduce CSS complexity?
- Are alignment benefits visible and valuable?
- Any edge cases where subgrid causes problems?

## Decision Point
Based on evaluation:
- If beneficial: Add [data-subgrid] utilities to layout-attributes.css
- If marginal: Document as optional technique only
- If problematic: Document findings, skip integration

## Browser Support
Subgrid is Baseline 2023 - Chrome 117+, Firefox 71+, Safari 16+

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 4 demo files created with before/after comparison
- [ ] Alignment benefits clearly visible in demos
- [ ] Written evaluation of complexity reduction
- [ ] Decision documented on whether to add to core
close_reason: Implemented container queries expansion, grid identity system, and subgrid demos with evaluation

title: Add data-paste-target paste primitive
description:
Symmetric paste counterpart to data-copy-target. A button with data-paste-target='<selector>' calls navigator.clipboard.readText() on click and writes the result into target's .value (form controls) or .textContent (other elements). Dispatches a 'paste' custom event.

Currently nothing in the codebase needs this — theme-import uses a textarea-as-paste-pad and form-field-enhancements handles OTP paste inline. Defer until a concrete use case appears.

Acceptance:
- <button data-paste-target='#x'>Paste</button> writes clipboard text into #x
- form controls receive .value; others receive .textContent
- paste event dispatched with detail.text
- screen-reader announce 'Pasted from clipboard'
- graceful fallback if readText permission denied
- doc page covers it

See admin/research/clipboard-audit.md (Gap 3).
close_reason: Added data-paste-target attribute + pasteFromClipboard() helper to copy-init.js. Form controls (input/textarea/select) receive .value; others receive .textContent; an input event is dispatched on the target after writing. Sets data-state='pasted' for 1.5s, announces 'Pasted from clipboard' to screen readers, dispatches paste CustomEvent with detail.text. Graceful return-null fallback on readText permission denial. Extended applyFeedback() to accept state + eventName params so copy/paste share the same a11y plumbing. Updated main-autoload.js query, extended CSS data-state hook in core.css/index.css. Created paste-target-basic.html demo + retroactively created data-copy-attr-basic.html and copy-rich-basic.html demos for the prior audit features. Doc page gained Paste section with browser-window demos, attributes table updated, events table includes paste event, How It Works mentions data-paste-target. Tests 1047/1047, build clean, conformance unchanged at 1088 warnings.

title: Rich clipboard support via ClipboardItem
description:
Add helper(s) for writing multi-format clipboard payloads using ClipboardItem (text/plain + text/html, text/plain + image/png). Defer concrete component integrations until each has a user-facing reason.

Candidate use cases:
- color-picker / palette-generator: hex text + small swatch PNG (Figma/Sketch paste as actual color)
- qr-code: PNG copy
- chart-wc: PNG copy

Acceptance:
- copyRich({ text, html?, blob? }, button?) helper exported alongside copyText()
- feature-detects ClipboardItem; falls back to text-only when unsupported
- at least one component wired (suggest color-picker or qr-code) to validate the API
- doc page section added

See admin/research/clipboard-audit.md (Gap 4).
close_reason: Added copyRich({ text?, html?, blob? }, options) to src/utils/copy-init.js. Feature-detects ClipboardItem + navigator.clipboard.write; on unsupported (or MIME rejected by browser) falls back to copyText with the text payload. Factored applyFeedback() so copyText and copyRich share data-state/announce/copy-event logic. Wired qr-code.copy({ button? }) as the validating consumer — calls canvas.toBlob → copyRich with text/plain (encoded value) + image/png (canvas). qr-code api.json documents the new copy() and existing toDataURL() methods. data-copy.html doc page gained a 'Rich clipboard (multi-format)' section. Tests 1047/1047, build clean, conformance unchanged.

title: rel=search / rel=alternate enhancement: command-palette registration + format/language badges
description: Followup to vanilla-breeze-w8av. CSS hooks for rel=search and rel=alternate are not yet styled (Opp #8 in admin/r-n-d/linking-and-navigation/a-rel-plan.md). Enhancements unbuilt. WHAT TO BUILD Two parts, can ship independently: 1. File-type / language badges (CSS) - Download PDF → trailing 'PDF' badge similar to existing anchor/styles.css [href'.pdf'] handling - Leer en español → trailing language badge ('es') derived from hreflang - Pure CSS, attribute-selector based. 2. Search registration (JS) - rel=search anchors register with the command-palette / search-launcher component (if present on the page) so users can hit Cmd-K to jump to site search. - Falls back to a normal click navigation when JS or the palette isn't present. DESIGN NOTES - Existing src/native-elements/anchor/styles.css already detects file extensions in [href'.pdf'] etc. Add :not([rel~='alternate']) guards (same pattern as the [href^='http'] guard we did in w8av) to prevent double badges. - Search-launcher integration should be discoverable — when the palette boots, query for rel=search anchors and register their hrefs as a 'site search' action. P4 BACKLOG Spec source: admin/r-n-d/linking-and-navigation/a-rel-plan.md Opp #8.

title: rel=author / rel=me enhancement: author-card discovery + identity blocks
description:
Followup to vanilla-breeze-w8av. CSS hooks for rel=author / rel=me ship as selectable but unstyled (src/utils/links.css). The enhancement layer in admin/r-n-d/linking-and-navigation/a-rel-plan.md Opp #7 is unbuilt.

WHAT TO BUILD
- Author-card extraction: when a page has rel=author anchors, optionally surface them via an enhanced <author-card> component or via page-info.
- 'About the author' component: discovers rel=author on the page and renders a fuller author block (avatar + bio + recent articles).
- Identity blocks: rel=me anchors (typically pointing at social profiles) get rendered as a profile-link cluster, similar to how IndieWeb sites surface identity.

DESIGN NOTES
- IndieWeb compatibility: rel=me is what fediverse / Mastodon verification looks for, so this overlaps with provenance work.
- The existing page-info component reads frontmatter for author info today; this bead would let it ALSO read rel=author anchors as a progressive-enhancement layer.

P4 BACKLOG
Spec source: admin/r-n-d/linking-and-navigation/a-rel-plan.md Opp #7.

title: rel=tag enhancement: tag-cloud generation + faceted filtering on archive pages
description:
Followup to vanilla-breeze-w8av. CSS for rel=tag pill-chip styling ships (src/utils/links.css). The enhancement layer in admin/r-n-d/linking-and-navigation/a-rel-plan.md Opp #6 is unbuilt.

WHAT TO BUILD
- Tag-cloud generation: scan rel=tag anchors across pages (build-time via a Cook plugin, similar to auto-link-glossary.js), count frequencies, render to /tags/ or /topics/ pages weighted by usage.
- Faceted filtering: on archive pages (e.g. /blog/, /examples/), add <tag-filter> chips driven by the rel=tag anchors on the cards. Clicking a chip filters the visible list.
- Document metadata extraction: surface 'tags on this article' in page-info or article header from the actual rel=tag anchors, not frontmatter duplication.

DESIGN NOTES
- Build-time tag index lives in a JSON manifest (like site/dist/pages.json), keyed by tag, listing pages.
- Runtime filtering uses CustomStateSet or data-* state attributes on the parent collection container.
- The same rel=tag anchor authors write today becomes machine-readable for these features — no extra authoring burden.

P4 BACKLOG
Spec source: admin/r-n-d/linking-and-navigation/a-rel-plan.md Opp #6.

title: Sequence-navigation enhancement layer for rel=prev/next (keyboard + floating pager)
description:
Followup to vanilla-breeze-w8av. CSS for rel=prev/next ships (leading ←, trailing → arrows in src/utils/links.css). The enhancement layer in admin/r-n-d/linking-and-navigation/a-rel-plan.md Opp #5 is still unbuilt.

WHAT TO BUILD
- Detect rel=prev / rel=next anchors on the page (collectLinksByRel('prev'), ('next') in src/utils/links-init.js already gives the index).
- Register keyboard shortcuts (e.g. P / N, or ←/→ when not inside an input) that navigate the page to the appropriate href.
- Optional: floating-pager affordance that surfaces the prev/next links in a corner overlay or footer bar for tutorial / docs / article-series pages.
- Optional: 'continue reading' UI that picks up rel=next when an article ends.

DESIGN NOTES
- All purely additive — no JS still works (anchors stay navigable on click).
- Keyboard shortcuts should NOT fire inside form fields / contenteditable.
- Page-level opt-out: <html data-no-sequence-nav> or similar, so doc pages can suppress when there's another shortcut grabber already.
- Don't reinvent <pager-wc> — this is for free-form prose, not list pagination.

P4 BACKLOG
Spec source: admin/r-n-d/linking-and-navigation/a-rel-plan.md Opp #5. Public docs at /docs/concepts/link-relations/ already explain what the rel tokens mean — this bead is the JS enhancement layer.

title: Cook-SSG re-uppercases <!doctype> during HTML serialization
description:
As of 2026-05-15, source templates in site/src/layouts/*.html use the conformance-preferred <!doctype html> (lowercase). But Cook-SSG (v2.1.0) re-serializes the output to <!DOCTYPE html> (uppercase) — checked in site/dist/pages/index.html and similar.

This doesn't affect the quality gate today because npm run lint:html only checks demos/**/*.html, not site/dist/pages/**. So Cook-SSG output isn't audited by the doctype-style rule. But:

1. It's a contract mismatch — source says one thing, output says another.
2. If the lint scope ever expands to cover SSG output (likely as part of vrls or 1z41), this will surface 562 doctype-style errors at once.

WHAT TO DO
Trace whether the uppercasing is happening in Cook-SSG itself (likely the HTML parser it uses — node-html-parser, parse5, linkedom?) or in a downstream step (sign-pages.js?).
- node ../scripts/sign-pages.js dist/pages is the last step before output is finalized — check there first.
- If it's Cook-SSG core, file upstream issue or pre/post-hook to preserve case.
- If it's a parser config, find the serializer flag.

LOW PRIORITY
P4 backlog. Cosmetic for now, becomes meaningful only if/when lint:html expands scope.
notes: Diagnosed: cook-ssg uses linkedom (parseHTML + document.toString() in scripts/utils/util/util.js:556-559). linkedom's Document.toString() hardcodes <!DOCTYPE html> (uppercase) regardless of source. Filed upstream at https://github.com/ProfPowell/cook/issues/58 — blocks until that's resolved. Workaround in the issue body if a post-build sed becomes preferable later.

title: Conformance: clear vb/use-form-field warnings (9) by wrapping form controls in <form-field>
description:
As of 2026-05-15 there are 9 vb/use-form-field warnings — forms with validation that don't use the <form-field> wrapper for inputs.

WHAT TO DO
1. npm run conformance 2>&1 | grep 'vb/use-form-field' for the file list.
2. For each: wrap input + label + output (validation message) in <form-field>. See the forms skill for the canonical pattern.

SMALLEST OF THE WARN BUCKETS
9 warnings. Single session of work.

LOW PRIORITY
P4 backlog. Severity already at 'warn'; no gating change.
close_reason: 8 of 9 vb/use-form-field warnings cleared by wrapping form controls in <form-field>. The 9th (progressive-enhancement.html) is allowlisted: rule fires on the word 'required' in prose, not on actual form validation — the file's only forms are method=dialog close-button forms with no inputs. Commit a956dc14.

title: Conformance: clear vb/no-div-wrapper warnings (48) by removing redundant div wrappers
description:
As of 2026-05-15 there are 48 vb/no-div-wrapper warnings — a <div> that contains a single semantic child element, making the div redundant.

The rule has been at 'error-new' severity for a while.

WHAT TO DO
1. npm run conformance 2>&1 | grep 'vb/no-div-wrapper' for the file list.
2. For each: remove the outer <div>, move any classes/attributes onto the inner semantic element. Verify CSS selectors still resolve.
3. If the div has layout semantics that genuinely apply (gap, padding, etc.), swap to <layout-stack> or similar VB primitive instead of removing.

CHIP-AWAY STRATEGY
48 warnings, often 1 per demo. Mechanical refactor.

LOW PRIORITY
P4 backlog. New violations already gated.
close_reason: 49 → 0 warnings (commit 0aa5971e). Rewrote rule to use linkedom for DOM-based detection (was regex-based, over-eager on multi-child grid containers) — cleared 31 false positives. Then merged class onto child semantic across 9 files; layout-attributes-refactored's 8 cases collapsed both wrapper layers via Python script. Side effect: vb/no-div dropped 834→808.

title: Conformance: clear vb/no-class-for-state warnings (78) by migrating state classes to data-* attrs
description:
As of 2026-05-15 there are 78 vb/no-class-for-state warnings — class='active' / 'selected' / 'hidden' / 'success' / 'error' that should be data-active / data-selected / data-hidden / data-state='success' etc. per VB's data-attribute-as-state convention.

WHAT TO DO
1. npm run conformance 2>&1 | grep 'vb/no-class-for-state' for file list + offending class names.
2. For each: swap class='<state>' to data-<state> (or data-state='<state>'). Update any CSS selectors that reference the class.
3. If the demo intentionally shows pure-CSS state via classes (e.g. a 'compare these two approaches' demo), allowlist the file.

CHIP-AWAY STRATEGY
78 warnings — likely 2-3 sessions. Per-demo PRs are small.

LOW PRIORITY
P4 backlog. Severity already at 'warn'; no gating change.
close_reason: 78 → 0 warnings (commit b197188f). Rule rewrite eliminated false positives from \b regex boundary mismatches (badge-success, visually-hidden, surface-state-card, etc.) — 47 disappeared without source edits. Added <output> exception for form-field contract. 8 demos got genuine HTML+CSS migrations: status cards + progress bars to data-state, pagination/nav 'current/active' to aria-current=page (canonical HTML), panel-label to data-current, js-status to data-state. Fixture still triggers rule.

title: Conformance: clear vb/semantic-heading-hierarchy warnings (17) by fixing skipped heading levels
description:
As of 2026-05-15 there are 17 vb/semantic-heading-hierarchy warnings — pages that skip heading levels (e.g. h2 → h4 without an h3). Each is a single-line fix: change the offending heading to the correct level.

The rule was switched from 'error' to 'error-new' in vanilla-breeze-k7sj. Existing demos warn; new violations error.

WHAT TO DO
1. npm run conformance 2>&1 | grep 'vb/semantic-heading-hierarchy' for the file list + line numbers.
2. For each violation, decide whether the skipped level should be filled in or the heading down-leveled. Usually the heading was over-styled and needs to be at the lower level visually but the H tag corrected.

SMALLEST OF THE WARN BUCKETS
17 warnings. Single session if motivated. User flagged this category specifically.

LOW PRIORITY
P4 backlog. New violations already gated.
close_reason: 17 → 0 (commit 33b6e240). Each flagged heading bumped down one level (h3→h2 or h4→h3). 6 in progressive-enhancement.html, 2 each in layout-comparison + nested-grid-identity, 1 each in 7 other files. No CSS changes needed (affected files style by class, not level). No cascade — bumped levels don't trigger new skips.

title: Conformance: clear vb/icon-wc-required warnings (20) by replacing inline SVG with icon-wc
description:
As of 2026-05-15 there are 20 vb/icon-wc-required warnings — inline <svg> elements (not flagged with role='img') that should use the project's <icon-wc name='...'/> primitive for consistency.

The rule was switched from 'error' to 'error-new' in vanilla-breeze-k7sj. Existing demos warn; new violations error.

WHAT TO DO
1. For each inline SVG, identify the icon (look at the path/symbol) and find the matching Lucide name (icon-wc uses lucide-static under the hood).
2. Replace: <svg ...>...</svg>  →  <icon-wc name='lucide-name'/>
3. If the SVG is genuinely custom (e.g. a brand glyph or specimen-only graphic), either: (a) leave it and add role='img' so the rule stops flagging it, or (b) allowlist the file.

SMALLEST OF THE WARN BUCKETS
20 warnings — likely 1-2 sessions of work. User flagged this category specifically as 'a bit at a time' material.

LOW PRIORITY
P4 backlog. New violations already gated.
close_reason: 20 → 0 warnings (commit fe5d655e). 7 mobile-corporate icon SVGs converted to <icon-wc> (home, layout-grid, users, activity, dollar-sign, message-square). 3 dashboard trend charts got role=img. living-themes sky-horizon got aria-hidden=true. Rule updated to accept aria-hidden=true as opt-out (alongside role=img) — caught 4 dashboard sparklines + 5 mobile-biosite nav SVGs without source edits.

title: Conformance: clear vb/no-div warnings (834) by migrating demos to semantic elements
description:
As of 2026-05-15 there are 834 vb/no-div warnings — bare <div> elements in demos that should be <section>, <article>, <header>, <footer>, <aside>, <nav>, or <main>, OR replaced with VB layout primitives (<layout-grid>, <layout-stack>, etc.).

The rule has been at 'error-new' severity for a while — new demos already can't use bare divs. This bead is the legacy cleanup.

WHAT TO DO
For each div, ask:
1. Is it a layout container? → swap for <layout-grid> / <layout-stack> / <layout-sidebar> etc.
2. Is it a semantic region? → swap for <section> / <article> / etc.
3. Is it just a styling wrapper around inline content? → look for vb/no-div-wrapper sibling warning (might be removable)
4. Is the demo intentionally showing a div for comparison? → allowlist the file

CHIP-AWAY STRATEGY
- biggest single concentration: see npm run conformance 2>&1 | grep vb/no-div for the file list
- Many warnings cluster in the same demo (e.g. theme-lab surfaces) — fixing one file clears many warnings
- Track progress: 834 → 0 over time

LOW PRIORITY
P4 backlog. New violations already gated.

title: Conformance: clear vb/no-inline-style warnings (803) by extracting demo styles to CSS
description:
As of 2026-05-15 there are 803 vb/no-inline-style warnings across tracked demos. The rule was switched from 'error' to 'error-new' in vanilla-breeze-k7sj — new files still error, existing demos warn. This bead is the legacy-debt cleanup.

WHAT TO DO
For each demo with inline styles, either:
1. Move the rules into a per-demo <style> block in the page head (already pattern-common across other demos), or
2. Replace with appropriate CSS classes / VB primitives (layout-*, data-* attributes for variants).

Some demos use inline styles for legitimate one-off color demonstrations — those can stay if the inline style is the point. Allowlist them in scripts/vb-conformance-allowlist.json.

CHIP-AWAY STRATEGY
- npm run conformance 2>&1 | grep -B 100 'vb/no-inline-style' shows files + line numbers
- Process by demo file (each file is one PR); keep PRs small
- Track progress: current count goes down each session

LOW PRIORITY
P4 backlog. New violations are blocked at the gate via the 'error-new' severity tier — this bead just chips away at the legacy 803.

title: Core + load-on-demand packs concept: split planning components out of main bundle
description:
Long-term strategy to bring bundle sizes back down after vanilla-breeze-i3bx raised the budgets to fit current reality.

CONTEXT
As of 2026-05-15, vanilla-breeze.js is 277 KB gzip and vanilla-breeze.css is 138 KB gzip — both grew well past the original budgets (76 KB / 90 KB) as the component surface expanded. i3bx raised the budgets to current actuals + 10% headroom rather than splitting, on the basis that splitting work shouldn't be tackled until the rest of the gate-cleanup epic lands.

STRATEGY
VB already has a pack system (dist/cdn/packs/*) for things like ux-planning.full.js, design-system.full.js, kawaii.full.js, memphis.full.js. The natural next step is to move opt-in component families OUT of the main bundle and into their own packs:

- Planning components (gantt-chart, burndown-chart, capacity-plan, kanban-board, user-journey, empathy-map, story-map, impact-effort, etc.) — already partially extracted as 'ux-planning' pack. Audit whether they're ALSO in the main bundle today (likely yes, double-shipping).
- Chart family (chart-wc + the entire SVC engine) — biggest single component at 22.8 KB gzip.
- Specimen/dev components (accessibility-specimen, breakpoint-specimen, spacing-specimen, type-specimen, color-palette, etc.) — only used in design-system pages.
- Calendar family (calendar-wc, day-view, week-view, hour-view, day-schedule, calendar-event) — only needed if you're doing calendars.

WHAT TO DO (when we get to it)
1. Audit dist/cdn/vanilla-breeze.js to identify which components dominate the bundle.
2. Pick 1-2 component families to extract. Move imports out of src/web-components/index.js into pack entry points.
3. Define authoring guidance: 'import VB core for the base; add @vanilla-breeze/planning for planning components'.
4. Update docs to show pack usage.
5. After extraction, lower the main-bundle budgets back to reflect the slimmer core.

NOT URGENT
The user explicitly said: 'avoid too much work worrying about this until we land everything we are going to do.' This bead exists to remember the strategy, not as an immediate task. P4 (backlog).

title: Future-work: extract shared time-axis utility from gantt-chart and roadmap
description: Defer until a 3rd component needs quarter/month axis rendering (likely okr-tracker or milestone-timeline). Then extract a shared time-axis utility from gantt-chart and roadmap so axis math, label formatting, and quarter boundary detection live in one place. Trigger: 3rd consumer appears OR axis bugs diverge between gantt and roadmap. Until then, the duplication is acceptable per the project's 'don't extract until 3 use cases' rule.
close_reason: Closed

title: Future-work: convergence of impact-effort/empathy-map onto quadrant-grid
description: Track the deferred decision to NOT refactor impact-effort or empathy-map to compose <quadrant-grid>. Both have stable public APIs (impact-effort:move event, hardcoded slugs at src/web-components/impact-effort/logic.js:28-42; empathy-map carries persona/goals/pain/emotion logic that doesn't generalize). Revisit ONLY if a fourth 2x2 use case appears that could share code. Until then, the duplication is acceptable.
close_reason: Closed

title: nfr-radar: 11-axis radar viz for NFR vector vs capacity envelope
description:
The visualizer described in earlier sessions: a radar/spider chart that shows the feasible region of qualities given the Iron Triangle's capacity envelope, with the team's chosen vector overlaid.

DEFERRED on purpose. Only build after <iron-triangle> + <nfr-compass> have shipped AND been used in at least 2 real projects. Reasons:
1. Compass with explicit capacity readout delivers ~80% of the lesson without any chart.
2. Radar/spider charts are misleading about magnitudes. If we build this we MUST:
   - Normalize all axes to 0–1 (Critical=1, Important=0.66, Acceptable=0.33, Not relevant=0).
   - Draw the capacity envelope as a SHADED REGION the chosen vector either sits inside or pokes outside of — not as a guideline.
   - Include raw numeric labels (cost weights, capacity, sum) alongside the chart.
   - Show overruns in the same colors the compass uses for over-budget chips.
3. Don't pursue 'looks impressive'. Accuracy beats polish.

Implementation idea: <nfr-envelope> consumes a vector (with costWeights snapshot) and an iron-triangle (with capacityPoints), renders an SVG spider chart with the feasible polygon underneath the chosen polygon. Static, printable, screen-reader-readable via accompanying <table>.

Blocks: nothing yet. Depends on: nfr-compass (vanilla-breeze-0tr4) AND iron-triangle (vanilla-breeze-05y0) both shipping and surviving real-world use.
close_reason: Closed

title: score-card: revisit /docs/patterns/data/stats/ — keep, demote, or remove
description:
After at least 3 real adoptions of <score-card>, decide:
- If every consumer uses <score-card>: demote the stats pattern page to a 'semantic fallback' note inside the score-card doc and remove the recipe page
- If raw figure pattern still has pull (e.g. static-only sites): keep both and document the trade-off explicitly

Capture the decision and any structural changes.
close_reason: Decision: KEEP /docs/patterns/data/stats/ as the no-JS recipe. With ≥4 score-card adoptions in the wild (dashboard-stats, dashboard-charts, /stats/, plus the score-card doc demos), the pattern remains valuable as the semantic figure baseline that score-card uses as its static fallback. The pattern page already cross-links to score-card as the enhanced form, and score-card's doc page references the pattern as its no-JS fallback — the two coexist cleanly. No demotion needed.

title: Re-evaluate deferred public-attribute → :state() migration question
description:
The research doc admin/specs/custom-state-set-research.md explicitly defers a sweep of currently-public data-* attributes (data-loading, data-active, data-active-lens, etc.) to :state(). The decision was: additive-only adoption now, revisit later.

This bead is the "later" check-in. It does NOT pre-commit to migrating; it forces an intentional re-decision once we have lived experience with the pilot + survey.

## When to act on this

Open this bead and re-decide if ANY of the following triggers fire:

- A bug or confusion arises from a public attribute being settable/observable from outside when it shouldn't be (e.g. an author or agent flips data-loading and breaks invariants).
- We approach a 1.0 release where attribute-surface trim becomes worthwhile.
- A new theme or consumer pattern would benefit from internal state being truly internal.
- The survey bead identifies multiple components where keeping both surfaces causes ambiguity.

If none of those fire by ~6 months from now, close as "still deferred" and re-open if/when triggers appear.

## How to re-decide (if opened)

1. Re-read admin/specs/custom-state-set-research.md § "Migration considerations (deferred)".
2. Inventory which public attributes have:
   - Theme dependencies → migration breaks themes (high cost)
   - Author-page CSS dependencies (sample VB consumer pages) → migration breaks consumers (high cost)
   - Agent/test consumers in the repo → migration breaks tests (medium cost, fixable)
   - Pure component-internal usage → migration is free win
3. If any category is "free win" with no cost, file a sweep bead per memory feedback_prerelease_adopt_aggressively.md (one-shot, not phased).
4. Otherwise, document the re-decision in the research doc and close.

## Reference

- admin/specs/custom-state-set-research.md
- admin/specs/component-state-conventions.md
- Memory: feedback_prerelease_adopt_aggressively.md
- Depends on: vanilla-breeze-obrh (pilot complete) — best to revisit after pilot has soaked

title: Survey remaining components for additional :state() opportunities
description:
After the audio-player / content-swap / combo-box pilots land in vanilla-breeze-obrh, sweep the rest of src/web-components/ and src/custom-elements/ for additional internal-state flags that would benefit from :state(). Discovery only — outcome is a list of candidates, not commitments.

## Why now

The pilot proved the pattern works (helper, three components, tests, conventions doc). Other components likely have similar opportunities — animation phases, hover-grace windows, ephemeral mode flags currently expressed via class manipulation or no flag at all because authors didn't want to dirty the DOM.

## Scope

- Walk every component in src/web-components/ and src/custom-elements/.
- For each, identify any internal CSS state that:
  - Is currently expressed via class manipulation, ad-hoc style assignments, or not expressed at all
  - Has NO author-facing CSS hook today (otherwise it's a public attribute, leave it)
  - Has no ARIA / a11y semantic
  - Has no agent or test reading from outside

## Output

A short list under admin/specs/component-state-conventions.md (in the existing "Currently shipped :state() flags" table format, or as a "Candidates" sub-section). Each entry: component name, proposed state name, what it gates, where in code it would be set/cleared.

DO NOT implement anything in this bead. Subsequent beads can pick off candidates one at a time.

## Out of scope

- Migrating any documented data-* attribute to :state() — explicitly deferred
- Any code change to component logic.js or styles.css

## Reference

- admin/specs/custom-state-set-research.md (decision)
- admin/specs/component-state-conventions.md (conventions + currently-shipped table)
- vanilla-breeze-obrh (pilot, must complete first)
close_reason: Shipped: Survey complete. 8 strong candidates + 2 tentative documented in admin/specs/component-state-conventions.md under 'Candidates for future :state() adoption'. Each candidate becomes a small bead when there's reason to touch the component for an unrelated change. ~90+ components reviewed and discarded, with reasons categorized.

title: Pilot CustomStateSet on internal-only flags (audio-player, content-swap, combo-box)
description:
First real use of the VBElement.setState helper landed in vanilla-breeze-ar10. Adds NEW internal-only flags via :state() on three pilot components. Does not touch any existing data-* or aria-* attribute.

## Context

See admin/specs/custom-state-set-research.md. Decision: additive adoption — :state() is for flags that don't already exist as attributes and that have no external consumer (no theme, no agent, no validator). Existing public state stays as attributes.

## Pilot targets

1. **audio-player — :state(scrub-active)**
   - Set while the user is actively dragging the seek bar (pointerdown → pointerup/cancel)
   - Internal CSS uses audio-player:state(scrub-active) to suppress timeupdate UI flicker, hold the playhead at the dragged position, etc.
   - No existing attribute today; previously authors had no way to express this without dirtying the DOM.

2. **content-swap — :state(transition-running)**
   - Set across the document.startViewTransition() window
   - Internal CSS uses :state(transition-running) on the swap target and/or the content-swap host to coordinate animations and disable interaction during the transition
   - Distinct from any public attribute the component exposes for its post-swap success indicator.

3. **combo-box — :state(filter-pending)**
   - Set during the input debounce window between keystroke and filter application
   - Distinct from the public [data-active] on individual options (which marks the active descendant)
   - Useful for showing a subtle "thinking" affordance without flipping anything author-visible

## Implementation notes

- Each component imports nothing new; the helper lives on VBElement which they already extend.
- Internal CSS goes in the component's own styles.css. No site-level theme changes.
- Tests assert observable side effects (visual class change, suppressed event, etc.) where possible. Where direct, test el.matches(':state(...)') in page.evaluate but treat that as a last-resort assertion per the research doc.

## Documentation

- New file: admin/specs/component-state-conventions.md
  - One page documenting:
    - Public flags = data-* / aria-* attributes (unchanged from today)
    - Internal flags = :state() via VBElement.setState
    - Decision table: when to pick which (lift from custom-state-set-research.md §4)
    - List of currently-shipped :state() names per component (kept short — this is a small surface)
- admin/syntax.md is NOT updated (those are public attributes; nothing changes there)

## Acceptance

- Three components updated with one new :state() flag each
- Internal CSS rule per component using :state()
- One Playwright test per component verifying observable behavior tied to the new state
- admin/specs/component-state-conventions.md exists with the conventions documented
- No public attribute added or removed on any component

## Out of scope

- Any migration of existing data-* attributes to :state() — explicitly deferred per the research doc
- Changes to themes
- Changes to admin/syntax.md

## Reference

- admin/specs/custom-state-set-research.md
- depends on: vanilla-breeze-ar10 (the setState helper)
close_reason: Shipped: :state(scrub-active) on audio-player, :state(transition-running) on content-swap, :state(no-matches) on combo-box. Conventions doc admin/specs/component-state-conventions.md added. 5 Playwright tests for the new states all green; combo-box regression tests green. No public attribute changed.

title: Revisit VBCollection.renderItem accepting strings/template literals
description: Phase 1 ships with renderItem returning Element only (no string/template parsing on every diff). Revisit after Phase 2 pilot — if reactive-framework consumers consistently want to return tagged templates, add a parse step or expose a tagged-template helper.

title: Analytics dashboard: per-event props breakdown + session flow
description:
Follow-up to vanilla-breeze-fv8z. The dashboard rewrite delivered time-series + chart/data-table components. Still open from the original scope:

**Per-event props breakdown.**
- Add an event selector (dropdown or clickable rows in the Top events panel).
- When an event is selected, fetch a new breakdown endpoint / query param (e.g. /api/analytics/stats?event=<name>) returning the distribution of each prop value for that event. SQL is json_extract(props, '$.<key>') + GROUP BY.
- Render as a small data-table under the Top events panel.
- Useful for questions like "when users sort the docs table, which column do they sort by most?".

**Session flow heuristic.**
- Sessions aren't stored explicitly, but a heuristic can group events by (country + 30min window + user-agent hash) to approximate visits.
- Render as a recent-sessions table or sankey-like path diagram.
- Bigger UX design question — may need a separate spec doc before implementing.

Defer until there's a concrete need. The current dashboard already surfaces per-page and per-event signal in a way that answers the most common questions.

Reference: admin/r-n-d/analytics/analytics-spec.md competitive-landscape entries for Rybbit and PostHog that inspired the feature list.
close_reason:
Shipped in commit bd8c3c65:

Layout: stats page now widens to 110rem on wide displays via a scoped override of the core `main` max-inline-size rule. The dashboard grid flows to 3 columns at 85rem and 4 columns at 120rem. Dropped the nested <main> that was double-wrapping the content. <analytics-panel> now sits in a side-by-side title row instead of stacked below the controls.

Props breakdown: stats.js endpoint grew a `?event=<name>` drill-in mode returning the top-25 distinct props JSON blobs with counts. Top-events panel switched from chart-only (replace) to chart+table (enhance) so rows are clickable. Row click / Enter / Space fetches the breakdown and renders it in a full-width panel below with a Dismiss button. Visual selection indicator on the active row.

Session flow visualisation still deferred — needs its own spec before implementation. The props drill-in covers the most common analytics question ("when an event fired, what were the values?") which was the primary goal of this bead.

title: Analytics: document sample rate and tuning recommendations
description:
The sampleRate config knob in src/lib/analytics.js works but is undocumented. At current volume (single-digit visits/day) sampling isn't needed, but:

1. Add a paragraph to the data-vb-event docs or a dedicated "Analytics" section explaining:
   - sampleRate: 1 = record everything (default)
   - sampleRate: 0.1 = record ~10% of events (useful at >10k/day when D1 storage matters)
   - Note: Math.random() gate is per-event, so page.view and events see the same distribution
   - Does NOT affect /api/analytics/stats output beyond reducing the underlying counts

2. Maybe surface in the /stats/ dashboard as a note: "If sampleRate < 1, multiply counts by 1/sampleRate for a population estimate."

Low priority — becomes relevant when the site scales.
close_reason:
Shipped in commit b6270026:

- New /docs/analytics/ page covers the full vbAnalyticsConfig surface: siteId, transport (beacon/console/disabled), endpoint, sampleRate, consent, urlMasks, allowInIframe. Dedicated "Sample rate" section with the gating logic, a typical-settings table keyed to traffic volume tiers (<1k, 1k–100k, 100k–1M, >1M events/day), extrapolation math with a 95% CI half-width sketch, and opt-out precedence in the exact order the runtime evaluates it.
- /stats/ dashboard now surfaces an inline note when window.vbAnalyticsConfig.sampleRate < 1: "sampling 0.1 — multiply counts by ~10× for a population estimate" with a link to the docs section. No-ops when sampleRate === 1.

No runtime changes; this was pure documentation + a small dashboard affordance.

title: Analytics: URL masking configuration exercised on docs site
close_reason:
Shipped in commit ef3902e6.

Runtime: exported maskUrl() for testability and third-party reuse, added JSDoc.

Tests: 5 new unit tests covering no-masks, first-match-wins, no-match, UUID collapse with /g flag, and per-page query-string strip. Suite grew from 484 → 489, all green.

Docs site: added an actual mask — `{ pattern: /^\/stats\/\?.*$/, replace: '/stats/' }` — that collapses /stats/ query-string variants (?site=…&window=…) into a single canonical path. Before the mask every filter combination produced a distinct row in Top Pages; after, one aggregated entry regardless of filter state.

Docs: new "URL masking" section in /docs/analytics/ with typical masks for user profiles, preview drafts, stats query, and UTM/ref stripping. Notes that masking is one-way and client-side. Pull-out callout points readers at head.html for the in-use example.

title: data-track: Analytics event attribute system for feedback loops
description: Not a component but an attribute system (data-track, data-track-event, data-track-value) that emits custom events for analytics integration. Enables the feedback loop from review/analytics back into planning. Closes the UUCD evolution phase gap.
close_reason: Superseded by data-vb-event analytics attribute system. The 'data-track' proposal was reframed to 'data-vb-event' to match the canonical vb:* namespace (see <meta name="vb:*"> taxonomy). Implementation: src/lib/analytics.js handleDocumentClick + extractDeclarativeProps. Docs: site/src/pages/docs/attributes/data-vb-event.html. Properties via data-vb-event-* dataset → camelCase props. Auto-wired by the analytics runtime on boot.

title: Create future web components wishlist for IA planning
close_reason: Created admin/future-wc.md — master wishlist of future web components organized by the 7 nav categories, plus upscale attributes section and IA validation checklist. Covers ~50 potential components across Interaction, Data & Charts, Media, Content Management, Social, Design Systems, and Planning.

title: CEM analyzer proof-of-concept
close_reason: PoC passed. CEM analyzer with 2 custom plugins extracts attrs, events, descriptions from existing JSDoc. Supplements compendium.json (cannot replace — no HTML variants). Config ready to expand to all components.

title: Upscale: sandbox → data-sandbox-report dev tool
description: Dev-mode only reporting of blocked sandbox actions to console. Helps debug silent sandbox failures. No production impact.
close_reason: Won't do. Dev-mode sandbox violation reporter — browsers don't expose a 'blocked by sandbox' signal, so any real implementation has to infer from side effects or inject a cooperating agent. Not worth the maintenance cost for a P4 debugging nicety. Out of scope.

title: Declarative observer wrapper elements (intersection, resize, mutation)
close_reason: Too low-level for the project's outcome-focused philosophy. Observers are already used as internal plumbing in dedicated init scripts (reveal, animate-image, etc). Generic wrappers wouldn't eliminate JS — they'd just move it to event handlers.

title: Flip card web component (flip-card-wc)
close_reason: Closed

title: Before/after comparison slider web component (comparison-wc)
close_reason: Implemented comparison-wc: logic.js, styles.css, demo, Astro doc page, all layout/nav/index updates

title: Inline sparkline web component (sparkline-wc)
close_reason: Not needed as a separate component. chart-wc's engine already auto-hides axes/guides at small sizes. Created vanilla-breeze-3qdj to add a data-size=sparkline inline mode to chart-wc instead — extends the existing component rather than duplicating it. Follows the project principle of fewer, more versatile components.

value: Hardcoded fonts.gstatic.com woff2 URLs in theme CSS rot when Google bumps font versions (e.g. /v15/, /v30/). Always use @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=...') instead — Google's CSS API resolves to current versioned URLs. Bundler hoists @import to top of bundle correctly.
